MB_FOTG_SPACE_CORUSCANT_EVACUATION,Coruscant is under attack by a massive Imperial fleet striking from the Deep Core. Many of the ships in this fleet were believed by New Republic Intelligence to have been destroyed years ago. Garm Bel Iblis and his hopelessly outnumbered defenders make a fighting retreat as the government evacuates...
MB_FOTG_SPACE_KATANA_BATTLE,The Katana Fleet was a task force of two hundred specially modified Dreadnaught Heavy Cruisers. The Galactic Republic commissioned this fleet just after the Stark Hyperspace War in an attempt to bolster the Republic Navy's ability to respond to pirate threats. To reduce crew requirements, the Katana Dreadnaughts were equipped with state-of-the-art automation technology, and the 199 ordinary ships of the fleet were slave-rigged to the flagship's computer systems. Unfortunately, the entire fleet was lost on its maiden voyage; a hive virus drove the crewmen insane and the admiral used the flagship's master controls to send the entire fleet on a blind hyperspace jump. They were not heard from again – until now...
MB_LAND_PLANET_BYSS_02,A team of New Republic commandos led by Lando Calrissian and Wedge Antilles has secretly stowed away in a shipment of Balmorran X-1 Viper war droids bound for Byss. Once they arrive, they will launch a sneak attack using the hijacked war machines. Their objective is as simple as it is audacious: destroy the Emperor's Cloning Facility and end his evil reign once and for all!
MB_MOD_BATTLEMAP_SPACE,Instantly build and battle with large, custom fleets of whatever size you like, for whichever factions you like.

STAT_CANTINA_COUNT,Smuggler Cantinas: %s
STAT_CREDITS,Total credits: %s

STAT_FORCE_PERCENT,Percent military strength: %s

STAT_INCOME_SMUGGLING,Smuggling income per cycle: %s
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_BASE,Base score: 10
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_CONTROL,Each planet you control: -2
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_DESCRIPTION_1,Your Infrastructure score is determined by the number of planets you control and how developed they are. It falls when you take control of new planets, and rises when you develop your planets by building ground structures, Primary Starbase levels, and space construction stations. 
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_DESCRIPTION_2,Different types of secondary space structure (construction, economic, & secondary defense) affect Infrastructure in different ways. Economic & secondary defense structures have no effect. Construction stations increase Infrastructure when present, but do not reduce it when missing (unlike Primary Starbase levels).
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_EFFECTS,Infrastructure Effects:
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_EMPTY_SLOTS,Empty ground structure slot or missing Primary Starbase level: -1
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_FACTORS,Infrastructure Factors:
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_FILLED_SLOTS,Ground structure or Primary Starbase level: +1
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_NAME,INFRASTRUCTURE
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_NEGATIVE_1,Infrastructure < 0 and > -30: +100%% price
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_NEGATIVE_2,Infrastructure ≤ -30: +100%% time, +200%% price
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_POSITIVE,Open slots < 5%%: -10%% time, -5%% price
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_SCORE,Infrastructure: %s
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_SHIPYARDS,Space construction station: +1
STAT_INFRASTRUCTURE_STRUCTURES,Corporation or Local Development Structure: +1 [Stacks with basic +1]
STAT_MINES_COUNT,Mining Facilities: %s
STAT_NET_CREDITS,Income after upkeep: %s 
STAT_PLANET_COUNT,Planets owned: %s
STAT_RESOURCE_MANAGER,Resource Information
STAT_RESOURCE_MANAGER_DESCRIPTION,This page tracks resources in your faction. These resources are gathered in different ways and have different effects within your faction. Each one explains its sources and effects.
STAT_SHIPCREWS_1,Influence - 5: +6
STAT_SHIPCREWS_10,Naval Academy: +75
STAT_SHIPCREWS_11,Cloning Facility: +75
STAT_SHIPCREWS_2,Influence - 6: +12
STAT_SHIPCREWS_3,Influence - 7: +18
STAT_SHIPCREWS_4,Influence - 8: +24
STAT_SHIPCREWS_5,Influence - 9: +30
STAT_SHIPCREWS_6,Influence - 10: +36
STAT_SHIPCREWS_7,Golan-I Space Colony: +15
STAT_SHIPCREWS_8,Golan-II Space Colony: +30
STAT_SHIPCREWS_9,Faction Capital: +75
STAT_SHIPCREWS_DESCRIPTION,Ship crews are required to build any type of ship and larger ships require more ship crews. Your overall ship crews are generated at the end of every cycle, sourced from planets above a certain influence level and structures such as Naval Academies, Faction Capital and Cloning Facilities.

TEXT_ABILITY_BOARD_DESCRIPTION,Ready the troops to seize an enemy ship. The closest applicable vessel will be marked for capture. After 5 seconds, the boarding action will either be successful or not, with a higher chance for success the weaker the ship is. Check ship descriptions to see if they can be boarded. After the battle, it will be refitted at one of your planets, indicated in the top left corner when you re-enter Galactic mode.

TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_DESCRIPTION_GAMEMODES,Extra game modes available below
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_DESCRIPTION_HISTORICAL,Historical maps focus on specific campaign, maneuvers or local wars, generally on a smaller map. Many will include special missions, events, heroes, and even roster changes to reflect the story and setting of the campaign. Broader systems from the progressive maps may be removed.
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_DESCRIPTION_INFINITY,Infinities maps represent situations that do not reflect Star Wars lore, such as challenge setups where most of the galaxy is unaligned, or alternate timelines.
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_DESCRIPTION_PROGRESSIVE,Era progressive maps represent most of the area, time period, and factions of the mod. As time passes, events, unit locks and unlocks, and researches will trigger that reflect the broad history of the Star Wars Galaxy.\n\n---\n\nThe starting era may be configured to change the planet layout of the map as well as the territory and forces of each faction. Territory is emphasized to keep playable factions on approximately equal footing with each other.
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_DESCRIPTION_REGIONAL,Regional maps are smaller versions of the progressive maps, focusing on a limited part of the Galaxy. Fewer factions may be present.
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_HEADER_GAMEMODES,==== GAME MODES =====================================
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_HEADER_HISTORICAL,=== HISTORIC CONFLICTS ==============================
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_HEADER_INFINITY,=== INFINITIES ======================================
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_HEADER_PROGRESSIVE,=== ERA-PROGRESSIVE =================================
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_HEADER_REGIONAL,=== REGIONAL CONFLICTS ==============================
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_DIVIDER_PROGRESSIVE,Era progressive maps represent most of the area, time period, and factions of the mod. As time passes, events, unit locks and unlocks, and researches will trigger that reflect the broad history of the Star Wars Galaxy.\n\n---\n\nThe starting era may be configured to change the planet layout of the map as well as the territory and forces of each faction. Territory is emphasized to keep playable factions on approximately equal footing with each other.\n\n---\n\nFull Galaxy maps include the Unknown Regions and have slightly more planets overall. Known Galaxy maps do not have the Unknown Regions but will have more planets within known space.
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_EVENT_ERAS_DISCLAIMER,Before completing Regime change objectives, keep in mind that Regime progression is not required to win nor is it always favourable. Consider the consequences carefully before proceeding.
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_EVENT_ERAS_DISCLAIMER2,Caution: Entering Dark Empire will lock you into your existing regime for the rest of the game.
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_EVENT_ERAS_EMPIRE,As the Galactic Empire, you will have several regime options you may choose over the course of the game in addition to those you may gain from integrating other Imperial factions. Each regime comes with a unique set of heroes and also includes minor changes to your roster.

TEXT_CAMPAIGN_EVENT_PROGRESS_PELLAEON_SSD2,- ALTERNATIVE UPGRADE: Upgrade Pellaeon's Chimaera (ISD-II) to Megador (Mandator-III).
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_EVENT_PROGRESS_PELLAEON_SSD3,- REGIME PROGRESSION UPGRADE: Upgrade Rogriss' Agonizer (ISD-I) to Dominion (Bellator).
TEXT_CAMPAIGN_EVENT_PROGRESS_PELLAEON_VOLUNTARY,If you complete the build option, Pellaeon will reluctantly take command of the Imperial forces with his unique hero roster, including Natasi Daala (Venator).

TEXT_COMPANY_HOLONET_RELAY_HQ,HoloNet Data Transfer HQ
TEXT_COMPANY_HOLONET_RELAY_HQ_DESCRIPTION,The HoloNet relays, which were satellite-based computer sorters and decoders, played a crucial role in the HoloNet network by facilitating the transmission of messages from one transceiver to another. These relay stations served as key components in the intergalactic communication system, allowing users to send and receive holographic transmissions across vast distances. In addition to their sorting and decoding functions, the relays also provided encryption and compression services to ensure the security and efficiency of the network. The widespread destruction of HoloNet relays was a common tactic employed by hostile forces during conflicts in order to disrupt communication lines and impede the flow of information.

TEXT_CUSOM_CAMPAIGN_GAME_TITLE,Scenario Setup
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_01,- Use a variety of prompts and dummy placements to decide what factions will be present and how much territory they should have, as well as what heroes to spawn and what units you can build. Once your choices are made, begin by conquering the planet you want to start at with the GC Starter (hero with a play button icon)
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_02,- First choose between the methods to pick enemies and their locations, arranged from simplest to most flexible.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_03,   Random Enemy Location lets you pick your planet and will procedurally generate the enemies you leave in the orbit of your starting planet several jumps away.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_04,   Pick Enemy Location will procedurally generate enemies around the planet where you leave their dummy, unless you sell the dummy or leave in above your starting planet. 
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_05,   Pick everywhere spawns a large set of dummies for each faction (including your own) with your starter. When you choose your starting planet, each planet with a faction dummy over it will be given to that facton. Planets that also have a capital dummy will spawn the faction capital, have extra starting forces, and add extra starting credits if the faction already has a capital.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_06,- Standard Roster will leave the starting roster for your faction and era unchanged. Roster Additions will spawn additional units from other eras, research, government mechanics, GC specific units, etc. that can be built. If these are positioned over your starting planet, they will be enabled. Custom Roster will also spawn the default roster and disable units if they are not over your starting planet. Set AI Tech will also spawn AI faction units that can be similarly enabled where the AI faction's dummy and an AI settings dummy share a location. Mapped Roster will use Custom Roster for AI, Random Maps will use Random Roster, Lootbox will enable all units.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_07,Mapped Roster allows you to define units from outside your faction to replace roster units when built. 2 copies of a base unit, a mapping dummy, and a stack of rough equivalent units will spawn on different planets. Leave only one equivalent unit on the same planet as a mapping dummy to select it, or move a random map dummy to select a random choice from all units with the mapping dummy when you build the base unit. Random Roster will enable a random selection of all custom roster units. Random Map will generate a mapped roster with each base unit mapped to a random choice. Lootbox Roster a will generate a mapped roster that allows any mappable unit to be rolled when a unit is built.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_08,- Default heroes will spawn a fixed set from standard libraries. Pick Heroes will spawn a large set of heroes of which up to 20 can be kept from if they are placed with the starter dummy. Random Heroes will automatically select a collection from the same hero options. For Pick and Random, Factional will limit you to heroes associated with appropriate faction, Universal will work with all heroes in the mod. Universal and random picks also allow setting AI heroes.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_09,- From the Ground Up gives the player a single planet and AI factions either 4 connected planets or a set of 3 and 2 connected planets. Small start will give each faction connected 6 planets and a small set of starting heroes. Full start will divide almost the whole map among the factions (e.g, appromimately one third of the galaxy for each of three factions) and spawn the same heroes as a progressive map at this era.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_10,   Any leftover planets will be given to Independent Forces. The player faction can use a modifier for a smaller or larger territory (e.g. half with one enemy to split the galaxy into one third and two third, single planet on small start to play FTGU with heroes). In pick everywhere, these choices only affect the hero spawns.
TEXT_CUSTOM_BODY_DESCRIPTION_11,- For advanced users, these scripts are not dependent on map layout. There is a utility in Data\XML\Conquests\Custom that can copy the layout of any map in EaWX to one of the Custom campaigns. The starting planet will be randomized if the defaults are not present. Data\CustomFactionName can set the name of your faction.

TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_INFLUENCE,Each planet has a loyalty level based on various factors. This influence level has several effects; including chance to get unrest stack (3 unrest stacks will cause revolt) and decreased credit output with lower influence, and other planet- or faction-specific bonuses when at certain levels. It also tends to factor into government mechanics, which can be found in the government tab. Influence updates at the end of each cycle, so the impact of events that change influence may not be immediately felt.
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_INFLUENCE_1,Influence 1:   -100% | -40
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_INFLUENCE_2,Influence 2:   -50%   | -50
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_INFLUENCE_3,Influence 3:   -15%

TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_REVOLT_INFLUENCE_4_5,Influence 4-5: No changes
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_REVOLT_INFLUENCE_6_10,Influence 6+: Removes 1 Unrest Stack per Cycle
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_REVOLTS,Having Low Influence level (1-3) on a planet can cause Unrest. High Influence (6+) removes Unrest. A planet will Revolt one cycle after getting a third Unrest Stack. All forces currently located on that planet will retreat to another planet. Control of that planet will flip to their preferred faction.

TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW,Galactic conquests in Empire at War Expanded have several story elements to them. Some are random missions, some are government related plot events, and some are other kinds of events or missions. Instead of tech levels, the game is broken into several eras which have different events within them. Eras progress automatically. These are going to be updated and added with each version. This guide gives a rundown of all events available to each faction. When starting after the default era, earlier events are usually completed or not based on canon timelines.
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_1,Era 1: 4 ABY - 6 ABY | 24 Cycles
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_2,Era 2: 6 ABY - 9 ABY | 36 Cycles
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_3,Era 3: 9 ABY - 10 ABY | 12 Cycles
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_4,Era 4: 10 ABY - 11 ABY | 12 Cycles
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_5,Era 5: 11 ABY - 11 ABY Month 6 | 6 Cycles
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_6,Era 6: 11 ABY Month 6 - 12 ABY | 6 Cycles
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_7,Era 7: 12+ ABY | ∞ Cycles
TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_STORYOVERVIEW_ERA_PROGRESSION,=== Era Progression ===================================

TEXT_DOCUMENTATION_BODY_TIERSANDTRAITS,Heroes in EaWX can have a command tier indicated in their description (XI being best, I being worst) which has a set of bonuses for units based on that tier. The overall bonus provided by a hero is summed up from their Command Tier, Traits, Focus and Specialization. Below is every tier and trait and its effect. If a faction has more than one hero the highest value in each category will be applied. Heroes do not apply their bonuses to themselves.

TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_AI_SETTINGS,AI Settings Dummy
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_AI_SETTINGS_DESCRIPTION,Place on the same planet as a faction dummy to enable setting tech and/or hero options by placing units or heroes on that planet. If none of a faction's dummies are with an AI Settings dummy, that faction will use standard tech/heroes. A faction dummy with an AI settings will cause that faction to be spawned in Random Enemy Location.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_AI_SETTINGS_DESCRIPTION2,In Pick Enemy Location with AI tech settings, duplicate faction dummies can be spawned. The one spawned with an AI settings dummy will set the faction's captial if they are placed in multiple valid locations.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_CAPITAL,Faction Capital
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_CAPITAL_DESCRIPTION,Places this over a planet with a factional dummy to spawn the faction's capital building and a larger garrison when the planet is given to them. One capital per faction will have heroes spawned in small start mode. Extra capitals given to a faction will increase their starting credits.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_FACTION_SPECIAL_CHISS_ALLY,Chiss Ally: Will not be chosen as a random faction if playing as the Chiss
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_FACTION_SPECIAL_COMMAND_STAFF,SIZE CHOICE: Command Staff slot count will be affected by FTGU/Small/Full choice
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_FACTION_SPECIAL_EOTH_ALLY,Empire of the Hand Ally: Will not be chosen as a random faction if playing as the Empire of the Hand
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_FACTION_SPECIAL_LEGITIMACY,SIZE CHOICE: Legitimacy integration threshold will be affected by layout selection. Only one faction may have a World Devastator chain: frigate Silencer-7 with be despawned if someone else has destroyer Silencer-7, Umak Leth will be despawned if someone else has Silencer-7.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_FACTION_SPECIAL_ZSINJ,SIZE CHOICE: Legitimacy integration threshold will be affected by layout selection. Will start with Rancor Base in Full starts, but an alternate ground capital in FTGU and Small.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_PLANET_DESTROY,Destroy Planet
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_PLANET_DESTROY_DESCRIPTION,Convert this planet into an asteroid field with 0 income at the end of setup. Has no effect on the GC Starter's location.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_PLANET_LOCK,Lock Planet
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_PLANET_LOCK_DESCRIPTION,Locks this planet at the end of setup so that no units may travel to it (units may still leave if assigned to a faction). Your starting planet cannot be locked. If all planets bordering your starting planet are locked and you do not give yourself other planets, full vision will be enabled. Has no effect on the GC Starter's location.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_FACTION,Random Faction
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_FACTION_DESCRIPTION,In Random Enemy Location mode, this faction will be spawned if this object is over the planet you conquer
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_FACTION_DESCRIPTION_INACTIVE,INACTIVE FACTION: This faction has no ai and is only used to set themed starting forces.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_FACTION_DESCRIPTION_PROTEUS,PROTEUS FACTION: This faction is used to represent a variety of smaller groups. Its options will be spawned on a planet during startup. If one is with the Starter Dummy or its Faction Dummy, the heroes and roster changes from that option will be chosen: otherwise, a random choice will be made. All units that can be disabled by choices will be present on Roster Additions.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_FACTION_DESCRIPTION2,In Pick Enemy Location mode, this faction will be given the planet this object is over. Sell this object or place it over the planet you conquer if you do not want this faction to be present
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_FACTION_DESCRIPTION3,In Pick Enemy Location mode, this faction will be given the planet this object is over. Factions will not be picked a random option if their dummy is sold.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_UNIT,Random Unit
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_RANDOM_UNIT_DESCRIPTION,Place this over a planet with a roster map dummy to save a list of all units from the stack sharing the planet. Each unit produced will roll independently from this list. Only units originally from the stack may be added.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_STARTER,GC Starter
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_STARTER_DESCRIPTION,Conquer a planet with this object to claim it as your starting planet and begin the GC.
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_STARTER_DESCRIPTION2,In Random Enemy Location mode, place faction objects over your planet before conquering it to choose them. They will be given territory a minimum distance from your choice
TEXT_DUMMY_CUSTOM_GC_STARTER_DESCRIPTION3,In Pick Enemy Location or Pick Everywhere mode, place faction objects over planets you want them to own before conquering your planet

TEXT_ENCYCLOPEDIA_BASE_SHIELD_00,Prevents energy weapon fire in a radius around the generator. Does not prevent solid objects, such as missiles or units, from passing through.  Only laser fire is stopped. Heavily dependent on power.

TEXT_ENCYCLOPEDIA_GROUNDWAR_DEFENSIVE_BUNKER,A stronger bunker with inbuilt weapons but provides less income (2 credits a second base)
TEXT_ENCYCLOPEDIA_GROUNDWAR_ECONOMIC_BUNKER,A weaker bunker which provides more income (4 credits per second base)
TEXT_ENCYCLOPEDIA_GROUNDWAR_REINFORCEMENT_POINT,A destroyable landing zone which can be built upon
TEXT_ENCYCLOPEDIA_GROUNDWAR_TUTORIAL,Instead of the homogeneous units of the GC, Units are often mixes of several squads and vehicles that arrive by a self-destructing spawner. This means that there is no population capacity in this game mod so battles can get large. In this mode the economy is derived from a steady flow from the command centre and then two bunkers buildable on the landing points. The economic bunker is weaker but gives more income. Landing zones can also be targeted and destroyed rather than having to push in and attempting to capture them whilst the enemy is landing on your head.

TEXT_ENCYCLOPEDIA_HYPERVELOCITY_00,A ground-to-space weapon that delivers widespread damage to a ship's hull by bypassing its shields. It is less effective against smaller, fast-moving ships. This weapon can be used to effectively retreat from a land battle without sustaining losses, even in the presence of an enemy fleet in orbit.
TEXT_ENCYCLOPEDIA_ION_CANNON_00,A ground-to-space weapon that is highly effective against shields, but does not result in permanent damage to the hull. If the shields are down, it temporarily disables the engines and weapons, though it is less effective against smaller ships. It can be used to safely retreat from a land battle without incurring losses, even if an enemy fleet is present in orbit.

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_DARK_EMPIRE_CHOOSE_INFO1,The faction that becomes the Dark Empire gains the following advantages.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_DARK_EMPIRE_CHOOSE_INFO2,• Unlock buildable: Eclipse Star Dreadnought, Sovereign Star Dreadnought, MTC Support, MTC Sensor, Hunter-Killer Probot, CompForce Assault Trooper, Dark Stormtrooper, Dark Side Elite, Chrysalide, XR-85.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_DARK_EMPIRE_CHOOSE_INFO3,• New heroes: Emperor Palpatine Reborn & Sedriss QL (Sith), Titus Klev (World Devastator Destroyer), Nahdonnis Praji (Secutor), Gilad Pellaeon (ISD-II), Xecr Nist (Assignable Fighter Hero), Umak Leth (Agent). 
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_DARK_EMPIRE_CHOOSE_INFO4,• Hero changes: If Maximilian Veers (AT-AT) serves the Dark Empire, he will be demoted to Captain with a Chariot LAV. If Grath (Stormtrooper) serves the Dark Empire, he will be converted to a Dark Stormtrooper.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_DARK_EMPIRE_CHOOSE_INFO5,• Unlock planet (if present): Byss. The Emperor's throneworld has its own fleet and infrastructure, including his capital and personal cloning facility.

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_DARK_EMPIRE_PALPATINE_SPEECH_NO_CONTACT,Palpatine: By the unlimited power of the Dark Side, I have returned from death! Those who remained true to me shall be rewarded. To the rest of you I offer only the choice to die now, or later.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_DARK_EMPIRE_PALPATINE_SPEECH_REFUSE,Palpatine: So be it... Traitor.

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_CRIMSON_EMPIRE_REWARD_HEADER,Crimson Empire [Carnor Jax's Regime]:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_CRIMSON_EMPIRE_REWARD_REWARD_1,Delta JV-7 Escort Shuttle [ Remains for Reunification ]
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_DARKEMPIRE_DESCRIPTION,In the dark center of the universe, the Omnipotent Battle Leader recovers his power and watchfully judges those who fight over the Empire's remains. His standards are high, and only Imperial factions that can cull the weak and cowardly pretenders will earn his favor. Once enough rivals are subdued and a faction has proven themselves in Legitimacy, the reborn Emperor will offer your submission to him in exchange for access to his most devout legions and cutting-edge weaponry developed at his citadel.

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_DARKEMPIRE_REWARD_HEADER,Dark Empire Rewards:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_DARKEMPIRE_UNAVAILABLE,Dark Empire is unavailable in this map, or has already formed.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_DESCRIPTION,With the Emperor's death the Empire has splintered into various Warlord states. They are competing amongst one another, as well as with the New Republic, to prove themselves as the Empire's legitimate successor. Different factors (explained below the levels) impact the legitimacy level of each faction, which provides different bonuses. When you increase your legitimacy there is a chance that the heroes of a minor Warlord group will become recruitable from your political options build bar. Heroes can only join one faction.

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_HEADER,Imperial Legitimacy:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EMPIRE_INTEGRATION_DESCRIPTION,When an Imperial faction has been weakened beyond hope of victory, its remaining planets and forces will defect to the most legitimate surviving Imperial faction. For this to occur, an Imperial faction must lose a planet while it has no Leaders and no Super Star Destroyers. If the faction holds %s or fewer planets after losing the planet, it will be integrated. (The number of required planets differs between Galactic Conquests.)

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_IMPERIAL_SELECT,Request Imperial Missions
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_1,The Empire of the Hand is a confederation of many
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_10,level pip to see the reward for each level.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_11,After reaching the second level of relationship with 
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_12,the Empire, you will unlock access to cloning tanks.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_13,This can be used to store the DNA of infantry heroes
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_14,you defeat in combat, or your own fallen heroes.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_15,The last five defeated heroes will appear in the available
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_16,tanks. To keep the sample available, activate the "store sample"
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_17,button, otherwise old samples will be replaced with new samples.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_18,You may begin cloning with the "start cloning" button beside the
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_19,name. Clones take 6 months to gestate and cost 6000 credits
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_2,inhabitants of the Unknown Regions, along with
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_20,A hero may only be cloned once.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_3,assets filtered in from the Empire to assist Grand
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_4,Admiral Thrawn in the pacification of the area.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_5,You can improve your relationship with each faction
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_EOTH_MENU_OVERVIEW_6,by completing missions from them. To request missions

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_GC_DISPLAY_INFO,This page holds all government information for your faction.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_GC_DISPLAY_TITLE,Government Overview
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_GENERAL_LIST,Active Ground Officers:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HAPAN,Hapes Consortium Government Information
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HAPAN_DESCRIPTION_1,The Hapan government must balance the competing interests of several noble Houses. Building anything on a planet with a House land title or House shipyard will give you +1 favour with that House. If the same House has both a land title and a shipyard on a planet, you will gain +2 favour with that House for each thing built there.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HAPAN_DESCRIPTION_2,Each House has a unique infantry type that can be recruited on planets with their land titles. Each company of House infantry that you build gives +1 favour with that House, on top of the favour from the land title (and shipyard, if applicable). You can spend House favour to recruit field and fleet commanders from specific Houses on the political options tab.

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS,Hutt Government Information:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_EMPIRE_2,When both the Scum & Villainy and Mobilization paths are completed, the Council will reform the Hutt Cartels into a renewed Hutt Empire and the mighty Bou'Halec Star Dreadnought will become available for construction. 
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_EMPIRE_HEADER,Rebuilding the Hutt Empire:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_EMPIRE_OVERVIEW,As galactic tensions rise, the Hutts see the need to rebuild their ancient military capabilities. New ship designs and heroes will unlock as mobilization points increase, with points increasing via missions and sending ships to Bootana Hutta. Each ship sent to Bootana Hutta will contribute mobilization points equal to its population value.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_MOBLIZATION_REWARDS,Mobilization Rewards:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_OVERVIEW,Using the political options menu on the galactic level, ships may be sent to Bootana Hutta. In tactical defensive battles, ships in Bootana Hutta may be called to assist from the Hutt shipyards.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_OVERVIEW_HEADER,Bootana Hutta:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_SCUM_AMOUNTS,Existing Smuggler Network:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_SCUM_HEADER,Scum and Villainy:
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_SCUM_OVERVIEW,The Hutts are the masters of the galaxy's criminal underworld. By deploying smugglers and completing missions, their control over the underworld will continue to grow, leading to new rewards. Smugglers can be hired on any planet where a Cantina is built.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_HUTTS_SCUM_REWARDS,Underworld Rewards:

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_CHIEFOFSTATE_OVERVIEW,The New Republic elects a new Chief of State every 10 cycles. Each candidate has their own bonuses or downsides. You can choose who to support from the story actions tab. Chief of State candidates will change depending on time period, controlled planets, and other factors.

TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_HERO_SYSTEM,The New Republic has a flexible pool of senior officers that may be appointed and dismissed at any time using build options at their location. Dismissing a hero will free a slot to appoint an available replacement. Assigning a hero as Supreme Commander will disallow them from other pools until dismissed. Losing a hero in battle will incur administrative costs before a replacement can be appointed.
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_HERO_SYSTEM_0,Active Command Staff at once (depending on GC):
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_HERO_SYSTEM_1,- Supreme Commander: 1
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_HERO_SYSTEM_2,- High Command: 1 (plus an additional slot when New Class Modernization Phase II is researched)
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_HERO_SYSTEM_3,- Fleet Officers: 2
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_HERO_SYSTEM_4,- Ground Officers: 3
TEXT_GOVERNMENT_NEW_REPUBLIC_HERO_SYSTEM_5,- Jedi: 1 (plus an additional slot when Jedi Praxeum is built)

TEXT_INTERVENTION_RAISEINFRASTRUCTURE_EMPIRE_SPEECH_02,Moff: Excellently done, Commander. With these amenities constructed for our people, you have assured their loyalty for years to come. I have managed to acquire some assistance to our cause, I place them under your able leadership.
TEXT_INTERVENTION_RAISEINFRASTRUCTURE_EMPIRE_SPEECH_03,Moff: It is a shame to see you lack vision. Tarkin's fate has shown we cannot rely on fear alone if the foundations of the Empire are brittle.
TEXT_INTERVENTION_RAISEINFRASTRUCTURE_EOTH_CHISS_SPEECH_01,Chiss Ascendancy Syndic: Fellow Syndics have expressed concern at the lack of proper infrastructure on several worlds. We cannot secure our borders in this state, improve conditions immediately.
TEXT_INTERVENTION_RAISEINFRASTRUCTURE_EOTH_CHISS_SPEECH_02,Chiss Ascendancy Syndic: Satisfactory. With the proper support structures in place we can free up several units for frontline use.
TEXT_INTERVENTION_RAISEINFRASTRUCTURE_EOTH_CHISS_SPEECH_03,Chiss Ascendancy Syndic: Foolishness commander, foolishness. Without a foundation, the power of a regime is hollow at best.

TEXT_INTERVENTION_RAISEINFRASTRUCTURE_EOTH_GARWIAN_SPEECH_03,Garwian Unity Liaison: You have done nothing to prove our doubters wrong. Most vexing if you wish us to continue our arrangements.

TEXT_LORE_IMPERIAL_MUTINY,Lore Holocron: Imperial Mutiny
TEXT_LORE_IMPERIAL_MUTINY_DESCRIPTION,In the years leading up to Shadow Hand, Imperial forces from all over the galaxy began to disappear without a trace, having been called to Palpatine's side as he was preparing his assault from the deep core. While the initial attacks by these forces in 10 ABY were successful, they soon began infighting, a process which Palpatine believed would weed out the weak. Among the casualties from what would be called the Imperial Mutiny were multiple of the Super Star Destroyers which had disappeared to and set out from the Deep Core- Whelm (Executor-class) and Panthac (Mandator-III-class) which destroyed each other at Alsakan, Javelin (Vengeance-class) which rammed a torpedo sphere defending Chasin, and the Aculeus (Mandator III-class), which was destroyed by TIE Droid squadrons over Drearia.
TEXT_LORE_LIINADE_CONQUEST,Conquest of Liinade III
TEXT_LORE_LIINADE_CONQUEST_DESCRIPTION,The battle at Liinade III became a focus of propaganda for both sides after plans for a Death Star-like superweapon were found in its aftermath. These plans rapidly replaced the murder of Sate Pestage as the reason to bring Delak Krennel to justice. For his part, the Prince-Admiral claimed that the plans were planted and that he would never develop such a horrible device. Krennel had been talking with the survivors of Alderaan regarding the establishment of a haven for them, and he sorrowfully announced to the galaxy that he had had Liinade III in mind as its location. In reality, the plans were a counterfeit of the real Ysanne Isard, who used the subsequent investigation to her advantage.
TEXT_LORE_REQUIEM_SQUADRON,Requiem Squadron
TEXT_LORE_REQUIEM_SQUADRON_DESCRIPTION,In the ambush at Distna, a moon of Corvis Minor V, Rogue Squadron was nearly destroyed by a wing of TIE fighters. But two squadrons of TIE Defenders intervened and took the surviving Rogues to the base of a secretive Imperial organization. This group sent the Rogues to Ciutric in a TIE Defender squadron, posing as Requiem Squadron led by one of Wedge Antilles' old aliases, Antar Roat. A message dispatched to the New Republic High Command detailed the timetable of their sabotage mission.
TEXT_LORE_TSOSS_CONFERENCE,Lore Holocron: Tsoss Conference
TEXT_LORE_TSOSS_CONFERENCE_DESCRIPTION_1,Upon her arrival in the Deep Core, Admiral Daala was horrified to discover that the Imperial leaders there were fighting each other instead of the New Republic. She found an ally in Vice-Admiral Pellaeon, who had fallen in with the warlord Treuten Teradoc after the collapse of the Dark Empire.
TEXT_LORE_TSOSS_CONFERENCE_DESCRIPTION_2,With Pellaeon's assistance, Daala called a meeting of thirteen Imperial leaders in the uninhabited Tsoss system. The most prominent of these leaders were Blitzer Harrsk, Treuten Teradoc, Sander Delvardus, and Malfkla Yzu; the names of the rest are lost to history.
TEXT_LORE_TSOSS_CONFERENCE_DESCRIPTION_3,The warlords were initially unwilling to discuss anything, but Daala was an unconventional negotiator - she sealed the conference room's only exit with a blast door on a three-hour automatic timer. As the timer ticked down, the discussion devolved from acrimony to outright violence; in the third hour, Harrsk leapt across the table and fell upon Treuten Teradoc in an attempt to strangle him to death.
TEXT_LORE_TSOSS_CONFERENCE_DESCRIPTION_4,With this outburst, Daala determined it was time to employ her other unconventional diplomatic tool. Pellaeon and Daala had secretly brought their own gas masks into the conference room, but the gathered warlords weren't so well prepared. When Daala released the poison gas, everyone in the room except Daala and Pellaeon were killed.
TEXT_LORE_TSOSS_CONFERENCE_DESCRIPTION_5,The deceased warlords' surviving forces didn't mourn their loss and quickly fell into line behind Daala and Pellaeon, forming the "United Warlord Fleet."

The Sith military of Revan took direct control of Czerka 431 to assess its work's applicability to the war effort. Much later, the site was used as secret military prison with a fearsome reputation.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AAR_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AAR_HISTORY,Aar was home of the reptilian Aar'aa, skinchangers that can slowly alter their natural grayish-tan coloration to blend in with their surroundings. The frilled predators frequently work with the Hutts as surreptitious enforcers that can become nearly invisible in dark areas.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AAR_POPULATION,Native: Aar'aa
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AARGAU_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AARGAU_HISTORY,Aargau was a member of the Galactic Republic from its discovery until the end of that galactic power. After the fall of the Galactic Empire, it was considered a New Republic stronghold. The region of space occupied by the planet would later fall under the hegemony of first the Galactic Federation of Free Alliances, and then subsequently Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire. Despite this, Aargau took a neutral approach to politics, which meant that warring factions were mutually welcome to conduct business on the planet. The planetary government imposed only three laws on citizens and visitors, called the Three Statutes of Aargau. These laws focused on the export of Aargau's natural resources, the absolute ban on weapons for visitors—and, conversely, the requirement to bear arms for Aargauuns—as well as maintaining the integrity of the Bank of Aargau. Breaking any of these rules was punishable by immediate execution.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AARGAU_POPULATION,Demonym: Aargauun\nImmigrated: Human | Muun | Other\n 4.8 Billion
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AARGONAR_FAUNA,Gouka dragon | Sarlacc
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AARGONAR_HISTORY,A dusty, desert planet located within the Mid Rim, Aargonar was ordinarily an insignificant planet, but did play host to a catastrophic loss for the Republic during the Clone Wars.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AARGONAR_POPULATION,Demonym: Aargonari\nImmigrated: Yuzzem
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AB343_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AB343_HISTORY,AB-343 was one of Czerka Corporation's laboratories situated in remote locations, in this case what was once a mineral rich asteroid field. The lab was overrun by Republic forces early in the Jedi Civil War and the mines were abandoned after they dried up.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AB343_POPULATION,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABELOTH_WORLD_FAUNA,Bird-moth | Drendek | Tree turtle | Wyvarl
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABELOTH_WORLD_HISTORY,Located within the unstable cluster of black holes known as The Maw is an unnamed planet with powerful connections to the Force. The planet was once home to the Ones, physical manifestations of the Force referred to as the Son, the Daughter and the Father who represented in turn the dark side, light side and balance of the Force. A fourth being, a mortal woman who served the Ones and later became the Mother, kept the peace between Son and Daughter. In an attempt to gain immortality the Mother drank from the Font of Power and bathed in the Pool of Knowledge, but the experiences from those Force Nexuses corrupted her and she became a powerful, twisted entity known as Abeloth, Bringer of Chaos. Seeing this, the Father, Son and Daughter instructed the Killiks to build Centerpoint Station and other facilities to imprison Abeloth on the planet for eternity. 
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABELOTH_WORLD_POPULATION,Native: Celestial
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABHEAN_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABHEAN_HISTORY,Located within the Mid Rim on the Perlemian Trade Route, Abhean was home to a series of large orbital shipyards.  In 500 BBY those shipyards were contracted to build the Chu'unthor training vessel for the Jedi Order.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABREGADO_RAE_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABREGADO_RAE_HISTORY,Abregado Rae is a manufacturing and trade-oriented planet located in the Abregado system of the Core Worlds. Abregado Rae is a temperate planet with rolling hills, lakes and rivers. The planet's spaceport seems safe, at least to the untrained eye. Beneath the spit-shine and polish lies the heart of a smuggler’s paradise, a spaceport where the galaxy's uncounted species mingle briefly, have wild and sometimes fatal flings before heading off again for parts unknown.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABREGADO_RAE_POPULATION,Native: Gados | Moocher\nImmigrated: Devaronian | Human | Ishi Tib | Other\n40 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABRIDON_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ABRIDON_HISTORY,Abridon was a pleasant, mountainous planet with special significance to the career of Brenn Tantor. Its capital housed the Abridon Government Center, while Sayan was the center of its shipbuilding industry. It was primarily notable to the greater galaxy as the site of Luke Skywalker's capture by the Galactic Empire. It was located on the Northern Corridor's Lipsec Run.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ADUBA_FAUNA,High-hound | Bantha | Sithspawn | Maze-fly
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ADUBA_HISTORY,Aduba-3 was settled as a religious agricultural colony growing maze-stalk, but an overeager chromium rush driven by dishonest speculators created a world the law forgot: a hard and violent place full of crime and vice, beset by Cloud-riders. Although it was once rare to find on star charts, the TriNebula Entertainment sensational holo-doc covering the adventure of the "Star-hoppers of Aduba-3" has made it a household name, even if scholars question how much truth lies behind the tale.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ADUMAR_FAUNA,Farumme | Turumme
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ADUMAR_HISTORY,A temperate world on the coreward edge of Wild Space, Adumar was settled approximately 10 000 years BBY. The settlers were Separatists banished by the Old Republic, leaving the planet isolated from galactic civilisation. Over the centuries, the planet became an industrial powerhouse, fueled by internal warfare. When it re-emerged on the galactic stage after the Battle of Endor, this industrial skill made it attractive to both the Empire and New Republic.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ADUMAR_POPULATION,Demonym: Adumari\nImmigrated: Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AEFAO_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AEFAO_HISTORY,A remote planet located within the Corosi sector, Aefao was the homeworld of the Aefans, a species of small orange-skinned humanoids. It remained within the boundaries of Sith territory for thousands of years until the fall of the Brotherhood of Darkness in the New Sith Wars, after which point it became part of the Republic. During the Clone Wars, Aefao seceded and joined with the Confederacy of Independent Systems. During the Third Battle of Aefao, the Separatist commander Hoom Garaf was executed by his tactical droid following a disagreement, leading to chaos in the Separatist rear guard and a victory for the Republic. After the war it became part of the Galactic Empire, and it remained part of Imperial space for over a century.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AEFAO_POPULATION,Native: Aefan
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AETEN_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AETEN_HISTORY,Aeten II is known for its deposits of rare stygium crystals, which are used in the construction of cloaking devices. Because stygium crystals are extremely valuable, a number of unscrupulous prospectors descended upon Aeten II, where they competed for the rare crystals with deadly abandon. By the rise of the Empire, Aeten II's cache of crystals had been all but depleted, making the manufacture of new stygium-based cloaking devices a near impossibility.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AETEN_POPULATION,Native: None\nImmigrated: Human | Sullustan | Twi'lek
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AGAMAR_FAUNA,Agamarian beast | Mugruebe
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AGAMAR_HISTORY,A backwater agri-world in the Outer Rim, Agamar was neglected by both the Old Republic and Empire, until anti-Imperial agitation drew the ire of the Imperial Navy and resulted in the destruction of the town of Tondatha.  Although shaken by the massacre the local Resistance continued to work with the Rebel Alliance and Agamar later hosted a refugee camp for Alderaanians who lost their homeworld to the first Death Star.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AGAMAR_POPULATION,Demonym: Agamarian\nImmigrated: Human

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AHCH_TO_FAUNA,Porg | Gull | Twinfin hyacander | Other
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AHCH_TO_HISTORY,Ahch-To, known by many names in ancient legends, was a planet mostly covered in water, with numerous rocky islands blanketed with green trees located in the Unknown Regions. It was the birthplace of the Jedi Order and held the ancient Jedi texts. Due to the planet's great distance relative to its twin suns, Ahch-To supported a temperate, damp, but volatile climate that frequently caused salt spray, cold winds and violent storms, unlike the desert world of Tatooine.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AHCH_TO_POPULATION,Demonym: Ahch-Tonian\nNative: Lanai\nImmigrated: Human

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AILON_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AILON_HISTORY,Located in the Inner Rim area of The Slice, the planet Ailon is famous for its millennia-long military history with the annual Ailon Military Ceremonies broadcast galaxy-wide. The Ailon Nova Guard, founded in 13 000 BBY, has a reputation for martial prowess equalled only by the Imperial Royal Guard and the Mandalorians. Members of the Guard are drilled from birth in military history and tactics, with a near-religious devotion to martial tradition and the highest of standards of physical toughness and professionalism. Combat is so central to Ailon culture that those rejected or expelled from military service in the Guard exile themselves from the home world in disgrace.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AILON_INFLUENCE_REWARD,•Unlocks Ailon Nova Guard at 7+ influence
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AILON_POPULATION,Native: Ailon\n500 Million - 1 Billion
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AJAN_KLOSS_FAUNA,Zymod | Various birds
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AJAN_KLOSS_HISTORY,Although Ajan Kloss was a moon, it had two moons. Its gas giant shielded it from harmful radiation.  Jedi Master Luke Skywalker compared Ajan Kloss with the planet Dagobah, where he trained years before with Yoda. He noted that the similarities between Dagobah and Ajan Kloss were that they were wet, warm, green and overflowing with life, but the main difference was that Ajan Kloss didn't smell bad. After the Battle of Crait, the Resistance established a base in the moon's mountains.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AJAN_KLOSS_POPULATION,Native: Kloss (Possibly extinct)\nImmigrated: Human | Cyclorrian | Mythrol | Other
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AKURIA_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AKURIA_HISTORY,Early in the Galactic Civil War, before the destruction of the first Death Star, the planet was the site of an Imperial base. The Imperials there were defeated by a Rebel force led by Colonel Odan. The Alliance originally considered basing themselves there but chose Yavin 4 instead. The planet was also the homeworld of the native Snow Demons, also known as Akurians, which the Empire hunted without realizing that they were particularly intelligent. After liberating the planet from the Empire, the Alliance allowed these creatures to reclaim their frigid world.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_AKURIA_POPULATION,Demonym: Akurians\nNative: Akurians
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALAGON_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALAGON_HISTORY,The Xeran Mining Corporation ran a mining operation in the system and initially employed sentient workers, who were ultimately fired and replaced with HND-9 mining droids. The former miners created the Worker's Militia and staged an uprising against the corporation, attacking starships that attempted to leave the system. Eventually, the New Republic intervened and ended the conflict.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALAGON_POPULATION,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALARIS_FAUNA,Gnasp | Gundark | Maru | Mynock | Nerf
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALARIS_HISTORY,First moon of Alaris, the sixth planet in the Kashyyyk system, Alaris Prime is so small against its gas giant parent that it has been overlooked and untouched by civilisation for eons. After its rediscovery in the final century of the Old Republic Alaris Prime was the subject of several legal and physical clashes between Wookiees from Kashyyyk and the Trade Federation, both of whom claimed ownership and colonisation rights to the moon. Alaris Prime teems with a multitude of flora and fauna and is abundant in natural resources, including rich seams of valuable nova crystals that made it such a desirable target for the Trade Federation. The forests are thick with wroshyr trees thought to be native to nearby Kashyyyk; no accepted scientific theory has explained how the trees could have migrated across space between the two.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALARIS_INFLUENCE_REWARD,• Unlocks Raddaugh Gnasp Fluttercrafts at 7+ influence
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALARIS_POPULATION,Immigrated: Wookiee | Trandoshan
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALASHAN_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALASHAN_HISTORY,Located in Wild Space on the fringes of the Outer Rim, the planet known as Alashan was once home to a highly advanced civilisation believed to date from before the founding of the Old Republic. Long since lost to the mists of time, the only record or evidence of the unidentified inhabitants exists in the form of the enormous underground city they left behind. A technological marvel, still operational and fully powered millennia later, the abandoned city's automated control center is capable of generating an immense creature to defend the city from intruders.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALASHAN_POPULATION,Native: Unidentified civilisation (extinct)
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERAAN_FAUNA,Thranta | Nerf | Other
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERAAN_GRAVEYARD_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERAAN_GRAVEYARD_HISTORY,Once a peaceful, utopian Core World that served as the spiritual heart of the galaxy. Alderaan boasted vast skies and plains that supported more than 8 000 subspecies of grass, numerous wildflowers and a wealth of unique species. Now an asteroid field, the Graveyard of Alderaan is all that remains of the planet blown up by the Empire with the first Death Star. It is called the Graveyard by spacers and free-traders that spin tales of mysterious Jedi artifacts and ghost ships amidst the ruins. The survivors of Alderaan, who were offworld at the time of its destruction, quickly developed rituals referred to as the Returning where they would return periodically in remembrance for their departed relatives and friends.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERAAN_GRAVEYARD_POPULATION,Demonym: Alderaani / Alderaanian\nNative: Killik (extinct)\nImmigrated: Human (extinct)
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERAAN_GRAVEYARD_REVOLT_OWNER,New Republic | Independent Forces
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERAAN_HISTORY,A peaceful, utopian Core World that serves as the spiritual heart of the galaxy. Alderaan boasts vast skies and plains that support more than 8 000 subspecies of grass, numerous wildflowers and a wealth of unique species. The people of Alderaan love the land and work with it, rather than destroy it. Their society is immersed in high culture and education, and is founded on ecological principles that protect the planet's natural beauty. The horrors of the Clone Wars were taken to heart and the people of Alderaan adopted pacifism, banning all weapons from the planet’s surface.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERAAN_POPULATION,Demonym: Alderaani / Alderaanian\nNative: Killik\nImmigrated: Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERATH_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERATH_HISTORY,Situated on the junction of the Celanon Spur and a minor trade route, Alderath is best known for its horticultural society that deals in a range of specialty plants, from the ultrarare Millenium blossoms sourced from the Hapes Consortium that bloom once a century, to flowers from harsh volcanic worlds with constant solar flares and hurricanes, to semisentient mobile blooms with poisonous spores or acid.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALDERATH_POPULATION,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALEEN_FAUNA,Bogwing | Can-cell | Sagcatcher | Scatalpen
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALEEN_HISTORY,A rocky, temperate world located within the Mid Rim, Aleen is the original homeworld of the Aleena, a reptlian species known to be friendly and boisterous, and the Kindalo, a subterrean species based around anarchic consensus. Aleena had a long history of expansion and technological advancement, before a cataclysmic war with the Kindalo in the early days of the Republic forced the two peoples into a compact of rigid separation, which took form in the Great Seal that rested in the ruins of a Jedi chapter house. From then on the Aleena embraced a pastoral, low-tech lifestyle out of their spiritual beliefs, while maintaining a proficiency with technology to the surprise of many travelers. Both Aleena and Kindalo harvest mineral-vegetable arbozoic trees, whose crystals are considered sacred to both of their faiths.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALEEN_POPULATION,Native: Aleena | Kindalo\n18 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALLANTEEN_FAUNA,Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALLANTEEN_HISTORY,Allanteen VI was situated in the Tynna sector of the Expansion Region and located at the intersection of the Corellian Run and the Shipwrights' Trace, in the 77 Sectors. A major naval hub since the restoration of the Republic Navy circa 15,000 BBY, Allanteen IV's shipyards have been building Republic fleets throughout its history, until it joined the Empire and proved to be a vital supplier of ships for the new regime as well.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALLANTEEN_POPULATION,Immigrated; Various\n100 to 500 Billion

The planet possessed chromite resources but the mining operations on Allyuen were barely profitable.

Ignored by outsiders, Almania sat in the shadow of its wealthiest moon, Pydyr. The planet was often raided for slaves by various pirate and slaver gangs.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALPHERIDIES_FAUNA,Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALPHERIDIES_HISTORY,Alpheridies was located at the edge of a giant molecular cloud called the Veil. After the Subterra Period, the world was discovered by the Miraluka who migrated to Alpheridies when their world of origin entered into a phase of geophysical and geochemical instability during which the atmosphere began to vent into space.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALPHERIDIES_POPULATION,Native: Alpheridian\nImmigrated: Miraluka\n1 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALSAKAN_FAUNA,Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALSAKAN_HISTORY,Alsakan was an ecumenopolis world in the Alsakan system in the Core Worlds. A Core Founder on the Perlemian Trade Route, Alsakan was an ancient, wealthy world that had been a major political player for centuries, and was responsible for the seventeen Alsakan Conflicts that had been fought with Coruscant over the nature and shape of the early Galactic Republic.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALSAKAN_POPULATION,Demonym: Alsakanese / Alsakani\nImmigrated: Killiks (extinct) | Rakata (extinct) | Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALTHIR_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALTHIR_HISTORY,Althir III was a remote, independent planet in the Althir system that was located outside of the borders of the Galactic Republic. It had a system of rocky planetary rings. Discovered by the Mandalorians early in the Old Sith Wars, it was the site of two major battles. As Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders invaded, the warriors of Althir fought valiantly against the barbarous conquerors but were eventually cowed under the forces of Canderous Ordo.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALTHIR_POPULATION,Native: Althiri

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALTHIR_TUTORIAL_HISTORY,Althir III was a remote, independent planet in the Althir system that was located outside of the borders of the Galactic Republic. It had a system of rocky planetary rings. Discovered by the Mandalorians early in the Old Sith Wars, it was the site of two major battles. As Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders invaded, the warriors of Althir fought valiantly against the barbarous conquerors but were eventually cowed under the forces of Canderous Ordo.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALTIRIA_ANARRIS_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALTIRIA_ANARRIS_HISTORY,The planet known as either Altiria or Anarris (depending on whom you ask) was a fertile, temperate world covered with sparse forests and roiling plains. The eastern continent was home to the democratic and scientific society of blue-skinned Altiri, while the militarized dictatorship of the horned and brown-furred Anarrians hailed from the west. Ever since the watercraft of the two crossed paths there have been finely balanced tensions between them, from skirmishes to decade long wars to space races and rival moon bases. But despite their mistrust of each other, both sides welcomed offworlder merchants and ambassadors, and trade languages were widely spoken on the planet.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ALTORA_HISTORY,The grasslands of Altora make for excellent ranching land, but the remote world has only developed a fragile economy. The primary barrier is the altagak, a two meter reptile described as "death on four legs". Altagaks can stalk prey for hours before charging at eighty kilometers per hour to bury their tusks deep into an unfortunate victim. Despite a bounty program, altagaks continue to thrive and have even managed to spread offworld. The Rebel Strike Force Altagak served with distinction at Mentanar Vosk.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ANDO_HISTORY,The three species of Aqualish (Aquala, Quara, and Ualaq) are each notoriously belligerent. Prior to the Clone Wars, Ando was one of the first planets to secede from the Republic, during what would be known as the Separatist Crisis. In the time of the Empire, the Aqualish faced heavy subjugation as retribution. Ando would go on to shuffle between various factions following the Battle of Endor, but would eventually settle upon independence following the Yuuzhan Vong War.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ANOAT_HISTORY,Once a sprawling city-world, Anoat served as the capital of the entire Anoat sector. However, years before the Battle of Yavin, the entire planet was gassed by the Galactic Empire, and the atmosphere became toxic and poisonous, forcing much of the population to flee from the polluted world. Some people, however, did not leave the planet. Those who did not die were driven insane by the toxins, evolving over time into savage and feral creatures referred to as "Lurkers." During the Galactic Civil War, the planet was under the control of the Galactic Empire and contained the sector's largest concentration of Imperial forces.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ARGAZDA_HISTORY,Argazda was a forested world colonized by humans from the Core in the early history of the Republic. Contact was lost and it diverged into a distinct population where Vianism was still practiced. By 4200 BBY, the Republic had expanded to the Rim, and Argazda petitioned to join for protection from Ereesi marauders and slavers. Despite this, the Argazdan puritans held the Republic in contempt and seceded once they had built up a fleet and the Republic was distracted by the Mandalorians. The resulting Kanz Disorders ravaged the sector for centuries before the Jedi ended the Argazdan Redoubt.

The population is sparse; perhaps more people would live here, but the mining companies and government restrict immigration to employees. Argovia is an important crossroads for the travel in the Endocray region. A well known landmark in the settlement of Argovas City is Tanza's: a three stroy transparisteel building filled with flashing lights and gaudy chrome statues.

During the Clone Wars the planet sided with the Republic, but a blockade enacted by the Separatists forced its surrender. The Republic responded with an invasion which led to the devastation of most Argul's major cities. leading to a general evacuation of these cities was ordered by Supreme Chancellor Palpatine. Reinforcements brought in by Master Yoda beat back the Separatists and allowed the Argullians to evacuate.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ARUZA_HISTORY,Aruza is a planet orbited by five moons of pale shades of tan, silver, and green. The native Aruzans were small people with light blue skin and very dark blue hair. Aruza was a peaceful and even-tempered society that abhorred violence, to the point that by 3 ABY the planet had not experienced a murder in one hundred of their years. During the Galactic Civil War, Aruza was controlled by the COMPNOR officer, General Sinick Kritkeen, who wanted the inhabitants of Aruza stripped of their humanity and turned into servants of Imperial doctrine. The Aruzans hired the bounty hunter Dengar to assassinate Kritkeen, yet when he succeeded a more ruthless general replaced him. Dengar with the Rebel Alliance's assistance would then evacuate the Aruzans using a captured Imperial prison barge

For millennia, politicians and generals have studied Atrisian writings on diplomacy, governance, and military strategy, most of them dating back to the years before the Atrisian branch of humanity ever left their homeworld. The Balmorrans longed for independence from the Empire, and were therefore friendly towards the Rebel Alliance.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BANDOMEER_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BANDOMEER_HISTORY,Bandomeer was a mining world dominated by the Offworld Mining Corporation until 44 BBY. When Obi-Wan Kenobi and Qui-Gon Jinn arrived on the world, they narrowly saved it from destruction at the hands of Xanatos. In 19 BBY the Ionite miners of Bandomeer refused to honor their contracts with the newly-formed Galactic Empire. In order to teach them to treat the new government with respect, Emperor Palpatine ordered his apprentice, Darth Vader, to deal with the local resistance. The Imperial Army quickly overwhelmed the lightly armed miners and established control over the planet.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BANNISTAR_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BANNISTAR_HISTORY,The Bannistar Station system was named after a major Imperial refueling station located in this part of the Hevvrol sector. The station's refineries were placed on the surface of an unnamed planet within the system with the rest of the station infrastructure located in orbit high above. In 0 ABY the station was under the command of commander Arno D'Vox and running at capacity, servicing both Imperial and civilian ships. Its location and importance in the Mid Rim allowed the Empire to expand further out to remote Imperial stations.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BANNISTAR_POPULATION,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BARAB_FAUNA,Durgolosk | hka’ka | rangi | shenbit bonecrusher | skotcarp | vsst
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BARAB_HISTORY,The planet of Barab I is one of extremes. As it lies only a short distance from a dim red dwarf star, it is dark and murky, but has a day period of 60 standard hours when the planet is blasted by a lethal mix of gamma, infrared, and ultraviolet rays, forcing all life underground to survive. Because all the standing water is evaporated under the intense heat of the day, sudden, torrential downpours can occur at night, forcing the inhabitants to hunt quickly, or be washed away. The native Barabel are superb hunters – highly aggressive and very territorial – often finding work as mercenaries and soldiers. The Barabel have a profound respect for the Jedi, as a Jedi mission engaged both sides in an ongoing civil war and ended an armed conflict that had raged for thousands of years. Although the Barabel subsequently developed a small fruits and ores export market to buy weapons and tools, they remain separate from the galactic community at large.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BARAB_POPULATION,Native: Barabel\n1.4 Billion
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BARDRAM_SCOFT_FAUNA,Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BARDRAM_SCOFT_HISTORY,Bardram Scoft was a planet located in the Unknown Regions. It was home to the Scofti, whose government changed frequently, often with a violent transition. On one occasion where the previous Scofti Prefect was assassinated, various outside groups, including the Chiss Ascendancy, sent emissaries to Bardram Scoft. While at Bardram Scoft, the Chiss Junior Commander Mitth'raw'nuru "Thrawn" learned that the Lioaoin Regime was supporting pirates that had recently been attacking at the edges of the Ascendancy.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BARDRAM_SCOFT_POPULATION,Native: Scofti

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BARISON_HISTORY,The water world of Barison supplied fish, seafood, and kelp to the worlds of the Back Reach within the larger Gordian. Its farmers live in floating towns on giant wooden platforms connected together to form lagoons. Many Barisoni have renounced technology for religious reasons; some have abandoned farming to make their living as pirates.

During their war with the Mandalorians around the year 4017 BBY, the Basiliskans chemically poisoned their planet in the last effort to defeat their conquerors. Despite receiving Jedi and Republic reinforcements under Sidrona Diath at the Battle of Basilisk, the Mandalorians conquered the world and enslaved the remaining Basiliskans.

Over time the world lost its original identity, and became permanently associated with its new codename.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BATUU_HISTORY,Batuu was a temperate planet that was located on the edge of the galaxy's Outer Rim Territories, within the Trilon sector's Batuu system. The planet orbited three suns—Ubaat I, Ubaat II, and Ubaat III. The world was the location of Black Spire Outpost, a haven for those looking to avoid attracting too much attention to themselves. Batuu's market stalls were known to have all sorts of items from all parts of the galaxy.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BEGEREN_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BEGEREN_HISTORY,The planet Begeren was located in the Begeren system of the Sith Worlds region—technically part of the Esstran sector—of the Outer Rim Territories. It was one of the end points of the Kamat Aegit hyperlane that ran across the Sith Worlds region to connect with Khar Delba. At least part of the planet was covered in rocky desert, dotted with large, light blue constellations of gemstones. Tall cliffs loomed over the landscape, some of which had been sculpted into enormous statues of notable Sith. Under the Galactic Republic, some of these monuments were destroyed to make room for a gemstone quarry.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BELGAROTH_FAUNA,Droidbreaker
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BELGAROTH_HISTORY,A small, rocky planet on the edge of the Core Worlds, Belgaroth was covered largely in inhospitable mountains, sparse vegetation, and a pair of splotchy, yellow seas supported by frequent rainfall. In the centuries prior to the Clone Wars, Belgaroth had only a single, insignificant city. With the rise of the Empire, however, the wayward planet was chosen by the Naval Department as a convenient location to perform weapon tests on. While the Empire's presence was still minimal most of the time, the occasional visit from Star Destroyers and Imperial R&D groups brought a temporary economic vigor to the planet. Despite this new presence, the listless population of Belgaroth remained largely impassive. When the planet was finally integrated into the New Republic, the apathetic locals remained just as indifferent as they had been under the Empire.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BELGAROTH_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human | Rodian | Twi'lek | Sullustan

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BELSAVIS_FAUNA,Glowbug | Hoverbird | Kintan crusher | Mookla | Phosphor bug | Watch-critter
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BELSAVIS_HISTORY,An ice-covered world in the Outer Rim, Belsavis has a very long history in galactic affairs. In the distant past the planet was used by the Rakata as a prison colony to hold the Esh-kha, the Old Republic later did the same with Sith Lords during the Great Galactic War. Millennia later the planet was resettled by Jedi Master Plett who used his home and laboratory as a safe house to hide a number of Jedi younglings during Palpatine's Jedi Purge. The settlement by then known as Plett's Well was taken over by the Ithorian Brathflen Corporation shortly after the Jedi enclave left following a foiled plot by Palpatine.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BERCHEST_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BERCHEST_HISTORY,Berchest's striking red-orange Leefari Sea and huge crystal deposits carved into cities by master artisans have long been noted for their extraordinary beauty. The largest is Calius Saj Leeloo, the City of Glowing Crystal, which is one of the Twenty Wonders of the Galaxy. The city's kilometer tall Sanctum Arcti is dedicated to the art citysculpting and is perhaps the finest example of it. Beyond its tourism, the planet is a thriving trade world in both the legal and illegal traffic that goes on in the radioactive spacelanes of the Anthos Sector.

By the time of the Yuuzhan Vong War, Bescane was a fortress world of the Imperial Remnant. The system was attacked by the Yuuzhan Vong in 28 ABY.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BESPIN_HISTORY,Located in Anoat sector of the Outer Rim, Bespin is a large gas giant that has no landmasses, but its upper atmosphere of billowing clouds hosts an envelope of breathable air called the Life Zone where a variety of life forms have evolved. Local inhabitants have established floating colonies here such as Cloud City, a popular resort and mining town. The Cloud City Wing Guard handles security, police and emergency duties for Cloud City while the Baron Administrator runs the city's day to day and mining operations. Bespin's chief and most profitable export is precious tibanna gas that is used to power blasters and other energy weapons.

The planet's population has struggled for years to establish their settlements atop the planet's island spires to create an island paradise. TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BETHA_HISTORY,Betha II once thrived as a rest point halfway along the Daranc Run. But over time, the planet's weak gravity began to lose its hold on its atmosphere and the remaining few colonists finally abandoned it. Not until 20 years before the Battle of Yavin did anyone set foot on the barren world again. An enterprising smuggler brought in atmospheric generators that re-established a gaseous envelope in a small area near the equator. A small smugglers' enclave sprang up around the generators, and Betha II became known as a hideout and repair base. Smuggling traffic has remained constant ever since.

The planet was loaned to the Galactic Republic at the start of the Clone Wars for fighter development, where Koensayr Manufacturing developed the Y-wing series.

The entire southern continent of the planet is covered in freighter manufacturing and testing facilities linked to the orbiting shipyards by 354km lift cables.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BOTHAWUI_FAUNA,Ak | Bothan sky dragon | Skar'kla | Krusk | Bok | Other
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BOTHAWUI_HISTORY,The base of the Bothan spy-net, Bothawui is a clean, mountainous, forested and cosmopolitan planet. Streets in the major cities are clean and wide, and lined with tall buildings built of a natural glittering stone. This well-organized world has always been considered neutral territory, since it has been an active hub for operatives of every stripe. Although they have much to offer the galaxy, Bothans are probably most well-known for their intrepid spies.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BOTHAWUI_POPULATION,Native: Bothan\nImmigrated: Human\n2.5 Billion
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BOTOR_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BOTOR_HISTORY,The ursine Botori were masters of the two dozen systems of the Botor Enclave tucked in the fringes of a nebula, ranging from Botor itself to Kerensik. The Botori feud with the neighboring Daupherim runs deep, with their latest five century war being one of no less than forty since their admissions into the Republic. Laws and regulation have been passed to deal with the perennial conflicts, but as long as they stay local intervention is rare.

This Praxeum joined with the Jensaarai and began to wreck havoc throughout the sector, and in response a Jedi task force led by Yoda was called in to deal with them. As a result, the Bpfasshi have long since held a hatred for all Jedi.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BRENTAAL_FAUNA,Kundril
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BRENTAAL_HISTORY,Located in the Bormea sector of the Core Worlds, the wealthy planet sits at the strategic intersection of the Perlemian Trade Route and the Hydian Way. Brentaal IV is a dry planet whose eight continents are separated by small salty oceans. Most available land is covered with starports, industrial facilities and thriving trade markets that serve its 65 billion inhabitants. Thousands of cargo haulers and transports pass through the Brentaal system daily making the area ripe for piracy and other illegal affairs. Brentaal IV maintains a local security force to maintain the safety of the trade lanes while these ships are in system.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BRENTAAL_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human\n65 Billion

Sometime after the Battle of Yavin, Imperial forces led an assault on a Rebel base in the system killing Rebel agent Karlon.

Brigians take the security of their environment seriously and their patrol ships are notable for the speed with which they intercept incoming shipping.

It was therefore chosen as a long term base for the Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit, who became local heroes and reshaped the planet's industry into a formidable fortress and ground vehicle factory.

In time, it became one of the four Mining Worlds of the Anoat sector. Eventually, chromium and dolovite mining operations ended up destroying the planet's indigenous flora, transforming Burnin Konn into a nearly uninhabitable wasteland.

Known both for its advanced technology and as the corporate origin of Blastech Industries and Byblos Drive Yards, both of which still have facilities on the planet, the giant cities and towering buildings often exceeded 50 kilometers in height.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BYSS_FAUNA,Nocturnal wildlife
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BYSS_HISTORY,The mysterious planet Byss lies at the heart of the Imperial holdings in the Deep Core. An eerie blue-green sunlight bathes everything on the planet in an unusual glow, contributing to the overwhelming strangeness of the world. The planet itself is something of a myth, built up to pique the curiosity of those who have heard of a mysterious paradise planet ruled by the Empire. With tranquil scenery, low geological activity, exceedingly mild seasons, and the feel of a luxury resort, the planet appears to be the perfect place to live, which is exactly what Emperor Palpatine wants people to believe. The Emperor's dark side energies corrupted the world and its inhabitants allowing the planet to be quickly built up.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_BYSS_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human | Other\n19 Billion

It is rumoured that the Jedi learned morality from the Caamasi people, whose name has come to mean "friend from afar" in many languages.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_CAL_SETI_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_CAL_SETI_HISTORY,Cal-Seti was a planet located several sectors from N'zoth. It was at the intersection of the Agricultural Circuit and the Metellos Trade Route. The capital, Ramsees Hed, was an Imperial docking facility. It was infiltrated by Kyle Katarn in 1 ABY. In 4 ABY, Imperial Intelligence reported that the Black Fleet had been destroyed near Cal-Seti, but in 16 ABY, it was discovered that the fleet had actually been captured at N'zoth.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_CALONICA_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_CALONICA_HISTORY,Calonica was an Imperial planet. The planet's capital was surrounded by 50 000 LX-2 comlink mines, but a spot inspection found that less than ten percent of the mines contained explosive charges. An investigation revealed that the Alliance to Restore the Republic had discovered the mine's comm code and disarmed them remotely. Then they were dug up, the explosives removed, and buried back in the ground.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_CAMDEN_HISTORY,Camden is an Outer Rim trade planet that sees many small freighters come and go. Its main starport is commonly referred to as "Snakes' Den," due to its unofficial capacity as a major fringe society crossroads. Outside the starport, the mysterious alien informant known only as "Eyes" carried on a brisk information trade in the Jade Simian bar to the detriment of the Empire.
