By the time of the Galactic Civil War, Tartaglia was the site of the Nova Vaults of Kakitai bel Toyouin, which supposedly were buried under the planet's surface and were filled with unimaginable treasures. However, the vaults resisted all attempts at detection, and eventually, millions of treasure hunters perished on Tartaglia. Over time, the planet's crust began to crumble and came to feature many deep holes.

Xim would use the planet to test a powerful Tionese weapon during his reign, whose destructive power was so great it lead to the creation of the massive Pentakus Crater. Its major spaceport of Yefowr would be a hotspot for smuggler during the Galactic Civil War.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TATOOINE_HISTORY,Along the Outer Rim, the harsh desert world of Tatooine orbits the twin suns of the Tatoo system. Tatooine is marked by mesas, canyons, and expansive desert seas. Most settlers operate moisture farms in the desert, but cities such as Mos Eisley attract a wide range of criminals and rogues. Tatooine's many dangers include bands of roving Tusken Raiders, carnivorous krayt dragons and sudden sandstorms. Tatooine is controlled by the Hutts, powerful gangsters and crime lords that have made the desert world into a hub of criminal activity including gambling, smuggling, and slavery.

It was created as an experiment in face-to-face customer service by an interstellar bank. Customers were guaranteed to meet with living bank representatives, did not need to make scheduled appointments, and were offered a fantastic variety of comforting recreational activities and accommodations.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TELLANROAEG_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TELLANROAEG_HISTORY,An astronomical object within the widest sphere of Hutt influence, Tellanroaeg is located on the Corellian Run in the Outer Rim. Due to its proximity to such an important hyperspace route Tellanroaeg is most often used as a layover point and transit hub for those traveling to and from other important planets in the region (including Ryloth further along the Corellian Run). This makes it a target during times of conflict, such as during the Clone Wars, and an ideal base for pirate groups.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TELLANROAEG_POPULATION,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TELOS_FAUNA,Cannok
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TELOS_HISTORY,An Outer Rim world on the Hydian Way, Telos was devastated during the Jedi Civil War. Following an Ithorian led project to restore the planet, it became known as a tourist trap for its natural beauty. In the last century of the Old Republic however, the planet experienced civil war and corruption. Industry polluted the planet and caused irreparable damage to the natural landscape.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TELTI_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TELTI_HISTORY,Telti was a moon in the Inner Rim and the location of a major droid construction facility which covered nearly the entire lunar surface. The moon had no atmosphere and all the manufacturing facilities were located in domed structures connected via air-tight tunnels and overland corridors. It was guarded by a wide range of battle droids and guard droids.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TENUPE_FAUNA,Monkey-lizard | Spidersloth | Wasber
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TENUPE_HISTORY,Tenupe was a mountainous and tropical planet located within the Tenupe system on the Sparkle Run. It was orbited by the Shattered Moon. It had multiple inland seas and was controlled by the Killiks; however the planet was captured by the Chiss during the Battle of Tenupe of the Swarm War in 36 ABY. Jagged Fel and Alema Rar spent two years marooned on the planet after the Dark Nest Crisis taking away a Twi'lek story-chain from there.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEPASI_FAUNA,Achtnak
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEPASI_HISTORY,Tepasi was a planet located within the Tepasi system of the Core Worlds. A forested world situated on the Commenor Run, it was colonized by Humans before 26 000 BBY and was already emerging as a hub of commerce when it became a founding member of the Galactic Republic. In 200 BBY, Tarzen Tagge provided aid to the planet after it experienced a series of groundquakes, precipitating the crime lord's founding of the Tagge Company. Tepasi remained loyal to the Republic during the Clone Wars, and after the death of Emperor Palpatine it was the site of the crumbling Tagge Company's only remaining facility.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEPASI_POPULATION,Immigrated: Humans
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEPTIXII_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEPTIXII_HISTORY,Teptixii was a blue-green planet located in the Teptixii system of the Unknown Regions. In 0 ABY, the Galactic Empire Task Force Admonitor commanded by Captain Voss Parck, alongside Captain Calo Drusan's Imperial Star Destroyer Chimaera, engaged a fleet of Warlord Nuso Esva's forces over the planet which comprised eight Firekiln warships and twenty-seven escort ships.

Long range communications are blocked due to the planets depth within the Transitory Mists. This makes it attractive both as a retreat for the wealthy and a haven for pirates and bandits.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TETH_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TETH_HISTORY,Once a prominent vacation world for the Hutts, Teth’s economy collapsed when the Galactic Republic began crackdowns on the criminals and deep space pirates based on this remote planet. The Galactic Empire further continued this program by building a military base and turning the planet into a Human-controlled agriworld as a bulwark against Hutt schemes. The Hutts responded with investment and bribery, significantly expanding the criminal underworld on the planet and exploiting part of the harvest income. Despite the turmoil Teth remains an important hyperroute junction on the edge of Wild Space.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TETH_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human\n45 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEYA_FAUNA,Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEYA_HISTORY,Teya IV was a planet located within the Teya system of the Expansion Region and was the homeworld of the Strak. It was where Willm Lywin founded the Teyan Praxeum in 4600 BBY. In 4000 BBY, Mistress Tannis taught Vici Ramunee and Veni Ramunee at the Praxeum. This is where the Teyan Apologia was written. The planet was made up of many villages and hamlets, with no real colonization.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEYA_POPULATION,Native: Strak\nImmigrated: Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEYR_FAUNA,Mesa Groat
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEYR_HISTORY,Located near the juncture of three busy spaceways and wearing a spectacular four-thousand-kilometer-long canyon like a dueling scar, Teyr was one of the galaxy's boom worlds. Most of the boom was in visitors and vacationers. Fearing unbridled growth, Teyr's leaders purposefully discouraged would-be immigrants with a maze of regulations, a series of successively higher application hurdles, and a determinedly officious Citizen Services Corps. The unofficial tourism motto was "Come see spectacular Teyr Rift. Then go home." Pyre Folas hosts Mobquet Presents: Fastest Land Beings, a celebrated annual race that breaks many speed records. Far from the Rift lie other, quieter regions of Teyr, places that aren't dependent on tourism to stay alive. In the heart of the agricultural Greenbelt lies Griann, a sleepy working-class town arrayed in a perfect checkerboard grid. Farther still is Sodonna, a city in decline whose strategic position at the mouth of the Noga River once allowed it to control all merchant traffic on the waterways.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TEYR_POPULATION,Demonym: Teyria\nImmigrated: Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THALASSIA_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THALASSIA_HISTORY,Thalassia was home to a species of ponderous shelled reptilians. To compensate for their ungainliness, Thalassians use jetpacks or personal repulsor units to hunt their prey. The Thalassians have been in the business of slavery for millennia and are well established in every hive of scum and villainy across the galaxy, from shadowports to under the table dealings of senators.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THALASSIA_POPULATION,Native: Thalassian
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THANDRUSS_FAUNA,Draft Animals
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THANDRUSS_HISTORY,Thandruss is a world with a rigid social hierarchy among its Krytollaks, a 3 meter tall race of carapaced insects who disdain ranged weapons. The plant and rock eating aliens had only acheieved primitive radionics when the planet was discovered, but already had a planetwide government under Empress Devir. Krytollaks have a deep admiration for central authority and Devir was quick to pronounce herself subservient to Emperor Palpatine. Though they see some of his servants are corrupt, Krytollaks have a near unshakeable belief in the justness and order of a single ruler and are confident that the excesses of their local governors will be corrected once the situation is made clear to the higher authorities.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THANDRUSS_POPULATION,Native: Krytollak

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_MAW_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_MAW_HISTORY,A black hole cluster near Kessel, the Maw is one of the wonders of the galaxy. It is visible only because of ionized gases being drawn into the black holes.  Grand Moff Tarkin created a top secret research and development facility at a gravitational island at the center of the black hole cluster.  This facility, known as the Maw Installation, is where the first Death Star was built.  Conjecture arose regarding whether the Maw could have occurred naturally or if it was built by a vastly powerful ancient race.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_MAW_POPULATION,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_REDOUBT_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_REDOUBT_HISTORY,The Redoubt was a remote star cluster that contained at least ten habitable planets and hundreds of stars. Discovered over 200 years prior to 22 ABY and claimed by the Chiss Expansionary Defense Force, the cluster was to be used as a refuge in the event that the Chiss Ascendancy was overrun by any foreign invader. Exploration of the Redoubt by the Chiss only began in earnest around 27 BBY. The Redoubt contained high radiation levels, and the proximity of stars to each other made navigation difficult. There were only a few safe routes into The Redoubt, and they were all guarded by Chiss fortifications, which prevented foreign intrusions. A planetoid in The Redoubt was the resting place of the ill-fated Outbound Flight Project which crashed there in 27 BBY.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_REDOUBT_POPULATION,Immigrated: Chiss
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_WHEEL_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_WHEEL_HISTORY,Constructed in 84 BBY by the Quirassh entrepreneur Doffen Gaitag, the Wheel is a large space station in the Mid Rim Besh Gorgon system. Benefiting from its location along the key Perlemian Trade Route, in a star system with no natural planetary bodies, the Wheel quickly gained success as trading post and renowned gambling den; a sprawling space city with a hundred docking piers, full of casinos and other facilities to entertain all visitors.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THE_WHEEL_POPULATION,Immigrated: Various\n300 000

It was home to a major Alliance to Restore the Republic base, Thila Command. The planet had relatively huge mountain ranges, of which the peaks of its gigantic mountains were visible from orbit.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THONBOKA_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THONBOKA_HISTORY,The ThonBoka, also known as the ThonBoka Nebula, was a sack-shaped nebula in the Centrality full of gases, organic molecules, carapace-creatures and interstellar plankton. Its name translated into Basic as "StarCave Nebula." It was home to the sentient Oswaft species, a vacuum-dwelling race that had a unique natural ability to make hyperspace jumps. The isolated location of the ThonBoka ensured the Oswaft lived in near-total obscurity until the Imperial Period, when the Galactic Empire learned of their existence and came to see the pacifistic species as a threat.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THONBOKA_POPULATION,Native: Oswaft
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THRAGO_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THRAGO_HISTORY,Thrago was a small moon located in the Thrago system of the Unknown Regions with a swirling atmosphere of yellow sulfuric clouds. It was little more than a hubba-shaped lump of rock, barely ten kilometers from end to end and so blanketed in dust that its thousands of craters had a soft, almost featureless look from orbit. It was the location of Supply Depot Thrago, belonging to the Chiss. Jacen Solo tricked a number of Jedi Knights, including Jaina Solo, Zekk, Tahiri Veila, Tesar Sebatyne and Lowbacca, into believing the Chiss were planning a pre-emptive strike on the Killiks. The Jedi used StealthXs to help Jacen attack Thrago, provoking the Chiss to attack the Killiks, as Jacen intended.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THRAGO_POPULATION,Immigrated: Chiss
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THRANTIN_FAUNA,Blarcluur
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THRANTIN_HISTORY,Thrantin, originally OM813, began as a small planet covered by low grasslands, orbiting a red giant star. But the planet has been reshaped by a well-funded terra-engineering project to create a range of extreme environments, from the Heavy Grav Mountains to the Ammonia Ocean. On this huge range of environments, the prestigous Galactic Outdoor Survival School holds training and exams.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THULE_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THULE_HISTORY,Thule was a semi-arid planet known for its rich savannas continually bombarded by lightning storms, but it also hid a secret Sith stronghold. The rocky outcroppings charred black from being hit by lightning served as a form of sustenance for unusual, bioluminescent moss that made the rocks glow with an eerie light.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THUSTRA_HISTORY,Thustra was a planet located within the Thustra system, Sumitra sector of the galaxy's Expansion Region. It was also the homeworld of a Near-Human race known as the Sephi. Until the advent of the Clone Wars Thustra was a member world of the Galactic Republic and thus represented in the Galactic Senate by a senator. By 21 BBY, approximately nine months into the civil war, the Sephi betrayed the Republic and joined Thustra with the rebellion of the Confederacy of Independent Systems.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THYFERRA_HISTORY,Thyferra is a green-and-white world covered with rain forests; it has little axial tilt and is unbearably humid. Although the Vratix already had colonized other bodies in their system, contact with the Republic ushered in a technological revolution. The Vratix soon invented the healing fluid called bacta; the remarkable fluid was extremely profitable, and powerful Vratix operations spread across many worlds.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THYRSUS_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THYRSUS_HISTORY,One of the Six Sisters, a confederation of six worlds in the Mid Rim that also includes Eshan, Begali and Almera, Thyrsus is the homeworld of the near-Human Thyrsians. Believed to be the result of Arkanian experimentation with the Human genome, the Thyrsians and their neighbouring Echani are diametrical opposites in both culture and appearance; the only commonality between the two sub-species being a shared history of strong warrior traditions. The famed Sun Guard mercenaries started out as a Thyrsian special forces unit founded during the Begali Uprising against the Echani-led leadership of the Six Sisters which eventually morphed into the nomadic army centuries later once peace was re-established between Thyrsus and Eshan.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_THYRSUS_INFLUENCE_REWARD,• Unlocks Sun Guard at 7+ influence

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TIBRIN_FAUNA,Fire-coral | Saberfish
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TIBRIN_HISTORY,Homeworld to the Ishi Tib, Tibrin is completely covered by small oceans, broken up by many small reefs and sandbars. The ocean currents distribute warm water around the planet to create a planet-wide temperate zone. Ishi Tib cities are built on and from the natural reefs and their society is very respectful of the environment, taking great care with new technology and use of resources. In the last years of the Republic the planet was ruled by a murderous dictator, Suribran Tu, until Count Dooku executed him personally and installed Sora Bulq as provisional governor under the aegis of the Confederacy of Independent Systems.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TIBRIN_POPULATION,Native: Ishi Tib\n300 Million

Though the reptilians are best known as assassins and gunbeings, they are savvy in business, well integrated into the galactic economy, and clever engineers of enviro-technology, droids, and high tech weapons. Nearly every family has a Tiss'sharl corporation as a patron, which, in return for dedicated labor, provides the family with nearly all of its material needs.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TOGORIA_HISTORY,Togoria was a richly verdant world largely unspoiled by industry or mineral extraction.  The native feline Togorians were highly suspicious of offworlders, with humans in particular not welcome unless invited (crash landing offering a one-time exception to the rule).  By 4 ABY the planet had become an Imperial fortress world which despite strong attention from New Republic forces survived intact to join High Admiral Teradoc's Greater Maldrood and was only finally liberated four years later.

J'Quille, a male Whiphid who worked as a bounty hunter, hailed from Toola.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TOYDARIA_FAUNA,Shaoryn | Grabworm
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TOYDARIA_HISTORY,A wet world covered in nutrient-rich muck-lakes, Toydaria is located in the Mid Rim.  The native Toydarians are winged mammals with an uncommon natural resistance to influence from the Force. The planet has historical close ties to Hutt Space both as a vassal and trading neighbour and in fact Hutt caravels are one of the few classes of vessel permitted to fly in within the planet's atmosphere - due to the high risk of collisions between airspeeders and the many Toydarians flying about. Toydaria remained officially neutral during the Clone Wars however King Katuunko did allow the Republic to use the planet as a base following an impressive display of strength by Jedi Master Yoda and three clones.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TOYDARIA_POPULATION,Native: Toydarian\nImmigrated: Hutt\n11 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TRANDOSHA_FAUNA,Frist shark
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TRANDOSHA_HISTORY,Located in the Mid Rim, Trandosha is in the same planetary system as Kashyyyk, the Wookiee homeworld. Trandosha is a desert world with few notable features. Also referred to as Hsskor, the planet is the homeworld of the warlike reptilian Trandoshan species, whose society is structured as a strict class system. Trandoshans are known throughout the galaxy as slavers and bounty hunters. The slavers often operate on Kashyyyk in search of Wookiees to sell as slave labor, while the many bounty hunters from and visiting Trandosha gather at the Bounty Hunters' Guild council chamber located here. Due to heavy competition for contracts, local bounty hunters are much more willing to provide their services.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TROOS_FAUNA,Armored Crebik | Bahl Fly
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TROOS_HISTORY,Troos is a wild planet of cold, subartic forests populated by an astonishing variety of arthropodal life. The most curious of these is the armored crebik, an arboreal predator that takes the novel approach of dropping onto its unfortunate prey to crack their shells. The crebik's own carapace is so thick that is affected little by these falls, and is indeed too strong for most larger predators of the world.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TRULALIS_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TRULALIS_HISTORY,Trulalis is an emerald-gold orb of largely untamed wildernesses of mountains, grasslands, noble black forests, and waterways; never heavily settled. After the Issori natives of the neighboring planet entered a spacefaring era, they quickly dominated the human colonists and established a low-tech world status. Few inhabitants object to the ban of higher technologies, nor are most infractions policed. In the Republic's golden age, Trulalis boasted one of the finest schools of liberal arts education. Lavish theater productions, dramatic performances and other fine arts made the now rural, backwater world a thriving center of cultural literacy.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TRULALIS_POPULATION,Native: Human\n1 Million

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TUND_FAUNA,Various (extinct)
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TUND_HISTORY,Tund was a verdant terrestrial planet and potent Force nexus situated upon the north-eastern borders of the Open Sea in the Centrality sector. A remote colony world of the Sith species first settled around 27,700 BBY, Tund saw use as a prison for heretics and exiles prior to the collapse of the old Sith Empire, and subsequently came to serve as both the headquarters of the Sith-blooded Sorcerers of Tund and the adopted homeworld of the Toong species. The planet was destroyed in 5 BBY by a biological warhead released by the Croke Sorcerer of Tund Rokur Gepta.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TURAK_HISTORY,Turak IV was a planet located within the Turak system of the Outer Rim Territories. It was a seismically-active planet covered in lava plains where the Galactic Empire inflicted great damage on the Rebel Alliance, although at the cost of many S-1 FireHawke Heavy Repulsortanks. This disaster befell the Imperials because General Maltaz allowed the two battalions of tanks to be cut off from their supply lines by counterattacking Rebel forces — known as the Hitak Harriers — based in the volcanic Hitak mountains.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TYNNA_FAUNA,Suuri
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TYNNA_HISTORY,Located in the Expansion, Tynna is a resource-rich planet that has long been a member of the galactic community. Corporations operating under the Old Republic developed Tynna's resources but kept its natural beauty intact. Tynna is the homeworld of the otter like Tynnans. The fur-covered Tynnans have poor eyesight. A layer of fat beneath their skins protects them from the cold waters of their planet. Tynnan society is entirely state-run: All citizens have free access to housing, food, education, and other benefits. Tynnan shipyards are known for the speed at which they can produce corvette sized ships.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TYTHON_FAUNA,Acid spider | Flame tygah | Guid | Hook hawk | Horranth | Horranth | Manka cat | Silik lizard | Uxibeast | Wingmaw
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TYTHON_HISTORY,Tython was a planet in the Tython system of the Deep Core that played a pivotal role in the histories of the Je'daii Order and its successor, the Jedi Order. A verdant world that was incredibly rich in the Force, Tython was inhabited by an unknown species hundreds of thousands of years before the rise of the Galactic Empire, and it was visited by both the Gree and Kwa, two of the earliest species to have achieved interstellar travel. In 36,453 BBY, the great pyramid ships known as the Tho Yor brought pilgrims from over a dozen species from across the galaxy to Tython, where the pilgrims learned to harness the Force and established the Je'daii Order, basing their philosophy of balance upon Tython's two moons, Ashla and Bogan. The Je'daii soon realized that Tython was unsafe for those who could not sense the Force, and as a result, the Tythans spread out to settle the other worlds of the Tython system.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_TYTHON_POPULATION,Demonym: Tythan | Tythonian\nNative: Flesh Raider | \nImmigrated: Human | Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UBA_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UBA_HISTORY,The Ubese had colonized the other worlds around their star when explorers found the Uba system, bringing off-world technology and trade. The rapid advancement seemed to be a boon, but it developed into an obsession to advance backed by unscrupulous leaders and work to revive forbidden weapons. Fearful and embarrassed neighbors sent a fleet to humble the upstarts and the bombardments tragically detonated the Ubese weapons. Uba I, II, and V were scoured by radioactive firestorms; Uba III was shattered into a new asteroid belt. Only Uba IV barely continued to support life in poisoned soil and parched ocean beds. The ashamed sector officials destroyed records of the system. The few surviving Ubese became savage raiders that often hired themselves as mercenaries and bounty hunters in the intervening centuries.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UBA_POPULATION,Native: Ubese
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UBERTICA_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UBERTICA_HISTORY,Ubertica is world with verdant fields and hills despite the low oxgyen content. After the destruction of the Uba, some ashamed activists transported a few of the surviving Ubese to the pristine world. These colonists prospered, but were despised by the remaining survivors on Uba IV, who called them yrak pootzck, cowards of impure blood. Some "True" Ubese hunt down the denizens of Ubertica across the galaxy. The worst offender was Savax Clan-Vorsazg, War-Master of the Southern Wastes, who collected a small fleet and bombarded Ubertica.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UBERTICA_POPULATION,Immigrated: Ubese

The planet was attacked during the Thrawn Campaign, using the trick of cloaked ships below the planetary shield creating the appearance of weapons firing straight through.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UTAPAU_FAUNA,Varactyl
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UTAPAU_HISTORY,Located in the remote Tarabba sector, Utapau is a planet pitted with enormous sinkholes. Many of these sinkholes contain intricate cities that stretch deep into the caves and crevices beneath the planet's surface. Because of the porous nature of Utapau's surface, water is scarcely found there. It is abundant on the floor of the sinkholes however, which grant the cities a readily available water supply. The inhabitants are near-human, divided into two distinct species, the tall, lordly, slow-moving Pau'ans, nicknamed Ancients for their astonishing longevity; and the stubby Utai, called Shorts, both for their stature and for their brief busy lives. Their energy economy is based on wind power. The planet's limited interstellar trade began only a few decades ago.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_UTAPAU_POPULATION,Native: Pau'an | Utai\n95 Million

Vagar Praxut was the homeworld of the sentient Vagaari species. Around the year 30 000 BBY, the inhabitants of Vagar Praxut became enslaved by the Infinite Empire of the sentient Rakata, who altered the ecosystem of the planet. As the Vagaari adopted a nomadic lifestyle in space no later than four decades before the Battle of Yavin, Vagar Praxut eventually became almost entirely deserted.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VAHABA_HISTORY,Vahaba was a red giant in a well-populated cluster of stars, circled by numerous planets. At some time in the past, a celestial catastrophe had destroyed the largest of those worlds and scattered its remains in a thin ring around the sun. The belt's misshapen stony satellites were spread across such an enormous distance that the Vahaba Asteroid Belt was not a hazard to navigation. Vahaba was known for its asteroid-mining operations and the skill of its metal fabricators.

As such, Tymb was charged with controlling the corporation's headquarters on Varada Five as well as coordinating the expansion of her employer's interests in the Varada system and the rest of the sector.

During the final stages of the war, the Human Sith Lord Ulic Qel-Droma attempted to force a Republic military officer to transmit orders to the Republic fleet instructing all the fleet's starships to arrive at the coordinates of the Vento system shipyards—an action that would cause the destruction of many Republic vessels. Qel-Droma's orders were not executed, however, due to the Sith Lord being arrested by a group of Jedi Knights.

The planet became an infamous site of military activity in the New Sith Wars, with numerous Sith Lords claiming the title of "Conqueror of Verdanth".
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VERDANTH_POPULATION,Native: None\nImmigrated: Bothan | Various
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VERGESSO_FAUNA,Dianoga
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VERGESSO_HISTORY,Asteroids located in the Lybeya system of the Bajic sector in the Outer Rim. The Tenloss Syndicate built a hidden shipyard in a nickel-iron asteroid referred to as the Vergesso asteroid. The asteroid is the size of a small moon and has a surface pockmarked with craters. The asteroid field containing Vergesso is one of the less violent asteroid fields in the galaxy and is easier to navigate than most others.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VERGESSO_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human\n85 000
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VERNET_FAUNA,Nightscreeg | Nerf | Barve | Mud Crab | Fish
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VERNET_HISTORY,Vernet was a planet with multiple moons and a mountain range running across the spine of its southern continent. The wide, fertile grasslands were dotted with scattered farms around the modest starport of Maz-Verlin. The farms were mostly worked by droids and produced barma beans, chorba-root, grains, and livestock. The Empire conscripted and indoctrinated the population quite successfully with propaganda and drugs. After the planet's native sons were shipped out, the base became a regional training center for other nearby planets, which could also tighten the grip on the local farmers with no conflicts of interest.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VERNET_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human\n4000
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VEROLEEM_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VEROLEEM_HISTORY,Veroleem was a planet in the Unknown Regions. The planet was a remote desert planet that became a refuge for Republic colonists who sought to escape an increasingly authoritarian galactic government. The colonists named the world after their leader and staked their claim on it, seeing it as both remote and with no readily exploitable resources.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VEROLEEM_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human | Other

Its inhabitants joined with the people of Junction V, Delaluna, and Bezim to build the Station 88 Spaceport. This coalition of planets chose to remain loyal to the Galactic Republic during the Clone Wars.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VIIS_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VIIS_HISTORY,The gladitorial traditions of the Viis Empire respected no surveyor's boundary: despite its location in the Unknown Regions, spokesbeings like Dahlomad Tkhii were vocal participants of the sport's community. Viis' Aaroun were bred to be graceful and brutal spectacles and their makers were contempuous of shortcuts such as fighting droids. The Viis Empire was eventually decimated by the parasitic horror Mnggal-Mnggal.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VIIS_POPULATION,Viis | Aaroun
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VIRGILLIA_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VIRGILLIA_HISTORY,The Virgillia system is home to several small colonies on its various habitable planets and moons. The recent history of the system has been shaped by the tensions between the ruling Aristocracy and the Virgillian Free Alignment that flared into open conflict before the Clone Wars. The Aristocracy used its near complete control of weapons and later Imperial backing to brutally suppress the Alignment, but stubborn resistance, support from the Rebel Alliance, and the withdrawal of Imperial forces to fight elsewhere meant the Alignemnt eventualy prevailed. The grateful freedom fighters supported the Rebels in turn after forging a new Virgillian Council and an uneasy truce with the Aristocracy.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VIRGILLIA_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VJUN_FAUNA,Pirate Gulls | Vjun Foxes | Whip-Smelts
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VJUN_HISTORY,Heavily pelted with an acidic rain and steeped in the Dark Side, Vjun was sometimes mistaken as a dead world. In truth, the dark world featured a vibrant, if hostile, ecosystem. In generations past, the pirate Malreaux extorted a title of nobility for himself from the locals in exchange for a cessation of his raids. In 29 BBY, the last Viscount of House Malreaux had begun experimenting with midi-chlorians, attempting to draw upon the power of the Dark Side. But within less than a year, nearly the entire populace was driven into a murderous frenzy that left few survivors. During the Clone Wars, Count Dooku briefly set up a base of operations at Chateau Malreaux, laying a trap for Yoda that ultimately failed. Darth Vader would later establish Bast Castle on the planet, which acted as both the Dark Lord's refuge and a space for his trophies. In the post-Endor period, various Imperial Remnant groups would appropriate Bast Castle, attempting to extract power from it.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VJUN_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human\n55 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VOLIK_FAUNA,Reyko
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VOLIK_HISTORY,Volik was a temperate planet with rocky terrain that was located within the Unknown Regions' Volik system. The planet's surface was dominated by expansive grasslands and small mountain ranges, as well as numerous canyons and waterfalls. Volik's plant life included the bannak and tanna trees and the mumau root, while the reyko was the most frequently encountered example of the planet's fauna.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VOLIK_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human | Other

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VONAK_HISTORY,Vonak was a harsh and warlike planet of plains and forests on the fringe of known space that prized warfare above all else, with dangerous beasts hunted for practice and children set against predators soon after they could stand. Medicine was a neglected field of study, but the quality of weapons belied the otherwise primitve looking society. None of these weapons or skills helped when the Yuuzhan Vong attacked.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VONAK_POPULATION,Native: Cilare

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VORTEX_FAUNA,Goa lawah
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VORTEX_HISTORY,Vortex was a Mid Rim planet in the Glythe sector. The planet was the homeworld of the Vors, a race with wings but no emotion. Vortex was known for its violent weather, in particular sharp winds caused by an abnormal tilt in the planet's axis. During winter months, vast polar ice caps formed almost instantaneously by the freezing gases in the planet's atmosphere. The sudden change in air pressure made huge currents, which swept across the entire planet.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VORTEX_POPULATION,Native: Vors\n6.5 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VORZYD_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VORZYD_HISTORY,Vorzyd IV was a planet in the Vorzyd system. All the Vorzydiaks on Vorzyd IV were dedicated to manufacturing products and were disdainful of fun and games. The Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn and his apprentice, Obi-Wan Kenobi, went to Vorzyd IV in 39 BBY and discovered that their monitoring systems were being sabotaged by the newest generation of Vorzyd IV, the Freelies, who wanted to change their parents' "work-only" attitudes. They succeeded, and Vorzydiak culture improved as a result.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VORZYD_POPULATION,Native: Vorzydiak\nImmigrated: Hark
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VOSS_FAUNA,Boliphant | Horranth | Mawvorr | Nexu | Shaclaw | Uxibeast | Vorantikus | Wingmaw
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VOSS_HISTORY,Voss was a terrestrial planet in the Voss system located in Allied Tion sector of the Outer Rim Territories. Originally only having one native sentient species known as Gormak, the planet was discovered by the ancient Sith who used Voss for its resources, until the Jedi arrived to drive them off. Seeking allies among the natives, the Jedi taught some of the Gormak tribes on how to use the Force, but the Gormak were unprepared and those tribes radically evolved into a different looking species named the Voss. The Sith were exterminated, while the Jedi left or died out, and the Voss settled on the mountain peak, establishing the city of Voss-Ka. The Voss society turned to Force-sensitives for guidance, as Voss Mystics received Force visions that proved true without exception, Interpreters made sure each vision's meaning was understood correctly, and the Three governed the entire Voss people.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_VOSS_POPULATION,Native: Voss | Gormak\nImmigrated: Human

Covered in swamps and bogs the world was granted to Grand Vizier Sate Pestage along with the rest of the Hegemony for his personal holdings. It would later be seized by 'Prince Admiral' Delak Krennel until its liberation by the New Republic.

It was conquered by the Yuuzhan Vong prior to the fall of Duro during the Yuuzhan Vong War.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_WAYLAND_HISTORY,A simple clerical error in the Old Republic planetary registry removed Wayland from all known charts and doomed the expeditionary vessel seeking to settle a colony there. Without support from the Republic, the human colonists regressed technologically, discarding their blasters for bows and arrows, their modern fabrics for furs and hides. The colonists continue to clash with the two native intelligent species on Wayland, despite being forgotten by the galaxy at large.

Due to the unscrupulous behaviour of KDY, particularly under the Empire, the planetary environment on Xa Fel became highly degraded due to high levels of pollution and excessive resource exploitation. In an attempt to rectify the situation the New Republic sent a taskforce of warships to Xa Fell to enforce the judicial verdicts and decrees compelling KDY to clean up operations and de-toxify the planet.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_XO_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_XO_HISTORY,Xo was a planet located within the Xo system in the Spinward sector of the Outer Rim Territories. It was the homeworld of the Zanibar species and the site of their sacrificial rituals. Before its collapse in 25,200 BBY, Xo was a remote holding of the Rakatan Infinite Empire. The Sith pureblood Kyyrah, a warrior, who was held captive by the Rakata in the Eternity Vault on Belsavis, was known as the "Jewel of Xo." Pre-Republic era humans constructed an elevated temple in the planet's jungle. The site of a great battle, the temple remained unconquered, but was abandoned, and had fallen to ruin by the New Republic era.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_XOBOME_POPULATION,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_XUAQUARRES_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_XUAQUARRES_HISTORY,A surprisingly vibrant planet in the Ash Worlds, Xuaquarres was rumored to be the homeworld of the Duinuogwuins, or Star Dragons. Powerful beyond reckoning, the Duinuogwuins preferred to avoid contact with the rest of the galaxy, staying largely uninvolved with galactic affairs. Xuaquarres, thought to be named for the mythological elder dragon Xua, was host to the Graveyard of the Dragons - a series of honeycombed caves in which the Duinuogwuins would go to die as they neared the end of their long lives. Due to their longevity, the planet was seldom visited by the Duinuogwuins, and the Galactic Empire would establish a royal Imperial Storehouse similar to the one on Mount Tantiss. Whether the Empire knew about the Duinuogwuins' graveyard is unclear, but when a group of space faring adventurers accidentally alerted the Duinuogwuins, the Star Dragons would return and wreak havoc, destroying the entire Imperial fortress and allowing the would-be heroes to escape with their lives.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_XUAQUARRES_POPULATION,Native: Duinuogwuins (speculated)
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAGA_MINOR_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAGA_MINOR_HISTORY,Located in the Outer Rim, Yaga Minor is a temperate world home to the insectoid Yagai. The planet is known throughout the galaxy for its major shipbuilding facilities. Its native species widely support the Empire despite its prominent anti-alien bias. The Old Republic project Outbound Flight departed on its mission from here in 27BBY.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAGA_MINOR_POPULATION,Native; Yaga\nImmigrated: Human\n4 Billion
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAGDHUL_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAGDHUL_HISTORY,Home to the Givin, Yag'Dhul is a barren world in the Inner Rim. The planets moons create powerful tidal forces that draw the atmosphere and water to different parts of the planet, leaving the rest in vacuum. Due to this, the Givin have evolved with a hard exoskeleton that enable them to survive in total vacuum for up to a day. The Givin are brilliant mathematicians and supported the CIS by discovering new hyperlanes during the Clone Wars.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAGDHUL_POPULATION,Native: Givin\n750 Million

Originally settled by humans during the time of the Old Republic the inhabitants lost contact with the wider galaxy for millennia until a Republic survey vessel rediscovered the planet. During that time the Yashuvhi developed a local Force tradition after a Jedi became marooned on the planet following a crash-landing.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAVIN_FAUNA,Piranha beetle | stinger lizard | bellybird | crystal snake | howler
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAVIN_HISTORY,Located in the Gordian Reach sector of Outer Rim lies the monstrous orange gas giant Yavin. The moon Yavin 4 is a hot jungle world with four main continents separated by six oceans. Volcanic mountain ranges and wide rivers can be found amid the thick jungles. The moon has both a wet and dry season, and chaotic storms whip across its surface every few months. Yavin 4 was settled by a Dark Lord of the Sith, Naga Sadow, and his minions. Over time, survivors of Sadow's people evolved into the deadly Massassi Warriors, who built dramatic temples throughout the jungles before they vanished into legend.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YAVIN_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human\n1 000
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YINCHORR_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YINCHORR_HISTORY,In 3 ABY Imperial Governor Marcellin Wessel held the Yinchorri Council of Elders hostage: in exchange for the Council's release, the Yinchorri attempted to deliver Leia Organa captive to the Empire. In the end, the Empire slaughtered the Council, and the Yinchorri rebelled. As a result, the Empire devastated the world, destroying its main city, Tol-Kashorn. It was during this battle that a young soldier, Redd Wessel, first came to the attention of his superiors.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YINCHORR_POPULATION,Native: Yinchorri\nImmigrated: Human\n200 000
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YLESIA_FAUNA,Reek
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YLESIA_HISTORY,Located on the edge of Hutt space, Ylesia was a tropical world with notoriously turbulent storms that made the planet's atmosphere deadly to navigate. During the reign of the Galactic Empire, the Besadii Clan Hutts charged their t'landa Til seneschals to set up a false religious colony, using a unique t'landa Til mental projection of joy to effectively drug pilgrims into willing slaves. Ylesia did not produce any spice, but the industrious rate of their spice refinement made the planet into a burgeoning financial power until the operation was disrupted by the fledgling Rebel Alliance. Years later, it would become a base of operations for the Peace Brigade.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YLESIA_INFLUENCE_REWARD,Ylesia's turbulent atmosphere prevents the landing and production of aerial units.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_YLESIA_POPULATION,Native: t'landa Til\nImmigrated: Human\n300 Million

An ancient feud existed between it and Goelitz, a planet several systems away, and Ylix occasionally was attacked by Goelitz revolutionaries. As a youth, the infamous Gallandro enlisted in the Ylix militia and served with distinction. The planet's forces eventually defeated Goelitz after many devastating battles.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZADDJA_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZADDJA_HISTORY,Zaddja was a barren planet located in the Zaddja system of the Trilon sector, beyond the most inhabited regions of the galaxy. It was orbited by a small asteroid shroud. The planet seemed mostly uninhabited, and never received any attention from the Galactic Empire during the Galactic Civil War. It was not until years after the fall of Emperor Palpatine that Imperials first visited the planet. Stormtroopers under the command of General Redd Wessel had been dispatched to the planet to capture Kir Kanos. The location, however was an ambush, and most of the stormtrooper squad was killed by explosives.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZAKUUL_FAUNA,Iknayid (native) | Mawvorr | Lurkers | Wingmaws
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZAKUUL_HISTORY,Zakuul was a backwater planet located within Wild Space having possessed almost no contact with the rest of the galaxy due to its position. But early in its history, the planet was conquered by the builders of Iokath, meant to be one of the many worlds to be used as live testing sites for their weapons. The early Zakuulans eventually immigrated to the planet and lived there, becoming a superstitious people who lived in tribes led by three Force-sensitive leaders known as Matriarch, High Shaman and Champion. In those early days, the Zakuulans believed in a deity known as Zildrog, who was a mentor and guide to warriors and could gift unto them the Breath of the Dragon, a spiritual boon.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZAKUUL_POPULATION,Demonym: Zakuulan\nImmigrated: Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZANBAR_FAUNA,Anooba | Momong
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZANBAR_HISTORY,Zanbar was a temperate moon in the Outer Rim Territories marked by stretches of swampy vegetation pierced by towering plateaus of striated rock. It was inhabited by anoobas, vicious creatures with pronounced lower jaws and sharp teeth, fearsome growls, and thick patches of fur across their spines that lived throughout the Outer Rim Territories; and momongs, small six-limbed carnivorous primates that also inhabited the planet Trandosha and its moon, Wasskah.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZANBAR_POPULATION,Immigrated: Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZELTROS_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZELTROS_HISTORY,Zeltros was a planet in the Inner Rim and the homeworld of the Zeltron race. In an opulent world, hedonism was the doctrine encouraged by the empathetic Zeltrons, and Zeltros was continuously in a state of never-ending revelry. The Zeltron's appreciation of beauty in its many forms led to luxurious art and architecture covering the world, and the galaxy's most wealthy favoured Zeltros as a vacation destination. Many who came to the planet were taken in by the celebratory atmosphere and the powerful pheromones of the Zeltron people to the point they found it difficult to leave, and even planetary invasions often floundered in the face of Zeltros' planet-wide festivities.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZELTROS_POPULATION,Native: Zeltron\n5.3 Billion
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZFELL_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZFELL_HISTORY,Located in the Galactic Core, Z'fell is, as a Yevethan colony, one of the original 13 worlds in the Duskhan League. The planet was attacked by the New Republic during the Battle of N'zoth.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZFELL_INFLUENCE_REWARD,• Unlocks T-Type and C-Type Thrustships at 9+ influence
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZFELL_POPULATION,Immigrated: Yevetha
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZIOST_FAUNA,Sith pincerbug | Ziost scorpion | Ziost reptomammal | Vine cat
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZIOST_HISTORY,Ziost, sometimes referred to as the "Gateway to the Empire" in the days of the Old Republic, was a rocky world and potent focal point of the dark side of the Force located in the Ziost system, a star system in the Sith Worlds region of the Outer Rim Territories' Esstran sector. It was an adopted homeworld of the Sith species. Connected to other nearby worlds by the Nache Bhelfia hyperlane, Ziost was considered one of the five sacred Sith planets and served as a capital of Adas' Sith Empire and the Old Sith Empire.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZIOST_POPULATION,Immigrated: Herglic (extinct)| Human (extinct)| Monolith (extinct)| Sith (extinct)\nHistoric: Millions\nModerne: None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZIRULAST_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZIRULAST_HISTORY,Zirulast turned into a dry and dusty world some two thousand years before the rise of the Empire. The brawny Temolak were scarcely fazed by this development: the hardy scavengers simply began harvesting water from the planet's icy rings. The Temolaks spend most of their time searching for derelicts they can repurpose for their own use or resell to primitive cultures. They prefer to avoid interaction with and regulations from high technology societies, but they will fiercely defend their salvage finds.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZIRULAST_POPULATION,Native: Temolak
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZOLAN_FAUNA,Herabe | Monzu
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZOLAN_HISTORY,A contagion caused by a meteorite strike some two thousand years before the fall of the Republic left the native Zolanders vulnerable to sunlight, forcing them to abandon large sections of the planet. Attempts to genetically engineer a resistance led to the creation of the shape shifting Clawdites. Though the changelings have prospered, tensions between them and the Zolanders have defined the planet's history ever since.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZOLAN_POPULATION,Native: Zolander | Clawdite
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONAMA_30BBY_FAUNA,Climber
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONAMA_30BBY_HISTORY,Zonama Sekot (Ferroan for "World of Body and Mind") was a living, sentient world capable of travelling through space. Zonama was the planet itself, while Sekot was the living intelligence of Zonama. It was also the seed of the original Yuuzhan Vong homeworld, Yuuzhan'tar, which had been destroyed during the Cremlevian War. Zonama Sekot's first settlers were black-haired humanoid Ferroan colonists and red-skinned Langhesi colonists, and the living world was ruled by Magisters. The Ferroans were believers in the Potentium view of the Force. The Langhesi and Ferroans settled the planet in 89 BBY.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONAMA_30BBY_POPULATION,Immigrated: Ferroan | Langhesi | Human
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONAMA_FAUNA,Climber
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONAMA_HISTORY,Zonama Sekot (Ferroan for "World of Body and Mind") was a living, sentient world capable of travelling through space. Zonama was the planet itself, while Sekot was the living intelligence of Zonama. It was also the seed of the original Yuuzhan Vong homeworld, Yuuzhan'tar, which had been destroyed during the Cremlevian War. Zonama Sekot's first settlers were black-haired humanoid Ferroan colonists and red-skinned Langhesi colonists, and the living world was ruled by Magisters. The Ferroans were believers in the Potentium view of the Force. The Langhesi and Ferroans settled the planet in 89 BBY.

TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONGORLU_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONGORLU_HISTORY,Zongorlu was a Galactic Republic planet in the Outer Rim Territories. It was conquered by the Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders during the Mandalorian Wars, shortly after the bombing of Serroco. The Mandalorians launched their assault on Zongorlu from Taris.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZONGORLU_POPULATION,Native: Zongorlu
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZOSHA_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZOSHA_HISTORY,Zosha was a planet located in the Unknown Regions. It was a rather uninteresting planet covered with green land masses and lakes. During the Great Galactic Wars, it was the site of multiple battles between Republic and Sith Imperial forces. During one of those battles Republic forces managed to disable the Harrower-type cruiser 'Scream of Ragnos', dealing a severe blow to the Sith Empire.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZOSHA_POPULATION,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZXK_100_FAUNA,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZXK_100_HISTORY,ZXK-100346.6a was the designation given to the sixth planet in the ZXK-100346 Outer Rim system by the Galactic Empire. It is a small, frigid world of ice with a relatively flat surface. Jedi Masters hid the Soulsaber, an ancient weapon of the dark side, on its surface. They constructed a location known as the Cavern of the Light, surrendering their bodies to form a light side barrier around the Soulsaber. The Dark Jedi adept Thaum Rystra attempted to locate and steal the Soulsaber during the Galactic Civil War.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZXK_100_POPULATION,None
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZYGERRIA_FAUNA,Brezak
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZYGERRIA_HISTORY,The planet experienced success during the thousands of years the Zygerrians maintained their slave labor industry, but after the Jedi cracked down on the slaving operations, it faded from glory. Slavery was brought up again when the Zygerrians collaborated with the Separatists during the Clone Wars. During the Zygerrian Slave Empire's height, the population had fortresses and cities built on top of the mesas scattered throughout the planet, using slave labor. After the empire's collapse, these buildings remained impressive sights to tourists, bringing visitors even from Alderaan. Aside from being a major slavery world, it hosted several podraces annually, which attracted large numbers of spectators.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZYGERRIA_POPULATION,Demonym: Zygerrian\nNative: Zygerrian\nImmigrated: Human | Togruta | Other\n500 Million
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZYZEK_FAUNA,Unknown
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZYZEK_HISTORY,Zyzek was a planet located in the Zyzek system of the Unknown Regions. It was a trade hub for several nations and species situated to the east and southeast of the Chiss Ascendancy. Zyzek and its star system were protected by the Zyzek System Defense organization.
TEXT_PLANET_INFO_ZYZEK_POPULATION,Immigrated: Chiss | Watith

TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ANTEM,An unassuming backwater, Antemeridias was home to an independent warlord until after Pellaeon had taken control of the Empire. But when Getelles risked an annexation of the neighboring Meridian Sector, the New Republic retaliation was swift and complete.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ARDA,The Arda system drew in various rebellious elements from the area to an abandoned mining planet into a cell that eventually joined the Alliance. After a betrayal the industrialized world of Arda II was crushed in a bloody campaign that reasserted Imperial dominance of the system.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_BAKURA,Bakura was a recent annexation into the Empire, with its traditional leadership firmly under the hand of an Imperial governor when mysterious invaders struck just as the Emperor died.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_BRAK,The resentful Brak sector was been built up to a vital link to outlying territories under the heavy hand of Moff Ramier. Despite his successful record at quelling resistance, Brak was among the first areas to throw off Imperial rule after Endor.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_CATO_NEIMOIDIA,Once home to palatial Trade Federation estates and foundries for the CIS, Cato Neimoidia's industry now fuels the Imperial war machine. Since Moff Tarko's death, Duke Erron Irbian of Jorra is the highest ranking official maintaining the Empire's law.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_CIUTRIC_HEGEMONY,The Ciutric Hegemony was a pocket of space long ruled by the Pestage family, and was thus the natural location for Sate Pestage to flee when Isard seized power. Delak Krennel was sent to retrieve the vizier, but instead murdered him and took control of the Hegemony.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_DASTA,The D'Astan Sector is home to a group of wealthy and influential families such as the Counts of Serenno and the monarchs of Valahari. Most important was the D'Asta family itself, members of the Imperial Ruling Council until the contemptible reign of Xandel Carivus prompted them to cut ties.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ELROOD,The isolated Elrood Sector fell on hard times under Imperial control, with key junctions held under martial law and the freer potions of the sector unable to trade with the larger galactic community.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ERIADU,After Sander Delvardus's strategic goals proved incompatible with the desires of Eriadu, the prominent families of the Quintad struck an independent course.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_GAROS,Garos drew the interest of the Empire for its rare minerals with applications in cloaking technology. Despite the motivated Garosian resistance, a strong Imperial presence was maintained until the New Republic liberated it.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_HAMMERS,The Imperial Hammers Elite Regiment, commonly called 'Hell's Hammers', found itself a pawn in the chaos of Endor: its battlegroup splintered, rushed to crackdowns, fighting petty battles in power struggles between warlords. After it found itself fighting one of its own support regiments, Johans issued a recall order to all Hammers associated units that many other interested commanders followed. On Brintooin he awaited a legitimate heir to the Empire, secure in his formidable garrison but isolated and with little ability to project power.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_IMPERIAL_LIANNA,Lady Valles Santhe leveraged the vast industrial capacity of Santhe/Sienar Technologies to secure independence for her world, threatening to cut off the supply of TIE fighters if Lianna was provoked. But not all Lianns were satisfied with the arrangement, including her own fiercely Imperialist son...
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ISECTOR,The Illisurevimurasi Sector (commonly called I-sector) was the petty possession of Moff Bliss Shargael deep into the Imperial Civil War, after most warlords had killed each other, fallen to the New Republic, or were murdered by Natasi Daala.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_JARDEEN,General Arndall Lott and Captain Iolan Gendarr formed an Imperial splinter group to wage a series of raids on the New Republic. The raids made heavy use of slave-rigged drone walkers, an innovation of Lott's that would be adapted for later Imperial war droids.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_KAARENTH_DISSENSION,Radical hardliners under the authority of self-proclaimed Supreme Commander Ennix Devian who zealously fight for the ideals of COMPNOR. Armed with experimental ion cannon technology, they plot to build a fleet that will deal with both the New Republic and other Imperials.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_KAMINO,After the termination of the Clone Army contract, Kamino served as a site for several of Darth Vader's secret projects.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_KASHYYYK,Kashyyyk had always been oppressed under the Empire, but after Endor the governor Hindane Darcc engaged in a lightning campaign of brutality with the aid of Trandoshan slavers and Wookiee traitors to cement his rule, while the Grand Admiral Peccati Syn used the area as a retreat as he turned to mysticism. Once the Wookiees managed to get word to the New Republic, the atrocities and meditations on the Dark Side were both ended in fire.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_KUAT,One of the crown jewels of the Empire, Kuat is the home of Kuat Drive Yards, chief supplier of the Empire's warships. It continued to be an Imperial fortress world for years after the Battle of Endor, relying on its latest designs for self-defense as the Empire collapsed around it.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_LAMBDA,The Lambda Sector was bypassed by the New Republic's early advances as they seized control of key hyperways, leaving a substantial base for Moff Par Lankin to consolidate. He was largely ignored for a few years while he waged his low intensity war against the New Republic and competed with his neighbors.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_LUMIYA,Shira Brie was one of Darth Vader's secret apprentices. After Endor, she declared herself the Dark Lady of the Sith Lumiya and carried out attacks and clandestine plots across the galaxy.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_MAELSTROM,The Albarrio and Relgim sectors bordering the Maelstrom Nebula were driven to work closely by the pirate threats lurking in the starry mists.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_NABOO,Palpatine's homeworld was viewed as a propaganda tool by some, but as home to Chommel Sector's own Moff Panaka. He remembered the slow procedure of the Republic with no fondness, the decisive reign of his former Senator with approval, and was unwilling to let his planet suffer another invasion from those he viewed as outsiders.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_PRAKITH,Though nominally subordinate to the leadership of Sector 5, Foga Brill kept Prakith isolated and aloof, hid behind the dangerous routes of the Deep Core, guarded by a formidable navy, and dominated by his Red Police and oppressive Church of the Dark Side doctrine. His unhappy kingdom was among the longest lived warlord states.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_PRENTIOCH,Moff Utoxx Prentioch was able to quickly secure the Sombure sector, though his grand ambitions found it difficult to dislodge Moff Eyrgen of the neighboring Wornal Sector before he drew the attention of larger powers.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_PROPHETS,The Prophets of the Dark Side are an ancient order of seers founded by Darth Millennial, recruited by Darth Sidious for Imperial purposes. After they predicted the destruction of the Second Death Star, the prophets fled to the Sith world of Bosthirda to escape Palpatine's wrath and were replaced with imitators.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_PROTECTORATE,The rogue Inquisitor Adalric Brandl collected a small force, including some of the Empire's finest pilots, to secure his homeworld from both the New Republic and fragments of the Empire.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_RADAMA,Ord Radama initially welcomed the Empire's order, but chafed under the cruel rule of Ellor Orgraal. None more so than the indigenous Devlikk who were already suffering under the colonists before the Imperial anti-alien policies began.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_RAYTER_SECTOR,The Rayter Sector was relatively quiet during the reign of the Empire, though it did suffer the ambitions of Braig Farool, brutal crackdowns on Solem, and Great Bantha Breakfast Biscuit Run disaster. Nevertheless, Moff Nile Owen quickly lost control after the battle of Endor.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_RESTORED_EMPIRE,Radical hardliners under the authority of self-proclaimed Supreme Commander Ennix Devian who zealously fight for the ideals of COMPNOR. Maintaining a surplus of Clone Wars era vehicles, they seek to overwhelm their opponents and restore Palpatine's vision of the galaxy with a dangerous zeal.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_SECTOR_5,Admiral Malfkla Yzu was able to establish dominance over Grand Moff Cinzero Gann in the Deep Core's Sector 5 and became one of the many warlords fighting in the largely ignored region. He was one of the thirteen major warlords invited to Tsoss Beacon for Daala's peace talks.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_SELLASAS,Sellasas was the subject of a relentless seven week siege by the Rebellion shortly after Yavin, but the garrison held strong until a relief force arrived. The area remained under the command of Governor Jedselk throughout and after the ordeal, who kept the unrest in line and encouraged the collection of bounties.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_SHADOW_REALM,The mad and mysterious Cronal was the former Director of Imperial Intelligence under the code name "Blackhole." When he went rogue, Cronal created the persona known as Lord Shadowspawn to lead his forces of Shadow Stormtroopers in acts of piracy and terrorism against the New Republic.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_TAGGE,The House of Tagge is said to be one of the original founders of the Republic and acquired great prestige and power in their support for the Empire. Tagge Company boasts a significant military of its own to back up this soft power.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_TAMARIN,The wake of Endor left the Imperial garrisons of Tamarin demoralized, scrambling to slap down an array of hopeful planetary governments seeking independence and criminal gangs exploiting the confusion. Governor Resuun ran the sector from his flagship to increase his responsiveness to the volatile situation.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_TAPANI,The proud people of the Tapani worlds only joined the Republic some 1500 years ago, and still keep themselves aloof, holding to their own traditions.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_TIERFON,The Tierfon Sector was home to a competent fleet that caused a disaster for the Rebel Alliance at Tocan and aggressively continued operations against them under Admiral Tzenkens.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_VOGEL,Lynch Hauser was appointed commander of the Dustig Oversector during the Clone Wars where he led from Vogel 7, and was promoted to Grand Moff during the Galactic Empire's reign. Hauser was eager to wipe out all traces of the Old Republic, and took control of M'haeli following the ouster of Governor Grigor.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_WESSEX,The Relgim Oversector is the domain of Grand Moff Denn Wessex, one of the original regional governors during the Clone Wars and husband of brilliant starship engineer Lira Wessex. Lira will do anything to advance her position in the Empire, and her voice has become a feared political weapon throughout the galaxy.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_WILD_SPACE,It was on the maiden flight of a terror design that Delurin heard the news of the Emperor's death and decided he was fit to fill the vacancy. He chose the Svekk system and its Bormterran warriors as the base of a new Empire he began carving out of Wild Space.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ZAARIN_REMNANTS,Grand Admiral Zaarin's audacious coup attempt left his followers as scattered fugitives hiding from the Emperor's justice. Reports are unclear as to how many might be alive and capable of coalescing into a new power base in the post-Endor reality of fragmented warlords.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ZERO_COMMAND,Blitzer Harrsk emerged from his wounds at Endor with madness and more ambition than ever. He left the regroup point for the prison world of Kalist and established the first warlord state of Zero Command, probably the most powerful of the Deep Core breakaways, and led its ascendancy in that petty ranking until the fateful meeting on Tsoss Beacon.
TEXT_PROTEUS_DESCRIPTION_ZSINJ_REMNANTS,Warlord Zsinj's Empire rapidly disintegrated, but some of his loyalists refused to be annexed into the victorious Maldrood and Ruling Council expeditions. Most of these hardliners ultimately joined the Kaarenth Dissension under Ennix Devian.

TEXT_RESOURCES_HEADER,=============         RESOURCES        =============
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW,There are multiple resources in EaWX: 
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW_1,- CREDITS: Generated by planets and economic buildings and used to purchase units and buildings. Also, many special events, research, etc. cost credits to initiate.
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW_2,- SHIP CREWS: Generated by planets above a certain influence level, as well as Naval Academies, Space Colonies, and select special structures. Crews are spent when constructing ships.
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW_3,- POPULATION: Global limit on how many ships and units you can have at once. Increase this cap by conquering planets and building Space Colonies.
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW_4,- INFLUENCE: Each planet you control has its own Influence score. It measures how much that planet's population supports your rule. Planets with low influence can revolt and forcibly eject your troops. Planets with high influence sometimes offer unique buildable units.
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW_5,- INFRASTRUCTURE: This is the difference between your territory's total capacity for structures and the number of structures you've actually built. When you have too many empty structure slots, credit prices and build times for everything will rise.
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW_6,- WORLD DEVASTATOR MATERIALS: Gained by fielding World Devastators and destroying enemy units and structures in tactical space battles.
TEXT_RESOURCES_OVERVIEW_TEMPORARY,=== Temporary Resources ===

The Rebels have yet to see the full fury of our power and might. The Prophets of the Dark Side foretell of a new leader of the Empire; the Supreme Slavelord of Kessel, Trioculus. If you swear your fealty to our new Emperor, we shall rule the galaxy and crush the Rebel Alliance!
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_CCOGM_RESULT,- CENTRAL COMMITTEE OF GRAND MOFFS: Bertroff Hissa will come under your command with a roster of heroes, and Kessel will join the Galactic Empire. Unlocks: PX-10, Treaded Neutron Torch, and Ye-4 Gunship. Locks: AT-PT and Beta ETR-3.
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_EITHER_RESULT,- EITHER OF THE ABOVE: Unlocks: Strike Cruiser. Locks: KDY Strike.
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_PROJECT_AMBITION,Ysanne Isard: I am Ysanne Isard, the Director of Imperial Intelligence. The death of our beloved Emperor has left our once-proud realm in hands of lesser men with no vision. Sate Pestage is a coward who will sell out what remains of the Empire to save his skin. We need a strong leader, one who can best the Rebels and force these ambitious warlords to unite under one banner again. You can join me, or be sure to fall with him.
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_PROJECT_AMBITION_FAILURE,If a different faction takes Coruscant, or Sate Pestage dies before completing either of the above options:
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_PROJECT_AMBITION_ISARD,- Isard's regime will assume control of the Galactic Empire.
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_PROJECT_AMBITION_RESULT,- PROJECT AMBITION: Ysanne Isard will come under your command with a roster of heroes, and Thyferra will join the Galactic Empire. Isard may be upgraded to Lusankya (Executor Star Dreadnaught) at the Imperial Capital.
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_PROJECT_AMBITION_SUCCESS,If enacted before Coruscant falls or Sate Pestage is killed:
TEXT_STORY_GE_ERA_2_PROJECT_AMBITION_ZEROCOMMAND,- Zero Command will take control of the following planets if the Galactic Empire controls them. [ Kalist VI, Abregado-Rae ]

TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DEFEAT,You will lose if all units are defeated. If your base is destroyed you can continue fighting incoming waves with your remaining units.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DESCRIPTION_01,- Once the battle starts you have 30 seconds before the first attack wave to purchase units and set up your defenses.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DESCRIPTION_02,- Purchased units are added to your reinforcement pool and can be brought in anywhere on the map.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DESCRIPTION_03,- Attack waves consist of randomly generated forces that increase in number and size as you defeat more waves.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DESCRIPTION_04,- You will be awarded credits after defeating each wave to spend on more units, upgrades and extra defense stations.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DESCRIPTION_05,- Upgrading your station will provide access to new units. In doing so, some units may be locked and will be no longer buildable.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DESCRIPTION_06,- The level of difficulty increases after waves 5, 10, 15, 20, and 25 when new, more powerful unit types are added to the attacking force.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_DESCRIPTION_07,- Every fifth wave will include a hero unit (boss battle). After wave 25 every wave will be a boss battle.
TEXT_SURVIVAL_BODY_VICTORY,When you are defeated, you receive a final rating based on how many waves you defeated.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_1H_TANK,The biggest and toughest variant of the Imperial repulsortank line, it was also the most widely produced by its manufacturer Ubrikkian Transports. Sporting weaponry that rivaled artillery in range and power, its durable armor allowed it to withstand sustained fire even from other heavy assault vehicles. The 1-H's lumbering size and speed means only the most highly trained and specialized tank crews can pilot them effectively.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1H_TANK_COMPANY,Company: 2x Imperial-class 1-H Repulsortank
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1H_TANK_HEALTH,Health: 375 (Heavy Vehicle)
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1H_TANK_MOVE,Speed: 1.5 | Turn: 2.0 
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1H_TANK_WEAP1,1x Heavy AV Blaster Cannon: 1 / 2.0s / 400m
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1H_TANK_WEAP2,1x AV Blaster Cannon: 4 / 3.0s / 400m
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1H_TANK_WEAP3,1x AI Blaster Cannon: 4 / 1.0s / 320m
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1L_TANK,An economical version of the standard Imperial repulsortank, and a by-product of Colonel Zel Johans' push to standardize the Imperial Army's use of repulsor vehicles. Usually crewed by two standard army troopers under strict supervision of sergeants by comlink. Without the expensive heavy gun intended for the standard garrison tank it is unable to match up against equitable vehicles on the field, but gains in speed and usage against infantry in exchange.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1L_TANK_COMPANY,Company: 4x Imperial-class 1-L Repulsortank
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1L_TANK_HEALTH,Health: 250 (Light Vehicle)
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1L_TANK_MOVE,Speed: 2.0 | Turn: 4.0 
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1L_TANK_WEAP1,1x AI Blaster Cannon: 4 / 1.0s / 320m
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1M_TANK,The standard class of the Imperial repulsortank, intended to replace the aging FireHawke. It has an additional primary weapon compared to the 1-L that grants it a vast increase in effective firepower. While 'average' for its class outside of durability, the 1-M is a danger to infantry, vehicles, and even larger armored units in tandem with other tanks. A favorite of platoon officers in the field for keeping close command of their units.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1M_TANK_COMPANY,Company: 3x Imperial-class 1-M Repulsortank
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1M_TANK_HEALTH,Health: 325 (Medium Vehicle)
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1M_TANK_MOVE,Speed: 1.75 | Turn: 3.0 
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1M_TANK_WEAP1,1x Heavy AV Blaster Cannon: 1 / 2.0s / 400m
TEXT_TOOLTIP_1M_TANK_WEAP2,1x AI Blaster Cannon: 4 / 1.0s / 320m
TEXT_TOOLTIP_2_WARPOD,Ulig Abaha Limited's #2 Warpod pinnace was a competent, short-range fighter in its time, but compares less favorably to modern strike craft; however, it is cheap to operate and possesses a relatively large cargo hold and personnel space, traits which have enabled it to serve a bomber role and kept it popular in CSA space.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_2M_CHERI_REPULSORTANK,The Empire of the Hand's derivative of the 2-M Repulsor Tank, the 2-M Cheri is sleeker and downsized compared to its Imperial counterpart. Its battlefield role mimics the Imperial 2-M, with the 2-M Cheri having the ability to move in and out of battlefields smoothly. It is armed with focused anti-vehicle weapons, making it best suited for harassing hostile vehicles and retreating if under too much pressure.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_2M_CHERI_REPULSORTANK_COMPANY,Company: 4x 2-M Cheri Repulsortank

TEXT_TOOLTIP_2M_REPULSOR,The successor to the TX-130 series of tanks that ditches the "fighter tank" role for a more conventional design and use. Front-facing weapons are replaced by a more traditional turret while the body was more reinforced compared to earlier models. It is more resilient and mobile then many medium tank options, with a varied weapon loadout.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_A_WING,A small and fast interceptor designed to eliminate TIE fighters in dogfights and strafing attacks. TEXT_TOOLTIP_A5RX,The A5-RX battle tank, also known as the A5-Recon Juggernaut, was a smaller, specialized version of the A5 Juggernaut, re-designed for speed and manoeuvrability across the battlefront. First used by the Galactic Republic during the Clone Wars and later by the Galactic Empire in the Galactic Civil War, seeing action across the galaxy, the A5-RX was well armed despite its smaller size.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_A9,The newest in a series of KDY interceptors, the A-9 is used in small numbers by the Imperial Remnant and other organizations that acquired production lines. While fragile it is deceptively powerful, speedy, and, in the right hands, is superior to many other fighters.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_AAC_1,A fast and maneuverable light tank perfect for lightning strikes and harassing Imperial walkers. The AAC-1 was developed for planetary defense forces after the Clone Wars, and the blueprints made their way to the Rebel Alliance via payment in a card game.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_AAC_2,A fast and maneuverable light tank perfect for lightning strikes and harassing Imperial walkers. The AAC-2 features added deflector shields, and while a rarer upgrade generally restricted to guarding command bases under the Rebel Alliance the larger resource base of the New Republic allows it to be standard.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_AAC_3,A complete redesign of the original AAC into a larger tank trading speed for power. It was caught in a difficult development process, with allegations of corruption and developer disagreements leading to the cancellation of the original project. Though the work was salvaged and a complete product delivered, the popularity never matched the second entry in the series.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_AAC_3_COMPANY,Company: 3x AAC-3 Hovertank

TEXT_TOOLTIP_AGGRESSOR,The Aggressor was an experimental Star Destroyer that gained prominence through Tyber Zann's Consortium after he recovered its blueprints from the first Death Star's wreckage. It was designed around two gigantic superheavy cannons: one turbolaser and one ion cannon. This allowed it to deal with capital ships at long range.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_AGGRESSOR_ASSAULT_FIGHTER,A large, high-performance assault craft that blurs the line between fighter and system patrol craft. It is staggeringly maneuverable for its size and power to the point of being best suited for droid or highly-adaptable pilots, even with inertial dampeners. While it is able to engage with any target it is best used against enemy fighters and bombers.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_AIREN_CRACKEN,General Airen Cracken was a resistance fighter from Contruum who joined the Rebellion during the Galactic Civil War. When the Rebellion became the New Republic, Cracken took command of New Republic Intelligence and during the New Republic's Liberations Campaign between 4-6 ABY, he oversaw Shadow Operations, a collection of Sabotage, Disinformation & Assassination missions. Over the next few years he would be replaced several times due to mainly political agendas; however each time was brief as his replacements would either be killed in combat or retire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ALESON_GRAY,Ninth Cousin of Queen Mother Tenel Ka Aleson, Grey was the Dukat and Duch’da to Lady AlGrey of the Hapan house AlGrey. He would have military command of the Battle Dragon Kendall during the Swarm war. The Kendell would be the flagship of both the "Hapan Fleet of the Glorious Defender Queen" and the "Heritage Fleet" during its career before being destroyed during the civil war over Hapes.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ALEXANDRA_WINGER,Force-sensitive daughter of the Imperial deserter Matt Turhaya, Alex was adopted by Tork Winger of Garos IV after she was found buried under rubble from an Imperial raid on Janara III. She became an undercover freedom fighter with the resistance on Garos and came in contact with Luke Skywalker, which brought the New Republic's attention to the world. After Garos was liberated, Alex would sign up with the New Republic under Blue Squadron as 'Blue Three' before serving the admiralty as a captain.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ALLEGIANCE,A small star battlecruiser design resembling a larger version of the Imperial and Tector classes. The design lacks a dedicated fighter hangar and devotes most of its volume to weapons and support systems that make it an extremely dangerous solo opponent to most small cruiser taskforces, able to self-escort and eliminate that sort of formation. Conversely, it is not as suited to fighting other peer heavies.

Its sheer size allows for multiple large capital ships to dock at once for resupply and refuelling. It even carries repair facilities which can dry dock frigate sized vessels and smaller. Altors are the unrecognised lifeline of Imperial fleet operations.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ALYSSIA,Niece of Ta’a Chume, and startlingly similar in appearance, Princess Alyssia of Hapes was also the younger sister to Princess Chelik. TEXT_TOOLTIP_AMATHA_FETZ,Amatha is a well-known and well liked rogue. She is a hero for the "oppressed working class" of the planet, having risen to fame and fortune through hard work, shrewd deals and a little piracy. Now she owns her own shipping firm, Tion Hauling, and has been accepted by the Liann upper class. Tion Hauling is one of the "tolerated" groups that pursued Valles Santhe's unofficial goals such as gunrunning to the Rebel Alliance.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ANDAL,Moff Andal was, by all accounts, a lazy and incompetent, if well-connected, administrator who typically left administration to the sector governor while he spent his time abroad. He was responsible for fostering rivalry between Captains Pryl and Zed, the best of the Elrood Sector Fleet.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ANLYS_TAKKAR,Wife of the exiled Moff Derran Takkar, Anlys would try to sabotage the wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade along with several of her husband’s followers, including her secret lover Chik Apla. Assassination attempts on Luke and Mara consistently failed with one of her followers, Banner Sumptor, betraying her and Chik Apla. Anlys attempted to get revenge against Banner, but would fail as with most of her endeavours.
