TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARBMAB,Arbmab was a tank commander in the Imperial Hammers who served in battles such as the assault on Protazk. Like most men in the unit, he was fiercely loyal to "Rancor" Johans and was a capable soldier, able to react and destroy enemies whatever the battlefield threw at him.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARC170,Ancestral to the later X-wing series, a multirole attack snub meant to survive interceptor swarms. The design dates to just before the Clone Wars and has seen service with various groups since. They are used to support dedicated bombers while still being able to assist other fighters or bulking up the firepower of fleet groups.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARC170_127TH,Rejected by the Galactic Empire but too loyal to defect, the clone pilots of the 127th Gunship Wing are one of many grandfathered clone squadrons from the collapse of the Old Republic. Efficient pilots marred by the prejudice for clone soldiers in the Imperial Military, Zsinj's utilitarianism was their only saving grace from a lifetime of patrol duty.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARC170_SHADOW_SQUADRON,A veteran clone squadron personally trained by Anakin Skywalker. The end of the Clone Wars and the rise of the Empire saw Shadow Squadron bearing its banners hunting down pro-Republic remnants before it was disbanded due to a history of disciplinary problems matched with a rising mistrust of clones. Many years later Vader would reform the squadron under his retinue following the sacrifice of one of its former members in his defense, granting them honorary tasks like guarding initial construction of the Death Star II.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARDAX,A Caridan army officer who served as second in command to Ambassador Furgan. Despite this, Ardax believed Furgan to be incompetent, foolish, and perhaps treacherous.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARDELLA,The Trandoshan leader of the Black Hammer Pirates who terrorized Galantos with her ships and swoop bikes. Ardella ended up in possession of the Freebird, an Imperial lander with a gruesome part in the Blackwing Virus incident.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARDEN_LYN,Arden Lyn was a female Human and master of Teräs Käsi who belonged to the Followers of Palawa. At the end of the Great Jedi Purge, Lyn was reawakened by three of Darth Sidious's Inquisitors, who spared her life so that she could serve Sidious as an Emperor's Hand. Lyn did so for a while, spurred on by the Sidious's promise that he would resurrect Xendor from the dead. When she finally came to accept that Sidious had been lying, she turned rogue, and aided Grand Admiral Demetrius Zaarin in his attempt to abduct Sidious. The endeavor failed, and Lyn was forced to flee.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARHUL_HOLT,The scion of a legendary Navy family the Holt Cross was named after, Arhul Holt served for nearly four decades in the Navy and became a teacher at the Raithal Military Academy during the Galactic Civil War. Disillusionment with the Empire's principles pushed Holt into defecting to the Rebel Alliance, and he was exfiltrated from the academy with the assistance of slicer Pash Galae.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARMADIA,The standard T-Type Armadia thrustships, these represent the bulk of the Duskhan League's home-grown ships. Thrustships possess relatively few weapons for their status as the Yevethan's main combat cruiser, but the weapons it does have are more varied than among peers in its size, and work well in combination with each other. Surprisingly for its small size it is able to carry a respectable amount of fighter support while maintaining a strong hull and shielding.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ARMYTROOPER,The most common of the Empire's ground fighting forces, Imperial Army troopers are drawn from conscripted and enlisted individuals from across the galaxy. As the Army benefited from substantially increased investment following the transition to the Empire, contemporary Imperial Army troopers are extensively trained and armed with gear for both planetary assaults and occupation. While less iconic than stormtroopers and not as zealous in their morale, Army troopers are plentiful, dedicated fighters who should by no means be underestimated.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ASSAULT_FRIGATE,Originally intended as a way for the Rebel Alliance to make better use of crew-expensive Rendili Cruisers, the Assault Frigate began as a stripped-down hull with additional automation, leading to a fast and surprisingly efficient vessel. Several sidegrades and additional developments have led to the current generation of New Republic vessels, which are purpose-built, fast, well-armed, and fitted with enough shielding to make up for the loss of hull density.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ASSERTOR,The second largest of the standard line of Imperial star dreadnoughts, the Assertor simplifies the design of the massive Executor in order to reduce costs and make a smaller more efficient frame for combat. Its outer hull is covered in weapon emplacements carefully placed to ensure maximum overlapping arcs of fire for each possible engagement angle. Apart from the excessive complement of guns, the Assertor also houses extensive fleet command facilities enabling it to be a capable and extremely powerful flagship.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_AST5,An aerial scouting vehicle popular with the Hutt Cartels. The AST-5 has two rotating anti-infantry cannons and an enclosed cockpit, and the capability to place itself in an upright sentinel position. The interior of the aircraft contained a variety of sensor feeds displaying information to the enclosed cockpit, and it is rumored skilled pilots could even take an AST-5 into a planet's exosphere or even space for a short time.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ASTARTA,Astarta was a Hapan privateer who would become the personal guard of the Hapan Prince Isolder and accompanied him on his hunt for the pirate king Harracan. Astarta would stay by his side until her eventual retirement, despite her unrequited love for the Prince. She was both a capable commander of ships and investigator into the plots common in Hapan noble society.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BATCH,Martio Batch was one of the original twelve Grand Admirals appointed by Emperor Palpatine. Batch preferred to avoid attention and disliked Imperial court politics. As a result of his low profile, he earned the nickname the "Invisible Admiral." Following the Imperial defeat at the Battle of Yavin in 0 BBY, the Emperor tasked Batch with designing a fool-proof cloaking device on pain of death. Despite succeeding initially, the project was destroyed by the Rebels and Batch fled into hiding, fearing the Emperor's wrath, until hearing news of Palpatine's death at Endor.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BELTANE,Governor Beltane is a quintessential Balmorran who single-mindedly pursued the development and sale of premium weapons. TEXT_TOOLTIP_BERI_TULON,Beri Tulon is an ex-Imperial officer who used to work directly under Malcor Brashin. He was Tantor's superior for a short while, and heavily impressed upon the young general the concept of a soldier's honour. Eventually he grew disillusioned with the Empire and chose to defect, but was captured by Tantor. Ironically Tantor would free Tulon after his own desertion, and both would go on to serve the Rebel Alliance and New Republic.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_BESK,The head of technology giant Dynamic Automata and only daughter of founder Metron Besk, Elta Besk was trained thoroughly in the subtle arts of corporate and armed warfare by her father. Charming and cunning in equal measure, it was her idea to have Dynamic Automata be among the first corporate signatories of the Pentastar Alignment Treaty. In the Alignment's Chamber of Order she continually sides with Grand Moff Kaine on most political fronts, and in turn Kaine sought her advice, to the consternation of other corporate members.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BLY,CC-5052, or Bly, was a clone who dutifully executed his Jedi Commander Aayla Secura when Order 66 was issued. In his service to the Empire Bly was reassigned to domestic crackdowns before he was contacted by Darth Vader to hunt the rogue Padawans down. Collaborating with Imperial Intelligence and the Inquisitors Antinnis Tremayne and Lanu Pasiq, Bly cornered the fugitives with Vader. TEXT_TOOLTIP_BOBA_FETT,A legendary bounty hunter, Boba Fett was originally an unaltered clone of the Mandalorian bounty hunter Jango Fett. Following his father's death Boba struck out on his own, with his time in the Clone Wars ranging from attempts to assassinate the Jedi Mace Windu to infiltrating Wat Tambor's citadel. Fett's notoriety truly rose in the Imperial period; often employed by the Empire and Darth Vader specifically, Fett displayed deadly efficiency in taking down his bounties. While he was knocked in the Great Pit of Carkoon during a brawl on Tatooine, even a sarlacc could not hold Fett for long.

Terrik runs his organization from the Star Destroyer Errant Venture, which he captured from Isard's fleet during the Bacta War. The New Republic allowed Terrik to keep his prize, but stripped it of most of its weaponry.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BORVO,A young Borvo found Naboo to be the perfect location to establish an extensive weapon and spice smuggling operation under the cover of the Nal Raka sporting rifle company. He tried to use the chaos of the Trade Federation invasion to his advantage, but was forced off the planet by Lieutenant Gavyn Sykes after his intentions were revealed. Naboo was a convenient base for Borvo's operations on nearby planets and he eventually returned, though carefully watched by Sykes. 
TEXT_TOOLTIP_BOSSATO,The Hutts of the Gorensla kajidic are widely recognized as the preeminent black marketeers of the galaxy, and none more so than the clan leader Bossato. She is ruthlessly profit focused, able to squeeze every credit from a venture, happy to sell to both sides of a conflict, and willing to forgive almost any transgression except losing money. Failures from Gorensla members might mean they are expelled from the clan, but Bossato will often mete out more permanent punishments for outsiders who have caused her problems.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_BOSSK,A Trandoshan bounty hunter and son of Cradossk, leader of the Bounty Hunters Guild. Following Trandoshan customs, Bossk started his career as a hunter of Wookiees, and progressed to hunting other species. During the Clone Wars he accepted contracts from the Separatist movement, and frequently assisted Boba Fett. During the Imperial period he developed a vendetta against Han Solo and Chewbacca, along with Fett when their relationship deteriorated. A fierce hunter, Bossk is a danger to all he sets his eyes on.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BRANDEI,A force-attuned captain of the Imperial Navy, Brandei had a long and storied career in the Empire. After Thrawn's death, he would settle on Janara III and patrol the sector, before being routed and forced to abandon the planet.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BRAZACK,First Lieutenant Brazack was male who served as a commanding officer of Second Platoon, B Company, of the Imperial Army Special Forces Ghost Battalion during the Galactic Civil War. Unlike almost all officers in the Battalion, he was not a graduate of the Imperial Academy on Carida; instead, he was an enlisted man who received a field promotion following a battle that saw all of his unit's officers killed. He later was promoted to first lieutenant following an action that resulted in four posthumous Medals of Valor for others in his unit.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BRELLO,An Imperial Moff charged with the governance of the Trans-Nebula Sector in the Mid Rim. Brello centralised his administration on the planet Milarian after taking over command from Moff Nebin Cray, becoming the third Sector Moff since the establishment of the Empire.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_BRENN_TANTOR,Brenn Tantor was the foremost pupil of Imperial Grand General Malcor Brashin, who trained him to command with the Battlefield Holographic Control Interface. Rapidly rising through the ranks, the young general would earn the favour of Darth Vader, and be placed in command of the ground assault on Echo Base. However, after his brother Dellis revealed the truth about their father's death and other events which shook the Tantor, he would defect to the Rebel Alliance. He'd go on to be crucial in the Battle of Endor and led the New Republic's campaigns to liberate the Core Worlds, culminating with Tantor leading the assault on Coruscant itself.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_BREZZIC,Male Human who served in the Imperial Stormtrooper Corps during the Galactic Civil War. He was assigned as the commanding officer of the 105th Stormtrooper Platoon while the unit was stationed aboard Starlyte Station. He was fanatically devoted to the Empire and his leadership kept the unit of less-than-sterling troopers from failing further. In an attempt to instil pride and creativity within his platoon, Marr broke with Stormtrooper Corps regulations by allowing the personalization of troopers' armor.

Daric LaRone christened them as the "Hand of Judgment" and they became Imperial-aligned vigilantes, hunting down warlords and corrupt officials in unofficial service of the Empire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BROADSIDE_TR,"Broadside" was the nickname of an experienced clone pilot who served in Shadow Squadron, an elite starfighter squadron personally trained by Anakin Skywalker. Broadside and his brothers stayed loyal to the Empire with its rise, but the squadron would eventually be disbanded and its members cast adrift in the Imperial military. Many years later Broadside was called to form a renewed Shadow Squadron by Darth Vader, a call the clone was only eager to accept. With Vader's death Broadside with the rest of Shadow Squadron returned to Imperial Center, ever eager to serve the Empire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BRUSC,The commander of the Star Destroyer Manticore, Brusc is a tough, demanding officer able push his men to amazing heights. TEXT_TOOLTIP_BTLA4_Y_WING,Common model of Y-wing tuned more towards a multirole fighter than assault bomber role. Reduced shield strength provides an increase in engine power and the weapon loadout is optimized for both its combat role and control by a single pilot. The ion turret is fixed forward compared to the bomber version, but this is an improvement over Clone Wars takes on the concept which lacked it entirely.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_BTLS1_Y_WING,The second of the debut Y-wing models from the Clone Wars, more common in defense forces and garrisons than well-equipped fleets. The S1 Bomber is less armored than the -B series but has better flight performance, leading to it being preferred for further development. It is best used to attack larger enemy vessels with its ion cannons and munitions.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_BUCCANEER_CORVETTE,Based on the venerable CR90 Corvette, the Buccaneer massively up-guns the frame to hold an array of turbolasers trading off fighter defence in order to be able to engage larger vessels more effectively. The Buccaneer inherits some of its ancestor's speed and uses it to control any engagement it is in while constantly dishing heavy firepower for a ship so small, making this design a threatening gunboat.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_BURKE,Known as a competent admiral held in high regard, Willham Burke helped train several of the Rebel Alliance's fighter squadrons. In the New Republic's campaign to Coruscant, Burke was placed in charge of the Third Fleet along with Kalback, and defeated Treuten Teradoc's task force at Togoria. Burke would then push to Chazwa, where following a series of raids and feints between the two ended in Okins' destruction at Colla. The following year Burke with Brenn Trantor seized Reytha, Gyndine and Commenor from the Empire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CANDEROUS_TANK,Produced by MandalMotors and a favorite among criminal groups with credits to spare, the Canderous-class assault tank is a powerful war machine. Powerful mass-driver cannons and a concussion missile launcher make it suited to take on nearly any target, and it has the armor and shielding to take what it gives out in exchange. Its repulsor-capabilities allow the Canderous to reach portions of the battle inaccessible to other vehicles of its weight class. Its major weakness is a slow and lumbering speed which could allow the vehicle to be outmaneuvered and flanked easily if lacking in proper support.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CARVIN,Carvin was an ambitious general appointed to the Imperial Ruling Council during Sate Pestage's rule of the Empire. TEXT_TOOLTIP_CBOATH,Joruus C'baoth was a clone of deceased Jedi Master Jorus C'baoth, and had severe mental instability from the cloning process. C'baoth would challenge and take the place of the Guardian of Mount Tantiss on Wayland some time after Palpatine's death, until he was found by Grand Admiral Thrawn who was in pursuit of the Emperor's storehouse. While C'baoth was initially hostile to Thrawn, he entered into an uneasy alliance that saw the Dark Jedi's Force powers aid in Thrawn's campaign, but C'baoth's mad ambition would not be held back for long.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CHARUBAH,Made by the Olanji/Charubah corporation, the Charubah was designed to be a rapid response frigate with a heavy focus on speed and alpha strike damage. Its sleek frame was a purposeful reference to an aquatic predator found on several Hapan worlds.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CHEWBACCA,Son of Attichuk, Chewbacca, or 'Chewie' to friends, was a Wookiee of great renown. When a job to take Ben Kenobi and Luke Skywalker off Tatooine led to involvement in the Rebellion, Chewbacca would convince Han to do the right thing and stay and help the Alliance. Chewie would be a stalwart ally to his friends and the New Republic for decades, while serving as a loving husband and father to his family on Kashyyyk.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CLOAKSHAPE_CW,Dated but modular fighter design commonly updated to compete with modern craft. This specific edition sports concussion missiles, and has significantly improved flight performance thanks to an upgraded power plant and engine system.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CLOAKSHAPE_NEW,A continuation of the time-honored tradition of modifying this fighter craft past its prime, the Cloakshape remains a fighter with a high cost to efficiency ratio even in the modern era. This specific edition adds on a shield generator and additional missile racks, with enhanced engines to compensate, resulting in similar flight performance to its mid-Clone Wars counterpart with the added benefit of increased sustainability in a fight.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CLOAKSHAPE_STOCK,A simple but ingenious design. The Cloakshape is nearly infinitely upgradable and modifiable, extending its lifetime in service well beyond when it was originally seen as an obsolete design. Extremely cheap, the durable but slow Cloakshape was adopted into service nearly everywhere in the galaxy where there was a need for an inexpensive defensive fighter from pre-Clone Wars Jedi to New Republic task forces.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CLUTCH,Created originally for Leonia Tavira's Invid gang, the Clutch was intended to disable freighters and convoy escort fighters on raids. An 'Ugly' reconfiguration of the TIE/ln with the addition of shield generator and ion cannon. Seen as successful enough for other groups and individuals to recreate.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CLUTCH_BOLT,Clutch fighter pilots under the Khuiumin Survivors' Bolt Squadron. The Survivors are the remnants of the Eyttyrmin Batiiv pirates who were wiped out by the Empire. They briefly worked as mercenaries, striking at Imperials and getting revenge on Captain Dadefra for his role in their massacre, but turned pirate again with the death of Palpatine. Allying with Leonia Tavira's Invids, the group would provide some of her best clutch pilots, with Bolt Squadron being the most prestigious. Leadership in the squad is elected amongst its members, a tradition for all officers in the Survivors.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CLYNGUNN,With the collapse of the Empire and the subsequent chaos surrounding the ex-Imperial generals and warlords fighting over the remnants, as well as penalties for smuggling growing stiffer, it became difficult for a smuggler to make a living. Clyngunn joined Talon Karrde's Smugglers' Alliance to fight against Grand Admiral Thrawn.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CODY,One of the most decorated clone officers of the Clone Wars, Cody was assigned to Battalion Commander of Imperial forces on Kamino's Timira City. As the Empire's cloning program was shifted elsewhere, Cody would bemoan the recruitment of enlisted humans for stormtroopers. Disdainful of their performance, Cody would often set his garrison to menial labor to punish their presumed incompetence. His nostalgia over the glory days of the clone army went so far that Cody refused to abandon his old armor, and in his private thoughts Cody admitted that the days he served alongside the Jedi were the best of his life.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_COMPFORCE_ASSAULT,Assault Troopers are soldiers of CompForce, the military branch of COMPNOR, and are rabid zealots more loyal to the ideology of the Empire over any officer or military institution. Assault Troopers are given priority in new gear and equipment, to the consternation of the Army, and undergo ferocious casualties in training that instil in them a willingness to fight on regardless of casualties. This mostly ensures those who are lucky to actually survive are processed to deployment over those with innate combat skills, which shows in the high casualty and failure rates Assault Troopers endure. These deficiencies are considered unimportant due to the vast reserves CompForce has available.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_COMPFORCE_OBSERVE,Members of the military branch of the COMPNOR, COMPFORCE Observe troopers are officially embedded into Imperial Line units to gain first hand military experience before going on to a career in the Imperial Armed Forces. Observe squads were often assigned to protect command staff in the field which also allowed them to shadow and learn from the officers. At least officially. In reality it was an open secret that Observe were political spies whose real job was to ensure that the Imperial Army was ideologically loyal to the New Order of the Empire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CORELLIAN_CORVETTE,The CR90 corvette, commonly referred to as the Corellian corvette or the blockade runner, also known as an Alderaan cruiser, was a small, multi-purpose capital ship manufactured by the Corellian Engineering Corporation. It became popular with many governments and private organizations because of its versatile, modular design, allowing different weapon emplacements to be switched out with ease.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CORELLIAN_CORVETTE_ASSASSIN,The CR92a Assassin class corvette was designed by the Corellian Engineering Corporation to be the militarised successor of the extremely popular CR90 corvette and was sold to the Imperial navy although some would fall into the hands of other users. The Assassin was a capable multirole corvette with lasers, turbolasers, and proton launchers allowing it to engage a wide variety of targets from fighters to even enemy capital ships if in large enough numbers or when in support of allied ships.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CORSEC_TROOPER,Corellian line infantry serving as the frontline troopers of CorSec against civil unrest and defenders against outside invasion. Their standard gear is produced mostly from local Corellian manufacturers, ranging from 611 Combat Armor to Corellian Arms CDEF blasters. Ion support gear is also a more significant part of their arsenal compared to most infantry. While their morale in active combat zones is unexceptional due to serving mostly as sector peacekeepers, CorSec Troopers versatile equipment and dense numbers make them foolish to underestimate.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CORVAE,Daughter of a famous Clone Wars General, Tessala Corvae is a highly effective commander in her own right. However it would largely be due to her father's fame that Tessala would be able to fight against the rampant sexism of the Empire in order to become a Major General and be one of the few high ranking female Officers in the Imperial Army. Her experience would lead to her creating the Firebird Society in order to prove the worth of women to the Imperial army.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CORWYTH,Imperial Governor of Metalorn, Corwyth ruled the industrial world as a highly regimented society that had repressive control of all information. News was suppressed to the point the Rebellion wasn't even considered a rumor among its denizens. Corwyth partnered with TaggeCo to install a weapons detection system to further enhance security, and hosted Baron Orman Tagge during an inspection of the system. Tagge's visit coincided with that of Leia Organa, who infiltrated the planet and escaped capture through the help of her old professor, humiliating Corwyth.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_COVELL,Freja Covell was a general in the Imperial Army. He was one of Thrawn's chief commanders for ground assaults during his campaign against the New Republic. His brain was completely dominated by Joruus C'baoth.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CREB,The battle of Endor was Creb's sixteenth mission in two years. Despite numerous opportunities for promotion, Creb has chosen to stay a squadron commander where he can enjoy the swift power of Sienar engineering and the joy of the hunt. He and his pilots (secretly modeled after the opposing Rogue Squadron) are something of a legend for both their successes and constant reprimands for unnecessary risks.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CRIMSON,Crimson Command is a fleet of Victory-II Star Destroyers led by High Admiral Teradoc. The name is derived from the striking red appearance caused by the use of havod alloy in their construction, but the material would have no significant impact on their performance compared to standard Victory-IIs.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CRIMSON_CONDOTTIERE,The rarest and most elite of the mechanical Guardian Corps, these droids coloured for their name are equipped with the same weapons but much better protection than their blue brethren. Working examples of Rakatan force-energizing dynamos and active energy-reflective armour more expensive than a starship of their time provide a notable boost to both their durability and the apparent wealth of their owner.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CRIX_MADINE,Founder of the feared Storm Commandos, Commander Crix Madine was once one of the most notorious men in the Empire, and set to marry into the highest echelons of Coruscanti society. However, he defected to the Alliance after being ordered to release the Candorian plague, wiping out an entire planet. In this act of defiance, Madine lost everything: his career, his prestige and his marriage. Despite years commanding Alliance Spec Ops, Madine has never escaped the guilt for his crimes.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CRONAL,Also known by his codename Blackhole, the mysterious Director of Imperial Intelligence was exiled during the Galactic Civil War. His use of the Dark Side extends his life at the cost of his sanity.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_CRONUS,Ivan Cronus was the second in command of Superior General Delvardus in the Deep Core. When Delvardus was killed at the Tsoss Beacon conference by Natasi Daala, Daala took his corpse and presented it to Cronus and the rest of his former garrison. Cronus for his part did not despair over Delvardus' death, and presented Daala with the secretly built Executor Night Hammer as a gift for her forces. Sharing Daala's ambition to unite the Empire, Cronus became one of her most trusted advisors.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_CRUSADER_GUNSHIP,A Mandalorian gunship design that incorporates as many weapon systems as the frame can take. It was originally offered to the Galactic Empire as a corvette, but the design was rejected for general naval service and, after a brief mothball period, was sold to smaller governing bodies. It is very effective at anti-fighter duties.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DAALA,A close associate of Grand Moff Tarkin, who he assigned to guard the secret Maw Installation. She stayed incommunicado in the Maw for ten years, until the chance arrival of Han Solo. She united the Imperial forces in the Deep Core by executing their feuding leaders, but her rule of the Empire was brief; she quickly resigned in favor of Gilad Pellaeon.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DANGOR,First Aide to the Emperor on matters of galactic security, who also took over the day to day management of the Empire at times, Ars Dangor served Palpatine loyally from his days as a senator until Palpatine’s death. Dangor would be one of the main players in the fallout of Palpatine's death, forming a new Ruling Council to keep the Empire united that found itself at odds with Sate Pestage and breakaway Warlords. When Grand Admiral Thrawn returned from the Unknown Regions, Dangor's council appointed him Supreme Commander of the Imperial Military.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DARCC,Once captain of Wilhuff Tarkin's Star Destroyer, Darcc settled on Kashyyyk and rose to be the local Moff. When he heard of the Emperor's death, he knew it was time to look after his own interest and he began a vicious crackdown, nearly an extermination, of the Wookiee population in conjunction with Trandoshan slavers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DARILLIAN,Formerly a captain in the Imperial Navy, Darillian joined Warlord Zsinj after the Battle of Endor.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DARIUS,Captain of an Imperial Navy Torpedo Sphere stationed at the Tallan shipyards. Ostensibly there to protect manufacturing capabilities, but also to keep an eye on Freeworlds infrastructure assets.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DARK_APPRENTICE,A clone of Galen Marek, created to replace Vader's deceased apprentice after countless failures previously. The Dark Apprentice through Vader's training has shed his attachment to Marek's connections and memories, feeling nothing but contempt for his template. When the Kamino cloning facilities were assaulted by an errant clone of Marek, Vader ordered his apprentice to watch the battle, and strike only if necessary. Vader would be captured, but the Dark Apprentice would wait patiently for the return of his master so he could fulfil his purpose and clash with the rival clone. Yet it would not come to pass.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DARK_STORMTROOPER,During the Imperial Civil War it was not uncommon for the best stormtroopers to meet random suspicious ends. Assumed dead, these stormtroopers had instead been retrieved by agents of the Reborn Palpatine and then experimented on with Dark Side alchemy within his citadel. Those who survived were forcibly reformed into elite soldiers with limited force powers and increased vitality. These Dark Stormtroopers are both deadlier than normal stormtroopers and stripped of any potential for disloyalty and life outside the cogs of the Emperor's war machine.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DARKTROOPER_P2,The second stage of General Rom Mohc's Dark Trooper Project, the Phase II Dark Trooper droids are a substantial improvement upon the preceding Phase I and serve as the programs basic combat unit. Its phrik body shell and repulsorlift engines give it excellent battlefield versatility and a distinctive frame. They are also equipped with massive assault cannons that could shred through infantry with scorching hot plasma. Combined with its relentless programming, the Phase II Dark Trooper is an extremely dangerous foe for any Rebel cell or other group it descends upon.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DECLANN,Nial Declann was a Human male Force-sensitive and one of the original twelve Grand Admirals appointed by Emperor Palpatine. Declann's impressive, Force-enhanced skills as a TIE pilot brought him to the attention of Emperor Palpatine's agents who sent him to the Prophets of the Darkside for training. When his training was complete, he was allowed to return to service in the Imperial Navy. Declann specialized in Battle Meditation to enhance his forces, but he suffered from severe mood swings.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DEFENDER,A modern descendant of the older Incom ATL Interceptor, the Defender-class starfighter is an early part of the New Class Modernization Project. The design is a symptom of the logistics requirements of a galactic power, needing a relatively cheap starfighter to be deployed en-masse for defense and supporting specialized New Class vessels. The end result is superior to the older TIE/ln.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DEKEET,Dekeet was one of the five powerful representatives present at the Pentastar Talks and was initially hesitant to join forces with Grand Moff Ardus Kaine; however, the Grand Moff's skills as an orator and negotiator won the governor over. In particular, Governor Dekeet objected to putting the Pentastar logo on vehicles and troops under their command.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DELURIN,Moff Delurin was in charge of the Dragon Heavy Cruiser program where only one prototype was completed by the time of Palpatine’s death. Having just completed a test of the prototype, Galaxy Dragon, on the world Miko, Delurin ignored Pellaeon’s recall order and broke away from the Empire. Now Warlord of Wild Space he would proceed to carve out his own small empire awaiting his chance to claim the vacant throne of Palpatine.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DELVARDUS,Sander Delvardus was an Imperial officer who married into Eriadu's Tarkin family. He fell for a refugee aid worker who became his mistress, but a heated argument left her comatose and Delvardus in despair. Delvardus with the support of Eriadu's nobles broke off following the rout at Endor and formed the Eriadu Authority. He then gave himself the title of 'Superior General' after acquiring a stockpile of military walkers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DELVARDUS_UPGRADE1,Upgrade: Praetor-II [4 ABY start only]

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DENGAR,A nasty bounty hunter who would sell out anyone if the price was right. Dengar flies the Punishing One, a modified Jumpmaster 5000 armed with a quad laser, ion cannon, and a torpedo launcher. It does not have any holding cells as Dengar prefers to collect his targets dead.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DENN_WESSEX,Denn Wessex distinguished himself at the Battle of Boz Pity and was made one of the first Moffs. He married Lira Blissex, a celebrated engineer who capitalized on Denn's influence to push her Imperator project. Wessex went on to become Grand Moff of the Relgim Oversector under the Empire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DERRAN_TAKKAR,Derran Takkar was made an Imperial Moff by Palpatine shortly before Endor, and afterwards would gradually grow bitter against the New Republic as it made gain after gain against the Empire including his sector. After the Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty, Derran would attempt to spoil Luke and Mara Jade’s wedding, seeing this as Luke stealing yet another symbol of Empire; however Luke managed to talk him down in the act. Takkar would defect to the New Republic because of this incident. 
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DESANN,Born on a planet unused to the Force, Desann's seemingly magical abilities caused him to be feared and shunned by those around him. Eventually he was recruited to Luke Skywalker's Jedi Praxeum, where he found peace for the first time in his life among beings who encouraged his talents. But gradually Desann changed, becoming a bully obsessed with power, culminating in him killing fellow trainee Havet Storm, claiming him 'too weak to be a Jedi'. He fled the Academy and joined Lord Hethrir's Empire Reborn, becoming his second in command. Coordinating with Admiral Galak Fyarr, Desann hoped to create a new race of Force sensitive warriors using the Valley of the Jedi's power.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DESANNE,Desanne was a native of Kalist whose political blunder saw him reduced to a shuttle pilot assigned to Death Squadron. After Endor, he returned to his homeworld and slowly regained responsibilities, partially through his own diligence and partially through lack of talented men in Zero Command's officer corps.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DEVLIA,A proud admiral happy with the ideals of the New Order, Devlia left his retirement to assist an Empire facing manpower shortages. He was placed in charge of a Vladet based task force, where he remained confident in his security arrangements despite increased New Republic activity.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DEZON,Pit Lieutenant Dezon was a conscript aboard the Imperial Immobilizer-418 cruiser Constrainer during Grand Admiral Thrawn's campaign. His service under its strict captain included trapping smuggling vessels with the ships gravity well generators.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DIE_WING,The TYE-wing was one of the cobbled together starfighters known as 'Uglies' TYE-wings combined the cockpit ball of the TIE Fighter with the engine nacelles of the Y-wings.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DIN,Namuura Din was a commander in the Imperial Navy, serving as one of Admiral Demetrius Zaarin's top commanders and later joining him when he splintered off from the Empire and founded a new faction during the Galactic Civil War. Upon learning of Emperor Palpatine's plans for the Rebel Alliance at Endor and the Death Star II superweapon's fully operational status, Din decided to defect to the Alliance and provide them with the information and a Missile Boat. While he met with the Rebel cruiser Link at a rendezvous point aboard his command ship, the Zeplin, and successfully transferred them, Imperial forces attacked the ships, and the Zeplin was captured with Din aboard. He divulged his knowledge to the Sith Lord Darth Vader in a subsequent interrogation.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DOMINATOR,A heavier interdictor design intended to survive in battle conditions that an Immobilizer 418 cannot. Initial ships were merely modified ISD's with hamstrung combat abilities. The design has since been refined with unique hull components, but it is not as armed as a proper combat vessel in its size range.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DOMINION,Before being appropriated by Pellaeon's forces, Dominion and its sister-ship, Megador, were part of Blitzer Harrsk's Zero Command forces, although he lacked the personnel to crew them. Pellaeon used both ships as part of his surprise offensive against the New Republic in the Orinda Campaign, taking advantage of the New Republic not knowing the vessels existed.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DORAT,Dorat was a strong supporter of Chancellor Palpatine in the latter days of the Old Republic, but was appalled by the New Order. After failed attempts to change the system, he defected to the Rebel Alliance. Despite his daughter informing the ISB of his plans, he eventually became an important Rebel and New Republic commander.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DORBULLA,One of the rarest and most impressive warships used by the Hutts, the Dor'bulla is a massive symbol of their might. Intended to fight off the increasingly common corporate and government battlecruisers developed at the end of the Galactic Republic's reign, it can take a staggering amount of punishment even for its size.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DORJA,Hailing from a family with a history in the military, Dorja was captain of the Relentless. When the Battle of Endor was lost, the fleet officers adherence to Pellaeon's orders to retreat grated at Dorja, whose seniority was ignored. Dorja aimed to take command of the Empire for himself, but Thrawn's return curtailed this, and made Dorja seethe as a non-human now lead the Empire. Years later, Dorja and the Relentless would host the Sinister Triumvirate in their plans.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DRAGON,A heavy frigate designed as a experimental terror weapon at Kuat Drive Yards shortly before the Battle of Endor. The final test run was supervised by Moff Delurin, who took the original prototype for himself and went warlord soon after. The Dragon is ideal for intimidating primitive worlds with its nightmarish shape, with its extensive turbolaser batteries and powerful concussion missiles.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DRAYSON,Born on Chandrila, Hiram Drayson began his career as an admiral of the Chandrila Defense Fleet. Drayson became acquainted with Senator Mon Mothma during his tenure, and when Chandrila was subjugated to the Empire he was put in charge of her command ship. Drayson was briefly placed in command of the New Republic's Second Fleet, and then of Coruscant's defense fleet following its liberation. While he fared poorly against Thrawn, Drayson would later be placed in charge of the Alpha Blue intelligence organization, where his experience would prove beneficial.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DREADNAUGHT,A crew-intensive but durable cruiser designed many decades before the Clone Wars. The design has spawned countless variants and has seen use by every galactic government to date in some capacity, remaining a suitable, if outdated, warship. These models boast added ion cannons and an upgrades main turbolaser battery.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DREADNAUGHT_KATANA,A modified dreadnaught recovered from the lost Katana Fleet, also known as Dark Force. The original fleet of two-hundred heavily automated cruisers were "slaved" to a single command ship in order to reduce the minimum crew requirements. The ships were originally constructed for use by the Galactic Republic but disappeared on their maiden voyage when the fleet's skeleton crew was ravaged by a fatal virus. When the fleet's secret location surfaced, both the New Republic and Empire managed to secure a number of the vessels from the armada during the Battle for the Katana Fleet.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DREDNAR,After a boring career on a merchant ship, Drednar joined Karn Granzor when the pirate boarded his freighter. Drednar learned combat and command from Granzor, but he was not alone in resenting the captain's hoarded loot and lack of style. He and several other mutineers took the chance to depart, acquire their own vessel, and defeat Granzor. Drednar went on to become a successful and colorful freebooter based on Taraloon, refusing a cybernetic eye in favor of his patch's "piratical effect". His favored tactic for finding prey was creating artificial ice asteroids in trade lanes to force ships out of hyperspace.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DREDNAR_ION,1x Medium Ion Cannon: 2 / 3.0s
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DREDNAR_SQUADRON,Fighters: 1 / 0
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DREXL,The Drexl originated in an early iteration of what would become the more famous Preybird heavy fighter, revived some years after the design changed and the original plans were shelved. Though not exceptional, it is a sturdy medium fighter.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DRK1_PROBE_DROID,Developed by Arakyd Industries, the DRK-1 probe droid was a small, spherical automaton equipped with sophisticated sensor and communications packages. The design dated back centuries to early Sith probe droid models based on schematics provided by Sith holocrons.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DROID_ADVISOR,Opens the Galactic Log, History, Tech Tree & Summary menus
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DROID_ADVISOR_NAME,Galactic Log Menu
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DROID_BOMB_FALSE_181ST,TIE Interceptor frames with custom-made Droid Fighter AI computers interred within and kitted with high explosive, remote detonated bombs. Drafted by Zsinj, these flying bombs would go under the guise of the 181st Squadron; when an unsuspecting foe begins to dogfight with the squadron, the human pilots disengage, and the bombs detonate. The massive ensuing explosion is a nasty surprise for any enemy pilots caught in the blast.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DROMMEL,A protégé of the infamous Grand Moff Tarkin, Drommel saw extensive fighting against the fledgling rebellion. His combat successes saw him rise in the ranks to the command of the Executor Class Guardian. It was in the Guardian he heard of the death of Palpatine whereupon he took his forces to his homeworld Oplovis to become a warlord. While Drommel was successful against the Rebellion, he would see only failure against the New Republic.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DROST,General Theol Drost is an Imperial Army officer known for his adept tactical skill in managing space installations, and has served nearly a decade as commander of a number of such stations. By the time of Grand Admiral Thrawn's campaign, Drost was promoted to security chief of the Bilbringi shipyards, which were a vital source of new Star Destroyers for Thrawn's armada. An attack by Talon Karrde's smuggler group which resulted in the destruction of an almost complete Star Destroyer drew Thrawn's scrutiny upon Drost, who was forced to revise his security system.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DUNELIZARD_FIGHTER,Developed by MandalMotors for the Empire in its early years, the Dunelizard design was rejected for military service as it was viewed as expensive and underpowered. It would instead find a place among the Hutt Kajidics and various pirate groups who were fond of its rugged nature. The Dunelizard is a highly modifiable craft, with this variant being equipped with slugthrower cannons and proton torpedo launchers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DUNELIZARD_INTERCEPTOR,One of MandalMotors fighter designs originally produced between the Clone Wars and the Galactic Civil War on commission from the Hutt Cartels. Based off the M3-A Scyk, the Dunelizard is designed to be more armored without sacrificing the maneuverability Hutt pilots rely upon. The result is a muscular medium starfighter that's easily customizable, with the basic armament baring linked laser cannons.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DUNHAUSEN,Dunhausen was Grand Moff who sat on the central committee of Grand Moffs, a secret committee established to rule the Empire after Endor. Dunhausen proposed Tatooine as a new capital world solely because he wanted control over Mos Eisley Spaceport. As the proposal lacked any merit, the recommendation was easily rejected by Trioculus.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DURGA,Born to clan Besadii with a disfiguring birthmark, Durga took over after the death of his father Aruk. Durga secured his position with Prince Xizor's aid, and slayed Aruk's murderer in ritual combat. When both Xizor and Jabba the Hutt died, Durga became the leading figure of both the Hutts and Black Sun. He used plans engineered by Death Star engineer Bevel Lemelisk to produce a new superweapon, the Darksaber.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DURON_VEERTAG,Duron Veertag served as a Major at the Massassai Base during the Battle of Yavin, and was later stationed at Arba Station as a general. During his tenure there, he presided over the trial of Commander Luke Skywalker for the apparent death of Captain Shira Brie under friendly fire, and found him innocent.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_DWARF_SPIDER_DROID,The Dwarf Spider Droid graduated from punishing tax avoiders for the Commerce Guild to the main line fire support platform for the CIS when the Clone Wars started. The droid proved to be extremely capable at hunting down clone troopers and their vehicles. The Empire would also later adopt the DSD to help police former Separatists' worlds and make up for the Empire’s dwindling manpower after decades of warfare.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_DYYZ_NATAZ,Dyyz Nataz was a former Sector Ranger of the Old Republic, rising through the ranks of his system's police force with a tough-as-nails reputation. While working with fellow Rangers Thaffe and Spurch Goa on a case, the trio were captured in an ambush set up by Jabba the Hutt. While Thaffe perished, Nataz and Goa escaped through Nataz crashing a freighter into Jabba's asteroid palace, earning him the nickname 'Megadeath.' Both Nataz and Goa were relieved of duty through the scandal and had bounties placed on them by Jabba. Left with few other options the two decided to become bounty hunters, with Nataz growing popular enough that a youth band adopted him as its mascot; he didn't mind so long as they followed the law.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_E_WING,Extremely well-rounded interceptor reminiscent of the X-wing and similarly armed. The fighter's use was hampered by a poor first production model and teething issues with the weapons systems, but it is now one of the New Republic's most effective snubfighters. It is able to handily perform any combat role in a fleet action.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_E_WING_PROTOTYPE,The initial production model of the well-rounded E-wing snubfighter. A well-rounded interceptor in its own right, this first model suffers from issues with its primary laser system reducing firing output until later iterations solved this.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_EC_NERWAL,Nicknamed "Easy Nerwal" by his wingmen, E.C. Nerwal was an elite Imperial pilot at the beck and call of Task Force Vengeance at the peak of his career during the Galactic Civil War. Nerwal was known for his innovative tactics and involved himself in modifying starfighters under his command. After the destruction of the task force flagship and the squadron's home in the Star Destroyer Rage, Nerwal would return to the capital of the oversector to continue his tour of duty within the Empire.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ECLIPSE,The first in a new line of Star Dreadnaughts, the Eclipse was designed to Palpatine's exact specifications as his flagship. While scoffed at by naval planners who simply saw it as an oversized yacht, the reality was that Eclipse would be the latest in a long line of the Emperor's superweapons. Its ebony hull bears modernized weapon improvements, including numerous laser cannons and gravity well projectors. The most important of these is a spine-mounted coaxial superlaser modeled on the main weapon of the Death Star, with enough power to shatter planetary shields and turn continents to slag.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_EG_STORMTROOPER,The Imperial Elite Guard were elite stormtroopers held back from the frontlines to protect valuable officers. Vital officers were assigned stormtroopers to act as their permanent guard. This role would exist even up to the Second Galactic Civil War with the Elite Guard protecting Gilad Pellaeon from his ascent to Imperial leader to his death.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ELLOR,A cold, calculating bureaucrat from the days of the Old Republic, Ellor Orgraal used the chaos of the transition into the Empire to tamper with records, swapping the hierarchical status of a deceased politician with his own. With his new service record Orgraal continued his sycophantic ways until he reached his desired position of governorship, on the world of Ord Radama. Orgraal would crack down hard on anyone who dared speak against the Empire, and was even rumored to dispose of commanders who displeased him.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_EPOAA,Mobile anti-air platform designed by the Empire of the Hand as a more fast-response platform compared to the Imperial AT-AA. The E-PO-AA can cover most of the length of a battlefield at a respectable pace, allowing the Empire of the Hand to ensure air superiority with its mounted flak cannon. It also has an anti-infantry blaster turret able to ward off small-scale infantry threats that could harass it.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ERISI_DLARIT,A Human female from Thyferra, Erisi Dlarit was an Imperial agent hired by Ysanne Isard to infiltrate Rogue Squadron. While Dlarit had an infatuation with squadron pilot Corran Horn, she would provide Isard with the means to capture and imprison Horn aboard the Lusankya facility, and also sabotaged efforts to treat the Krytos Virus by leaking a bacta convoy's location to Warlord Zsinj. When Isard fled to Thyferra and took control of her homeworld, Dlarit followed her and was placed in command of the Thyferran Home Defense Corps fighter wing, where she personally led Elite Squadron.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_ESPO,Security Police of the Corporate Sector Authority, commonly referred to as 'Espos.' Clad in brown uniforms with KZZ riot armor, Espos are tasked with corporate peacekeeping, and are infamous for their cruelty in protecting the Authority and its property. Primarily equipped with blaster rifles and riot guns for anti-insurgent tactics, in combat Espos also favor the modifiable FC-1 Flechette Launcher for anti-personnel duty, which suits their love of excessive force. The average Espo while well-trained is not highly motivated, working simply for the decent wage the job gives them.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FAARL,"The Child Conqueror", rumored to be a noble scion of Korlings, started in vicious but small time piracy. But he quickly discovered the vulnerability of the primitive worlds in the Parthovian Cluster and carved out a slave empire based in an ancient warlord's tower. Pirates and mercenaries have flocked to his brutal regime to command his enslaved soldiers, enough that a small city has grown around Fortress Baarlos. Though a cunning warrior, Faarl prefers to direct his captured fleet from his base surrounded by personal entertainers and his extensive library.

Adalric agreed, and during his harsh training Fable and Jaalib fell in love. Fable left Trulalis following an Inquisitor's attack, but she would meet Jaalib later, fully devoting herself to him and his father, despite her inner doubts.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FIRE_DRAGON,The Fire Dragon tank is a rare purpose built design for the Hapan military rather than a civilian conversion. Doctrine dictates the Fire Dragon is deployed in swarms where its heavy weapons are capable of rapidly destroying even the heaviest of enemy armour before return fire is able to break through the vehicle’s shielding and hit on the relatively weak armour. The lack of armour was a conscious design choice to allow for greater mobility so the Fire Dragon could retreat to safety when its shielding is under threat of breaking.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FIRESPRAY,The Firespray 31 class patrol and attack craft was designed by Kuat Systems Engineering for the Republic Correctional Authority however the initial batch of prototypes was destroyed by Jango Fett bar one which Fett stole. This forced KSE to mothball the project for several years although they would return to the project after several years when the Company was in a better financial position. They were heavily armed and armoured along with decent speed for a fighter its size. A very noticeable and novel feature of the design was its need to horizontally land with the engines against the ground and then rotate in orientation to actually fly after taking off.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_FIRESPRAY_BASE,Health: 26 | Shields: 12

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FLARE,An older design developed around the same time as the Baidam, intended to fulfil a similar role as a Battle Dragon screener to the point the two were conflated at times. The Flare Corvette, in comparison to its erstwhile sister design, is a multi-role support vessel. While it is unable to act as a dedicated counter against munitions payloads, it is able to engage heavier targets with results with its wide array of weaponry. Its small size and respectable speed allow the Flare to be deployed in a flexible answer to nearly any gap in Hapan formations.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_FLARE_HULL,Hull: 750 (Corvette)

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FLARE_S,Developed from the outlawing of Podracing and the rise of swoop racing to replace it in the world of high-speed galactic sports, the Mobquet Flare-S quickly became the most popular bike on race circuits. Equipped with many features that gave it increased speed and mobility compared to other swoops, yet despite this it is incredibly more difficult to operate than a standard speeder bike. Beyond the races the Flare-S was incredibly popular among swoop gangs, mercenaries and bounty hunters, leading it to be frequently modified with a forward-mounted laser.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FLEET_COMMANDO,One of the many Imperial special forces programs, Fleet Commandos are a naval branch parallel of the Imperial Army Commando program. Recruits are drawn only from the best officer candidates to the Imperial Navy and given even more training than the standard. They are also given access to some of the more exotic weaponry and equipment the Empire has access such as personal shields. Excessively dangerous enemies in nearly any situation, these commandos excel in close range assaults on enemy positions regardless if they are on land or space.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FLIM_TIERCE,A con man posing as Thrawn, Flim was able to perfectly mimic the late Grand Admiral through extensive study, excellent acting skills, and advanced prosthetics. However, Flim had no military experience, so his deception hinged on the strategic aptitude of fellow conspirator Grodin Tierce. Tierce, a flash-trained clone, was the mastermind behind the Sinister Triumvirate.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_FOGA_BRILL,Foga Brill served in the Judicials as Director of Investigation. He embraced the formation of the New Order, becoming a Moff over the recently colonised Prakith system. After the Battle of Endor, Foga Brill created a micro-empire out of his territory and established a fortress world on Prakith with isolationist policies. He briefly lifted the isolationism during the Dark Empire, but when he attempted to return to his personal fiefdom his officers quickly grew tired of living in poverty, overthrowing Foga Brill and surrendering to the New Republic.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_FORCECULTIST_MINOR,Nonspecific or local force cultists given over to the corruption of the Dark Side or assorted entities. After the New Sith Wars, groups like these are often equipped with stylized, modern-quality paraphernalia of the conflict. Many groups retain standard weapons in lieu of lightsabers or other melee oddities. Rather hard to kill individually for foot soldiers.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FORMBI,Chaf'orm'bintrano, core name Formbi, was a Chiss male who served as Aristocra of the Chaf family for more than 40 years. He served for a time as an officer in the Empire of the Hand, clashing with Force Commander Thrawn frequently. Formbi used the rediscovery of the Outbound Flight to bait the Jedi and New Republic into a plot to defeat the Vagaari, a gambit which ended out successfully despite some casualties. After the struggle against the Yuuzhan Vong, Formbi sought to quell the expansion of the Killik Colony and took command of the Chiss military against them.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_FORTRESS,A heavily armed repulsorlift that was designed in the re-conquest of the Rim to aid ground assault and occupation forces. The vehicle is preferred in urban environments where the weapon placement and maneuverability give it an edge over heavy walkers. It is best used to crush infantry groups and light vehicles and kept away from open field engagements against heavy tanks.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_FOUC,Mandus Fouc is the youngest of the sector's Imperial Customs captains. He was in the process of being transferred to a command position in the regular navy when the battle of Endor occurred. With the death of the Emperor and the chaos that followed, his transfer was never completed. He is not happy about this, because he was eagerly awaiting escape from Customs duty. Tall and handsome, Fouc has always had a way with the ladies. Unfortunately, the only ones he meets now are smugglers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_FRANGELIC,Frangelic was a career military Garwian who had a long history with Mitth'raw'nuruodo, from pirate raids to Lioaoin troubles to the Nikardun. He possessed great respect for Thrawn's abilities and intentions and was known to bend policy to work with the curious Chiss over the Ruleri's misgivings.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_FREDJA,As garrison commander for the Maw Installation, Fredja had the uneasy position of reporting to both Daala and Tol Sivron. Though a competent leader in most military operations, Fredja found himself hampered by Sivron's stifling bureaucracy and often had to cite regulations to get anything accomplished.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_G1,The G-1 was the result of a collaboration with the Gungan firm BullbaBong to develop an in-system version of the more famous N-1. A much reduced hyperdrive system gave the G-1 more power for shields and weapons, but it never proved as popular as the original model.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_G9_RIGGER,The G9 Rigger was an outdated light freighter of Corellian design. It was slow and unwieldy by the standards of fighters and other small craft in the Clone Wars era leaving it vulnerable to attack by pirates. These vulnerabilities meant it would gradually fall out of favour but its cheap design meant it would not entirely disappear from use with some records documenting that one was being still used in 137 ABY.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GANIS,Ganis was one of the many Hutts in charge of a criminal empire. His rivalry with fellow crimelord Nawnam culminated in a failed attempt to kill Ganis and take over his organization and Ganis putting a price on Nawnam's head.

Despite being one of the highest ranking survivors of the New Order, the vast majority of his underlings would answer to him in name only as they ran their own independent fiefdoms inside the Deep Core.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GAVRISOM,A Calibop Senator who served the New Republic as a member of Leia Organa Solo's coalition. Ponc Gavrisom has a reputation as a calm, if verbose, negotiator with a bevy of supporters including the Wookiee Chewbacca. Gavrisom was elected following the Corellian Insurrection to fulfil the rest of Leia's term as Chief of State, and would preside over it during the Caamas Crisis. Once the crisis was settled, he would be one of the signatories of the eponymous Pellaeon-Gavrisom Treaty that would end the Galactic Civil War with the remnants of the Empire after decades of conflict.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GELAGRUB,Their preferred habitat was among the planet's towering mushroom forests. The gelagrub had a wide mouth above which were two vertically aligned eyes, and two rows of stubby legs endowed with adhesive characteristics that enabled it to climb steep inclines. The gelagrub had to eat constantly to maintain its natural sunscreen.

With the death of the Empire, he and his associate General Lott formed their own Warlord group, leading many attacks on New Republic systems.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GETELLES,Tol Getelles was one of the final Moffs to be promoted under the Emperor's Reign. TEXT_TOOLTIP_GOLANDA,The eldest daughter of a wealthy industrialist, Golanda developed a passion for ordnance research and design during her time at Shumogi's Imperial Ordnance Research Facility. When Tol Sivron came offering her a position, Golanda initially rejected it, before Sivron bribed her director into firing her so she would have to accept. To her resentment, Golanda became director of Ordnance Research and Development at the Maw Installation. Her division developed the torpedoes and targeting systems of the Sun Crusher. She was also responsible for conducting artillery trials at the facility, which Golanda complained provided no useful data in the gravity fluctuations of a black hole cluster.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GRANT,Octavian Grant was a Tapani nobleman and one of the original circle of twelve Grand Admirals appointed by Emperor Palpatine. Grant saw himself as superior to those not of the nobility, and held a particular dislike for droids and other species, despising fellow Grand Admiral Thrawn in particular. After Endor Grant joined Grand Moff Ardus Kaine in the Pentastar Alignment, but held reservations about remaining there.

Su-mil would become a stormtrooper and replaced Twister in the group, eventually rising to position of Aurek-Seven's squad leader.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GRAVE,Imperial sharpshooter and member of the Hand of Judgment, Taxtro Grave followed a similar path as his companions. The catalyst of their defection, LaRone's killing of their superior officer, would cause him and his friends to flee the Empire, knowing they would be executed for their superior's death. Serving in an unofficial capacity as vigilantes, the Hand of Judgement would roam from place to place taking down small-time dissidents of the Empire.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_GREFNAKK,Grefnakk was sold to the Maldor Slaver Ring as an enforcer. After saving the life of the second in command from an attack by rival slavers that killed the Maldor leader, Grefnakk was given his freedom and turned to bounty hunting. He quickly racked up an impressive capture count, seeking out high risk, high reward jobs, alone or working with a more tactically-minded partner. He prefers bringing in his targets with brawling and throwing knives rather than "puny" energy weapons.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GREGOR_RAQUORAN,A former corporate head and shrewd businessman, unlike many of his peers Gregor Raquoran rose to prominence in the Empire in spite of not being an initial supporter of Palpatine or a devotee of COMPNOR. He would become Commerce Master Commissioner of the Velcar Free Commerce Zone through spreading favors among local Imperial officials which ensured his nomination. While unconcerned with political theory, Raquoran signed Ardus Kaine's Pentastar Treaty out of fear the New Republic would see the Velcar Sector as a prime target. Raquoran firmly believed Pentastar's military might should defend the Free Commerce Zone to the last, despite whatever Kaine may say.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GROT,An Admiral in the Imperial Army and was one of the key commanders at the Battle of Restuss. Grot, as a Zabrak, was one of the rare Non-Humans in the Imperial Army, an occurrence made even rarer by his high rank.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_GRUNGER,Josef Grunger was one of the original twelve Grand Admirals appointed by Emperor Palpatine at the New Years Fete Week Celebrations in 2BBY. TEXT_TOOLTIP_H_WING,The Long Range Strike Fighter/Bomber was a successor design to the famous Y-wing however the H-wing would never reach the same levels of fame and mass use of its predecessor. Slow speed, a three-man crew need, and a high cost prohibited any widespread adoption but the design’s heavy armour and armament meant it was seen as worthwhile for limited deployments where its flaws could be ignored.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_H60,A predecessor design to the famous B-wing of the Rebellion, the Tempest was a bomber designed in the waning years of the Republic. Its design contained promise but it was heavily flawed and unpopular due to its limitations and its strange design compared to the Galactic standard. Despite this unpopularity, Tempests would see some service during its life in less wealthy navies where something is better than nothing.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_HAND_OF_JUDGMENT,The Hand of Judgment is a squad of vigilante stormtroopers that defected from the Galactic Empire after Daric LaRone had killed their commanding officer due to their refusal of an order to massacre civilians. TEXT_TOOLTIP_HARRSK,Once a rising star in the Imperial Navy, Blitzer Harrsk suffered severe disfigurement during the Battle of Endor. Taking the titles of both Lord High Admiral and Supreme Warlord, Harrsk would recruit disaffected Imperials while engaging in near-constant fights against his regional rivals.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_HARSH,Combative Imperial who quelled an uprising on the planet Chabosh by leading a company of stormtroopers into battle, where his legs were severely injured. Rather than accept cybernetic replacements, Harsh willed himself to heal without surgery. His deeds made him a legend, and less than a year later the Emperor appointed him Moff of the Bosph sector. Harsh survived the upheaval following the Battle of Endor and conspired to bring the mineral-rich planet Otunia under Imperial control.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_IILLOR,Uwlla Iillor was an Imperial who persevered through the Empire's bigotry enough to earn a post as captain of the Black Asp, an Immobilizer 418 Cruiser. Her service record under the Empire marked her as a resourceful commander as well as able to think outside the box. Uwlla would engage in several skirmishes against the New Republic and Rogue Squadron in particular, but constant interference from her superiors would prompt Uwlla and her crew to defect. Uwlla and her ship, renamed Corusca Rainbow, would quickly earn the trust of their new allies and served the New Republic with distinction.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IL_RAZ,Ishin-Il-Raz was promoted by Emperor Palpatine to be one of the original twelve Grand Admirals in spite of having no military training and his appointment was met with much criticism. A fanatical supporter of the New Order and its core precepts, Il-Raz began to idolize Palpatine, and initiated a series of bloody massacres in an attempt to win favor with the Emperor.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IMMODET,An elderly General, Immodet supported Carnor Jax and his plot against Palpatine, and was rewarded with a spot on the Interim Ruling Council. By that point Immodet was the highest-ranking general left in the Imperial military, with his troops controlling several sectors as the first line of defense against the New Republic. Immodet, much like fellow council members Banjeer and Jeratai was highly xenophobic towards the non-human members of the council.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IMPELLOR,A massive fleet carrier designed by Fractalsponge that sits between the Secutor and carrier variant of the Praetor in tonnage, dedicating much of its immense volume to fighter capacity. Its onboard weaponry is surprisingly weak for a ship of its size as a result, but its vast reserve of fighters and bombers more than make up for it.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IMPELLOR_HULL,Hull: 21000 (Super Capital)
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IMPELLOR_MOVE,Speed: 2.45 | Accel: 0.1 | Turn: 0.1

TEXT_TOOLTIP_IMPERIAL_ACE,A highly adaptable and enigmatic pilot who prefers to only go by the name "Imperial Ace" and has an extensive career of flying craft from the TIE Advanced series of TIEs. The most remarkable engagement of Imperial Ace's career was the Battle of Mygeeto in 3 ABY, when the Rebel Alliance had established an outpost on the planet. After the battle, Piett remarked about Imperial Ace's skills and personally recommended him to Darth Vader, who believed the pilot to be force-sensitive and watched his progress closely.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_IMPERIAL_CARGO_SHIP,The end of the Clone Wars saw an explosion in demand for cargo ships and freighters, alongside a post-war surplus of previously mass-produced Acclamators that were no longer needed in peacetime. Thus the remaindered military Acclamators were widely converted into cargo ships to meet this new demand. Many hulls were damaged from their war time service and were repaired using salvaged elements from the numerous wrecks and decommissioned hulls of CIS Munificent star frigates - resulting in a strange hybrid appearance.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_IMPERIAL_GALACTIC_MARINE,Legacy infantry formation based upon clone assault marine units used by the GAR and incorporated into the Imperial Army with minimal differences. Significantly less common due to the prevalence of the Stormtrooper Corps for assault and hostile environment actions, along with the improved availability of EVO gear to regular army units. Often used in ceremonial duties because of this, though they retain the specialty of effectiveness against enemy infantry and fortifications.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_INTERCEPTOR1_FRIGATE,A class of assault vessel developed near the rise of the Empire baring similarity to the Action VI frigate. Lightly armed for its size, the Interceptor frigate makes up for its weapon deficiencies with strong hull plating and shields. It would become popular among pirates and criminal organizations like Black Sun during and after the Galactic Civil War.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_INVINCIBLE,The Invincible is an ancient cruiser design based on the Alsakan Atgeir-class in use for millennia before the Galactic Civil War. Despite its size, it was typically reserved for fighting smugglers and pirates, as well as deploying ground forces, but it's simple systems and sheer size often led to it being used as a training and auxiliary vessel.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_IRBIAN,Duke Erron Irbian was the Imperial ruler of the Cato Neimoidian city of Jorra. His rule is mostly distant, issuing edicts delivered from his palace to local officials, and he is content to ignore criminal elements so long as they render unto the Empire its dues. Most of Irbian's energy is spent watching for signs of sedition, and he maintains a network of informants and bounty hunters to ensure any suspected rebels are monitored and executed if he sees them as threats.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IRD,The Authority IRD starfighter was designed to serve in a variety of roles within the picket fleet, especially Interception, Reconnaissance, and Defense. It boasts powerful engines and dual medium lasers cannons as well as the best ejection seat of contemporary fighters; however, it lacks shielding and a hyperdrive.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IRDA,Though the IRD was a successful starfighter, its aerodynamics are poor enough that many pilots refuse to use it in-atmosphere. To rectify this, the IRD-A redesign carefully considered flight surfaces without compromising stellar performance. Among other improvements, the IRD-A also sports a concussion missile launcher in addition to its predecessor's lasers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_IRDA_SHIELDED,An uncommon modification of the IRD-A that incorporates a shield generator. Though it increases survivability, the tradeoffs to speed and handling are frowned on by most Picket Fleet commanders, as well as the increased cost by most investors.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_IVPIKKIS,Leader of the task force that led the Ssi-ruuvi Imperium's invasion of Bakura and a respected officer among his fellow Ssi-ruuk. A member of the red-scaled military caste, Ivpikkis is one of the few with any combat experience, serving in the military for roughly half a century starting as a scout. While not eager to take part in the Shreeftut's expansionism, he carried out his orders as expected of his caste. Ivpikkis would survive the Ssi-ruuvi fleet's rout at Bakura, and fought the Alliance fleet to a standstill during their counter-invasion.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JAALIB_BRANDL,The son of Inquisitor Adalric Brandl, Jaalib was raised by his father following his mother's death. As he grew older, Jaalib fell in love with the Rebel agent Fable Astin, and helped her escape rather than have her become a servant of the Empire. Adalric then gave Jaalib to Inquisitor Tremayne to be trained as a Dark Jedi. Eventually Jaalib rejoined his father, and seduced Fable into joining them.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JABBA,Jabba Desilijic Tiure, better known as Jabba the Hutt and often called "the Bloated One", though never to his face, was one of the most notorious Hutt crime lords in the galaxy, who governed a large criminal empire located in the Outer Rim Territories from his desert palace on Tatooine. At the height of his power, Jabba was one of the most powerful crime lords in the galaxy, even having contact with Prince Xizor, the head of the Black Sun Syndicate.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_JAG,Jai'galaar, known simply as "Jag," was a clone pilot who served under Plo Koon in the Clone Wars. An efficient pilot who was a cut above the regular clone pilots, Jag's most remarkable kill across his entire flight career was of his Jedi General during Order 66. As the Galactic Empire began phasing out clones from active duty, Jag received a backwater post on the fringes of the Empire where he served diligently, as with many of his brothers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JAGGED_FEL,Jagged "Jag" Fel was the third of Soontir Fel's six children, born on Nirauan in 7 ABY. He joined the Empire of the Hand's pilot academy in 18 ABY as the only non-Chiss in attendance. While at the academy, he led his fellow cadets in a successful defense against a pirate raid. By age 16, he had graduated and been given command of his own squadron.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JAKE_FARRELL,A no-nonsense fighter instructor with a focus on discipline from the days of the Old Republic, Jake Farrell's disgust with the Empire led him to join the Rebel Alliance. Farrell was an highly valued pilot and instructor to the Rebellion, helping design the A-wing alongside Adar Tallon, Ackbar, and Walex Blissex. At the Battle of Endor Farrell flew in Green Squadron, assisting in the assault within the Death Star II and distracting several TIEs from pursuing Lando Calrissian and Wedge Antilles.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JANAH_BOMBER,A heavy bomber developed by the Garwian Unity in response to several attacks from Lioaoin pirates which overwhelmed the Garwian's smaller patrol vessels and static space defenses. Designed to complement the Unity's naval weaknesses, the Janah is equipped with the speed and the firepower to batter the shields of hostile heavy cruisers and ensure Garwian naval superiority.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_JEDI,The Jedi are an order of monks who have served the Light side of the Force since before even the foundation of the Old Republic. While they have long claimed to be keepers of the peace, the Jedi have a long tradition of becoming warriors when challenged by the Dark Side of the Force or other evils that exist in the Galaxy. In battle Jedi use their symbolic and near mythical lightsaber as their primary weapon alongside manifesting all kinds of force abilities to support themselves and their allies or to attack their enemies.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_JEDSELK,The Imperial Governor of the planet Sellasas. Jedselk would see his planet fall under siege by the New Republic for several weeks but would manage to withstand the siege until an Imperial relief force arrived. This resulted in both Jedselk being promoted to the Governorship of the Dolla System and also saw the Governor acquire the services of a Srrors’tok called Tyionsis Cex as a bounty hunter under his patronage.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JEDSELK_AMARA,Amara Jedselk was the second in command of the garrison of Sellasas during the Siege of Sellasas as well as being the daughter of the Governor. She would sustain multiple wounds during the course of the siege as well as saving the life of Tyionsis Cex who would swear a life bond to her.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JENDON,Jendon was a highly respected test pilot within the Empire primarily involved with flying numerous experimental projects, including the TIE Defender program. Jendon was the first person to fly the finished TIE Defender, and for this feat would be assigned to Onyx Squadron alongside similarly remarkable pilot Yorr. Darth Vader would take notice of both Yorr and Jendon, and would assign them to pilot his personal shuttle. After the destruction of the Death Star II, Jendon would swiftly be reassigned to Onyx Squadron.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JERATAI,Jeratai is a brilliant but reckless Admiral and member of the Interim Ruling Council, who took pleasure in putting his command's lives in danger for his own gain. Despite this he secretly feared being put in danger himself. Jeratai was also a staunch xenophobe, and as members of the council were being assassinated he was quick to blame his non-human colleagues.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JEREC,Jerec was a former Master and archaeologist for the Jedi Order. After the Clone Wars, Jerec was found by the Inquisitorius and converted to the dark side where he would help hunt down his former allies. Loyal only to himself however, he plotted against the Emperor to which end he gathered a loyal group of Dark Jedi and backers. After the Battle of Endor, Jerec would briefly become a warlord before submitting fealty to the Pentastar Alignment only to gain access to their resources to fund his quest to locate the Valley of the Jedi and its untold power.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_JOCAOS,Malau Jocaos was raised on his homeworld of Ord Radama hearing tales of the corrupt Republic from his Separatist-supporting parents. This endeared Jocaos to the New Order and its strict ideals which made him enlist in the Imperial Academy, where he was fast-tracked for a post in Imperial Intelligence. Jocaus operated on Ord Radama where he maintained a number of spies, and hopes to take its governorship for himself one day. His demeanor is outwardly friendly and disarming, but underneath Jocaus is a cold-blooded killer.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JOHANS,The premier hovertank officer of the Empire, Zel Johans has a long and successful record, with the Rebel's pyrrhic victory at Turak IV the only defeat of his 'Hell's Hammers' unit. This valiant defense enhanced the prestige of Johans, and led to the creation of the Imperial-series repulsortank which was built to Johans specifications. After Endor, Johans based his regiment on Brintooin and supported various warlord groups. The confused infighting led him to reject oversight and become a de facto warlord, but he continued to lend his strength to groups he saw as legitimate.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_JONUS,Jonus was an experienced TIE Bomber pilot within Vader's Death Squadron. Originally a member of the bomber flight of Gamma Squadron, Jonus would destroy several Rebel blockade runners during his time there. By the Battle of Hoth, Jonus would be transferred to Scimitar Squadron and operated as Scimitar 2, flying top cover during bombing runs while Major Rhymer took the lead. Stationed on the second Death Star during the Battle of Endor, Jonus along with Rhymer only narrowly escaped the stations destruction. He would stick with Rhymer helping to rebuild the squad as the Empire fractured around them.
