TEXT_TOOLTIP_K_WING,A Y-wing successor and improvement upon earlier models, the K-wing MK2 is extremely well-armed and durable with wings covered in munitions plus a pair of turrets. Its rugged nature and built-in SLAM system allows it to endure against enemy fighters when performing runs against enemy capital ships.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_K_WING_PROTOTYPE,The successor design to the Y-wing developed by the New Republic, the K-wing sports a heavier payload compared to standard bombers. Its armament would be limited to a flechette launcher before subsequent models added on to its fighter to fighter capabilities while seeing improvements in its durability and speed. The implementation of the SLAM system also allows the K-wing to gain bursts of speed when performing runs or escaping from heavy fire.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_K222,The zZip K-222 Aero-Interceptor is a large and powerful airspeeder that is one of the fastest in its class. TEXT_TOOLTIP_KADANN,Kadann was a Jedi with precognitive talent who left the Order to study the Dark Side. He led the Prophets of the Dark Side, a fortune-telling cult that the Emperor pressed into his service. When they predicted the Rebel victory at Endor, they lost Palpatine's favor and went into hiding. Grand Moff Hissa's Prophets were imposters, a team of Imperial Intelligence agents who employed spies to inform their "prophecies" and field operatives to make them come true. Kadann was eventually killed by his former pupil Azrakel.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KADANN_FALSE,Rajah Ubooki was a Bimm con artist in Imperial Intelligence. In the months after the Battle of Endor, Cronal ordered him to pose as Supreme Prophet Kadann to establish the Church of the Darkside. Without the Force, Ubooki relied on spies and tricks to make his 'prophecies'. Shortly after Sate Pestage was deposed Ubooki went rogue, supporting Trioculus as Emperor before making his own bid for the Throne.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KAINE,A Sartinaynian native, Ardus Kaine was one of the earliest converts to the New Order. He oversaw acquiring military support for Palpatine and the founding of COMPNOR, which earned him Moff status. When Wilhuff Tarkin perished, Kaine was made Grand Moff of Oversector Outer, and took charge of Scourge Squadron and its Super Star Destroyer Reaper. Upon hearing of the Emperor's death, Kaine consolidated his territory with his corporate allies into the Pentastar Alignment of Powers.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KATEEL,Born a member of Kuat's high nobility, Kateel was mind-wiped by her sister Kodir and sent to work as a dancing girl for Jabba the Hutt under the name of Neelah. When Jabba died, she accompanied the bounty hunters Boba Fett and Dengar in their journey. 'Neelah' would rediscover her true identity on the way, and was captured by Kodir when she intercepted Fett's ship. Kateel would overpower her sister and sent her to stand trial on Kuat, and took Kuat of Kuat's place as chairman of KDY following his death. Years later Kateel refused an offer from the New Republic to have KDY surrender, but she was outvoted by a majority of stockholders.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KEEK,Chief of the Internal Security Police of the despotic New Regime of Brigia, Keek was a wicked Brigian who lead the swift ambush that attempted to halt the University of Rudrig's delivery of academic technology to his impoverished home planet. His mission would have succeeded, were it not for the intervention of a pair of freelance smugglers in a modified YT-1300. Treacherous by nature, he was nonetheless a poor liar when it came to dealing with offworlders.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KENDEL,Kendel was an Imperial Admiral of the Kuat Yards who ordered havod metal alloy welded to the frames of 100 refitted VSD-IIs during ongoing doonium shortages to the yards. While this was praised by Imperial Defense Daily for its resourcefulness, Kendel was also met with a wave of criticism from recruits and traditionalists who saw red hulls as garish and an affront to centuries of naval design.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KERMEN,Uther Kermen was placed in charge of the Empire's fleet by the Imperial Advisors after the Battle of Endor. He personally ran the defense of Milagro, and ended up bombarding most of the planet before it fell into New Republic hands to deprive them of its resources. Kermen would enter negotiations to secure the world of Kaal over Moff Prentioch and the New Republic. This would end in failure for Kermen, and the resulting battle saw his flagship damaged and in retreat.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KFLIM,Karrek Flim was a Viceprex based on Corellia during the Galactic Civil War where he carried out a number of suspect actions through Didina Lippinoss.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KHORAK,Khorak was an Imperial General who led an operation to purchase a holocube from a freelancer that contained the locations of Rebel bases. His subordinate Shad was sent to meet the seller at the gambling center of Kushal Vogh, but Shad was waylaid by Rebel agents posing as partygoers. Khorak led a squadron of stormtroopers into the area to intervene, only to find his men beaten up by the Rebels and the information destroyed when he arrived.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KIEZ,A promising Admiral, Kiez was granted the honour of captaining the first Imperial II Star Destroyer and rapidly promoted from then on to the command of the First Sector army with the Executor class Whelm as a flagship. Kiez was a cunning commander who was able to frequently ambush the Rebellion as they staged for operations.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KIMOGILA,A made-to-order update of an older design used during the Ithulian War, the Kimogila serves as a bomber for the fleets of the Hutts and a variety of mercenary and pirate groups. While lacking somewhat in flight performance, the rugged frame and respectable weapon loadout are considered to outweigh that downside when it comes to providing wanton slaughter on civilian targets or attack runs in fleet combat.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KLAR,Klar was a TIE pilot aboard the ISD Admonitor. Considered an excellent pilot by Captain Niriz, Klar helped carry out a ploy concocted by Admiral Thrawn to fool the Ebruchi pirates within the Unknown Region using a stripped down TIE. When the pirates captured Klar's TIE hoping to learn its capabilities, it fooled them into thinking the TIE Fighter was far weaker than it was. When Thrawn's trap was sprung, Klar escaped and Admonitor's TIEs easily wiped out the pirates.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KLEV,Klev was born on Alsakan to a long line of career officers and joined the Sub-Adult Group and Compforce at an early age, later attending the Academy to study Dedicated Siege Platforms. Klev attracted notice from his superiors by discovering a saboteur during the siege of Wann Tsir and was ultimately chosen to lead the World Devastator task force of the reborn Emperor.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KLICK,GC-1000 was one of the original Fett clones. He was given the name Klick by a Rodian Jedi Padawan during the Clone Wars in reference to his designator being the slang term for "kilometer." After the battle of Endor, Cronal approached many surviving Fett Clones by using the original codes of Emperor Palpatine to convince them to join his force. Klick later fought at Mindor during Cronal's attempt to restore the Empire under Luke Skywalker.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KLOFF,Imperial General in charge of forces on Goroth Prime alongside Admiral DeGoort. A lifer in the Imperial Army and an unyielding disciplinarian, Kloff's professionalism garnered the highest respect from the men under his command.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KOL_ATTAN,The Proctor of Defense before Tho Voota, Kol Attan was also the captain of the Beauty of Yevetha, a CR90 Corellian Corvette. The corvette itself was modified with a tractor beam and a hangar bay.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KOLLIS,Major General and Governor of Nishr, Kollis was a lenient governor but lost control of his troops who abused the native Nish leading to a planetary revolt. This rapidly escalated to a planet wide war as the Rebellion sent SpecForce troops to aid the natives. Despite a numerical advantage, Kollis was unable to crush the Rebels or the revolt and the war turned into a stalemate.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KOMRK,Kom'rk Skirata was a Null Class Advanced Recon Commando who started life known as Null-6. This change in name came due to close ties with his Mandalorian trainers such as Jaing Skirata. Kom’rk specialised in espionage and interrogation with unique talent at extracting information out of people with one such highlight being the discovery of General Grievous' location. He would desert the Republic during Order 66 in order to live with his adoptive Mandalorian Clan Skirata.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KOMRK_GUNSHIP,Coming from a Mandalorian word for Gauntlet, the Kom'r'k was used by the Mandalorians as both a fighter and transport. Despite being labelled as a fighter by its creators MandalMotors, the Kom'r'k was too large to actually be considered a fighter by the majority of people who instead classified it as an assault ship or gunship. A noticeable design feature was its wings which rotated to face upwards when landing to save horizontal space. The wings were also able to rotate 360 degrees around the main hull while the Gauntlet was in flight. While used by a variety of Mandalorian groups, it was their usage by the extremist group Death Watch which saw the most prominent usage during the Clone Wars.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KRANE,An Admiral of the Rebel Alliance, Krane was a tough officer who despised the Empire and gave little leeway to pilots under his command. Krane would train the Rebel pilot Rookie One in the usage of TIE Fighters in preparation for a mission to stop the production of TIE Phantoms, and escorted him partway on their journey. When Rookie One successfully destroyed the Imperial facility, Krane personally offered his congratulations to the pilot along with a medal.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KRAYT_DESTRUCT_DESCRIPTION,Forces a chain reaction within the main power core causing a tremendous explosion.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KRAYT_GUNSHIP,A MandalMotors starfighter commissioned by Jabba the Hutt as both a command ship and gift for his most valued lieutenants. Produced as the last of the "scaled killer" fighter series, the M22-T Krayt gunships origins as a speciality order craft shows in its customizable panels and space for two passengers. The Krayt carries four laser cannons, a gunner's turret that can be armed with laser or ion cannons, and a concussion missile launcher. This heavy firepower makes the Krayt an effective attack craft but too expensive to mass produce, limiting its presence to wealthy patrons.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KREFEY,The grandson of disgraced general Laryn Kre'fey, Traest Kre'fey utilizes unique tactics due to his unorthodox Bothan education.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KRENNEL,A sadistic Humanocentrist with grand ambitions. Krennel began his naval career under Grand Admiral Tigellinus, but his brutality so disturbed his superior that he was reassigned to Thrawn's Empire of the Hand. Krennel soon provoked Thrawn into severing his arm and sending him back to Coruscant. Fortunately, he returned during the upheavals following the Emperor's death – not only did he escape punishment; he was promoted to Admiral. By siding with Ysanne Isard against Sate Pestage, Krennel amassed enough force to make himself Prince-Admiral of his own warlord state, the Ciutric Hegemony.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KRIEG,Krieg was a Zabrak admiral based out of Kachirho who handled a number of tasks on Kashyyyk. He had many duties planetside and often acted through contracts to interested spacers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_KRIN,Angela Krin rose to prominence as a Lieutenant Commander in the Corporate Sector Authority fleet after enforcing the quarantine of Endregaad in 19 ABY. She was also responsible for halting the plans of Mika Anjiliac Chiera.

During the Thrawn Campaign, he helped to evacuate Atrivis Command when Thrawn assaulted Generis.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_KUUZTIN,Kuuztin was the captain of the Imperial-class Star Destroyer Courageous during the Galactic Civil War. In 3.5 ABY, Kuuztin was selected to join Grand Admiral Thrawn's task force during his campaign against the traitorous Grand Admiral Demetrius Zaarin. After leading a successful raid on a supply convoy bound for Zaarin's fleet, Kuuztin learned the location of one of Zaarin's supply depots and decided to attack it without Thrawn's authorization. Kuuztin hoped the ambitious attack would lead to a promotion, but his overconfidence led him into an ambush, and he was killed when his ship was destroyed.

Kyle would go on to discover his father's Jedi heritage and learn the ways of the Force, overcoming foes such as the Dark Jedi Jerec and Desann. TEXT_TOOLTIP_LIGHTNING_SQUADRON,Elite cavalry unit created during the Clone Wars for autonomous and dangerous missions, consisting of a mix of veteran clones, stormtroopers, scout troopers and army personal under the empire. Considered a floating unit that operates where needed, and one of the most notable Imperial special forces units not destroyed in the fallout from Endor.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_LIRA_WESSEX,Daughter of the famous engineer Walex Blissex, Lira forged her own path to fame with the design of the Venator class Star Destroyer. The success of this vessel allowed Lira to secure a politically advantageous marriage and secure support for a new project: The Imperial Class Star Destroyer, which would be used extensively by the Empire and far beyond its collapse. Not content with that, Lira would also go on to design the Executor Super Star Destroyer for the Empire.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_LIVETTE,Livette was the General in command of the Hapan Special Forces unit "Her Majesty’s Select Commandos" which served directly under the Queen Mother. Livette was highly unusual for a Hapan due to the lack of vanity she displayed as she let her hair grow openly grey and never covered up a burn scar on her cheek.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_LIVETTE_HEALTH,Health: 190 (Infantry)

TEXT_TOOLTIP_LOCAL_FIGHTER_BASE,An outpost of the planet's military equipped with a small garrison of locally sourced fighters. When influence levels are 7 or higher, they will fight for the defenders. At 5 or 6, they will be independent and fight both sides. At 4 or lower, they will ally with the attacker to rid themselves of their hated overlord.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_LOCKERBEE,Orrell Lockerbee was a lieutenant and IRD-A pilot in the Picket Fleet. A serious and disciplined man fond of saluting, he was stationed on Angela Krin's Resolute during the quarantine of Endregaad, where he met the Jedi Master Mander Zuma. Lockerbee attempted to stop Zuma's party from breaching quarantine, and brought them back after Zuma's party found Mika the Hutt.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_LON_DONELL,Lieutenant Donell is the former first officer of an Interdictor cruiser who assumed command and began a campaign of ship theft. His intentions are poorly understood: though based at the Imperial shipyard at Barpine, he has developed extensive underworld contacts to fence stolen ships and may ultimately be a free agent.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_LOOSE_CANNON,The Loose Cannon Pirates are a small outfit based in the Tamarin sector. Rather than simply waiting in ambush along likely routes, the Loose Cannons will lure likely marks to remote areas with promises of shady business opportunities.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_LORKA_GEDYC,Mandalorian Warrior of Clan Gedyc, Lorka Gedyc was a member of Death Watch who managed to climb the ranks to become its leader around the time the Galactic Empire was formed. Gedyc would bring the group out of hiding and directly allied with the Empire. Death Watch would proceed to assist the Empire in creating and maintaining an Imperial garrison on Mandalore in close proximity to the capital of Keldabe.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_LORRIR,A former lieutenant within the infamous 181st Imperial Fighter Wing, Lorrir defected to the Ciutric Hegemony with his wife Kandise some time after the battle of Brentaal IV, blaming Soontir Fel for betraying the Empire there over Admiral Lon Isoto. Lorrir was a mediocre pilot, and had this demonstrated during a training session with the disguised Wedge Antilles.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_LOTT,Human male born in 48 BBY on the planet Kwenn. Lott joined the Imperial Army where he showed a considerable aptitude for walker operations. Was promoted to the rank of General and placed in command of Walker Operations and Tactics at the training center on the planet Jardeen IV after serving a two-year tour under Veers. Joined Captain Iolan Gendarr in forming a warlord splinter faction.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_LOVETT,Hesha Lovett was the Captain of the Hapan Royal Vessel during the Yuuzhan Vong War.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_LUCREHULK,Wartime configuration Lucrehulk that retains the same large carrying capacity of fighters as the converted freighters. Hull modifications increase the durability and allow sufficient weaponry to defend itself and potentially engage multiple enemy vessels. A constant presence of fighter superiority both for its antifighter capabilities and complement.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_LUXURY_BARGE,A large repulsorlift designed as a luxury vessel by Ubrikkian Industries, often favored by wealthy Hutts. Utilizing huge, retractable sails capable of catching winds for leisurely trips, the standard sail barge is intended as a civilian product able to transport large amounts of guests and lacking armor or weapons. Modifications intended to withstand assault increase its armor giving it competent durability, and mounted portable heavy blasters and a powerful deck gun allows it to ward off incursions on the barge's owner.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MAELSTROM,An impressive battlecruiser, the Maelstrom is the largest proto-Star Destroyer developed by KDY for the Republic Navy at the outset of the Clone Wars. The Maelstrom is optimized for fleet command duties, and serves just as well as a fleet carrier and planetary siege platform with its powerful defenses and weaponry. Despite these capabilities, the Maelstroms proved unpopular due to the vast amount of resources required to maintain them, and were phased out soon after introduction in favor of more innovative classes.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MAKATI,Afsheen Makati was one of the original twelve Grand Admirals. During a mission, Makati developed a deep hatred for Supreme Prophet of the Dark Side Kadann, due to being tortured with Force Lightning by the Prophet after his first officer ridiculed the dress of the Order's members. At the Battle of Endor, the Grand Admiral escaped the destruction of the second Death Star and retreated to Coruscant, swearing his allegiance to whoever controlled Imperial Center.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MAL,Open-market artillery platform produced by Merr-Sonn Munitions, popular amongst local and criminal groups and the occasional larger force. Has a complex folding turret able to handle multiple types of missile weaponry, along with shields and decent mobility. While dated, the platform saw a surge in popularity for criminal fighting during the Galactic Civil War.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MALCOM,The Moff and governor of the Brema sector near Sullust, Malcom struggled and failed to suppress the Pinacism religion which was spreading in his sector. TEXT_TOOLTIP_MALCOR_BRASHIN,Brashin is a man of humble origins who became a fanatic Imperial when his wife and daughter were murdered by non-human thieves. He prefers to command ground forces from orbit, concerning himself with strategic matters rather than personal heroics. He relays orders to his troops remotely via the Inquisitor's Battlefield Holographic Control Interface and a data-linked TR-MB.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MANDATOR3,The third in the Mandator series of Super Star Destroyers alongside its sister class the Bellator, the Mandator Dreadnought would upscale the previous II design to create a massive hulking weapon platform in a complete mirror opposite of the Bellator design philosophy. The sheer scale of the Mandator design allowed it to bristle with weaponry and armour while it also enabled it to carry a significant contingent of fighter and bomber craft on board to supplement its own weapons and probably more importantly help compensate for the noticeable lack of speed of the Mandator-III.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MANKVIM,A swarm-based fighter with barely noticeable shields developed by the Techno Union, the design is most recognized for its impressive speed and saw proliferation into the Corporate Sector Authority and civilian markets after the Clone Wars. It is best used to intercept enemy bombers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MANOS,Manos was the Devaronian leader of a major Imperial labor union, and was a strong proponent of equality between humans and non-humans. Manos pledged his support to Carnor Jax and his plot against Palpatine; for his support, he was given a spot on the Interim Ruling Council. He, along with all the other non-humans on the council, would be removed from the council and arrested by Xandel Carivus following Jax's death.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MARA_JADE,Once known as an Emperor's Hand, Mara Jade has renounced the Empire and is now a Jedi under the tutelage of Luke Skywalker. She remains deadly in combat, and her abilities have only grown in the time since the Emperor's death.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MARAUDER,Originally designed by Sienar as a patrol and escort gunship for the Judicial Forces, the design was declined for purchase by the Republic government proper. Sienar instead ended up selling the hulls to other groups, including the Corporate Sector Authority and various independent groups, where the ships often ended up in pirate hands. It is well suited for supporting other vessels against capital ships.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MARAUDER_MISSILE,A refit of the Marauder-class corvette made by the Rebel Alliance after purchasing stolen Corporate Sector Marauders from smugglers. The base Marauder's laser cannons were removed and its engines downsized to accommodate concussion missile pods, in hopes to make an equivalent to the Empire's Broadside Cruiser. The result allows this Marauder variant to provide devastating missile barrages to vulnerable ships at the expense of speed and maneuverability.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MARCROSS,A native of the planet Shelkonwa, Saberan Marcross was born into a heavily Imperial family, his uncle of which being the Governor of his home sector. A staunch believer in his duty to the Empire, he followed every order unconditionally and without question. During the Raid on Teardrop after the Battle of Yavin, Marcross had followed orders to kill civilians under the impression they were Rebel supporters. Days later, LaRone had killed the ISB Major who issued the order, and Marcross chose his friends over his duty and fled with them.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MAS_2XB,The MAS-2xB Self Propelled Turbolaser was a siege platform designed by the Loronar Corporation and used by the New Republic. The massive vehicle dwarfed even AT-ATs, was extremely slow, and maintenance intensive however in return it brought to the battlefield an extremely potent turbolaser. Some estimates placed the weapon as being on par with the turbolasers of an ISD in terms of firepower, although it had a terrible recharge rate, which allowed the MAS to crack any defence or even target capital ships in the atmosphere. Smaller turbolasers and lasers protected the platform from any threats attempting to get close.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MASSA,A Duros admiral who was given command of the Fourth Fleet after Endor, with Chel Dorat as second in command. Massa won a series of victories across the Corellian Run, including the decisive Battle of Denon where the admiral perished.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MASTON,Cass Maston staunchly believed in the New Order which led him to volunteer for ISB service. He received many commendations in his Surveillance Branch posting within the Genesia System, and was transferred to the Brak Sector where he became the top Investigations agent in the region. This proved to be less successful than his previous assignment, but he diligently follows up any leads he acquires. His position allowed him to investigate anything at will or even call on local military assistance.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MC80_LIBERTY,An Alliance-era Mon Calamari starship retrofitted for action as a moderately armed ship of the line. The Liberty-type was originally designed for civilian use or to be converted into warships despite being built to civilian specifications. It is well protected due to Mon Calamari shield technologies and a large amount of redundant emitters, but it is lacking in firepower compared to similarly sized vessels. It is best used as a cheap or low-priority line cruiser until other options are available.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MEGADOR,Before being appropriated by Pellaeon's forces, Megador and its sister-ship, Dominion, were part of Blitzer Harrsk's Zero Command forces, although he lacked the personnel to crew them. TEXT_TOOLTIP_MELVAR,Former Imperial Intelligence Destab member with fanatic patriotism for the Empire, Melvar was reassigned to Warlord Zsinj where he was promoted to the rank of General. Acting as the Warlord's second-in-command, Melvar would spearhead Zsinj's ground and starfighter forces and also was the architect of the Raptor program. Extremely sadistic, Melvar had his fingernails replaced with cuticle implants to provide him with an immediate torture device.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MENNDO,Menndo started off as a bounty hunter, A starting career path of many male Rodians, but would rapidly transition into becoming an assassin along with doing espionage jobs because he found them to be far more lucrative for much less effort. The Empire was a frequent employer of Menndo, who was often called upon to kill troublesome high ranking Imperial officials.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MERES_ULCANE,Charismatic Imperial officer who bypassed academy requirements from his uncles influence. The ambitious Ulcane rose from field promotions as those around him died suspiciously, and immersed himself in the study of alien cultures. Ulcane fled from a mutiny of his troops following the Empire's defeat at Endor and joined Ennix Devian's Kaarenth Dissension, who tasked Ulcane with stirring dissent among nonhumans against the New Republic.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MIKA,Mika was the younger son of Popara who lived a hidden and frustrated life of a Hutt who could touch the Force but with no one to train him. He rejected his father's focus on legitimate business and created a modified strain of Glitterstim and an underground network to distribute it. With this new "Tempest" spice Mika was able to ensnare a Jedi and enthusiastically began learning the ways of the Force and the lightsaber.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MIKTISS,Miktiss was a Saurton warrior forced off his homeworld for illegal partisan actions. Miktiss fled to the Kathol sector, where he stole a small fleet and founded the Des'maric Pirates. He partnered with crime lord Crev Bombaasa, though Bombaasa plotted to supplant Miktiss with his trusted Saurton lieutenant Ginzet.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MILLENNIUM_FALSEHOOD,A modified YT-1300 light freighter commissioned by General Han Solo to serve as a false version of his iconic Millennium Falcon during the Zsinj campaign. Acquired from a Corellian scrapyard and furnished by Wraith Squadron, the Millennium Falsehood while not equal to the Falcon's capabilities it did serve its purpose admirably. Taking part in several stealth missions designed to end explosively, Zsinj was tricked into thinking it was the real Falcon. At the end of the campaign Han Solo thought to donate the ship to a museum, so he could stop requests to donate the actual Falcon.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MIRAX,Mirax was the daughter of the notorious Corellian smuggler Booster Terrik and a childhood friend of Wedge Antilles. After her father was arrested by Hal Horn and sentenced to hard labor in Kessel, the young Mirax took charge of Terrik's extensive operation and turned it into a profitable and almost entirely legitimate business. Her friendship with Wedge led to her aiding Rogue Squadron on several occasions. There she met Corran Horn and, much to her father's chagrin, began a relationship with the son of the man who had arrested him.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MISSILE_BOAT,An advanced Cygnus starfighter deployed to counter the rogue Grand Admiral Zaarin's TIE Defenders. They are well defended, agile, fast, and equipped with an astonishingly large ordnance payload, though only armed with a single laser cannon.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MIST,Developed in parallel to the Magnetar Battlecarrier, the Mist’s offensive capability is nearly entirely reliant on its onboard strike group as its internal weapon systems are lacklustre. This lack of offensive capability meant the Mist was never a popular design for the Hapan Royal Navy and its officers often see posting to a Mist carrier as punishment or as a sign of disfavour from their superiors. Regardless, its strong shields and large hangar bays make the Mist a capable dedicated carrier meaning it still sees noticeable usage.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MORNINGSTAR_A,Superiority fighter configuration of a tri-winged picket fighter originally produced by Kuat Systems Engineering for planetary defence, but more popular among mercenary and criminal groups for its varied capabilities. TEXT_TOOLTIP_MORNINGSTAR_B,Dogfighter configuration of a tri-winged picket fighter originally produced by Kuat Systems Engineering for planetary defence, but more popular among mercenary and criminal groups for its varied capabilities. The Morningstar-B model packs additional laser cannons to deal with other fighter craft, but lacks munitions for use against other target types.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MORNINGSTAR_C,Bomber configuration of a tri-winged picket fighter originally produced by Kuat Systems Engineering for planetary defence, but more popular among mercenary and criminal groups for its varied capabilities. The Morningstar-C model has a series of munitions modules intended for attacking enemy capital ships but is not as effective engaging other fighters as the A and B models.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MORUTH_DOOLE,Moruth Doole was a Rybet who began his career as a minor bureaucrat on Kessel. He slowly built up a network of influence and wealth that led to Doole becoming kingpin of a spice smuggling ring, but lost an eye to Jabba the Hutt's thugs for interfering with Han Solo's Kessel run. After Jabba died, Doole led a prisoner revolt against Lord Overseer Twane, and became overlord of Kessel. He would spend years after investing in a large mercenary fleet and stockpiling spice to protect Kessel's independence.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MOVE,Move
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MOVE_01,Orders the selected units to move to the targeted location, ignoring all enemies along the route.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MOXIN_TARK,Son of Imperial lieutenant Boes Tark, Moxin Tark was drilled at a young age in martial matters in hopes they would serve the Empire. The young Tark was restless though, and at age 18 fled his family estate to take up a life of bounty hunting. Quickly making a name for himself, Tark became a highly demanded bounty hunter. Wearing armor based off the iconic Sun Guards of Thyrsus, whom Tark idolized, he is a lethal combatant armed with a variety of weapons. He would work jobs for the Rebel Alliance numerous times until Tark's murder of Captain Narn Detan earned the new governments ire, though Tark's familiarity with their procedures allowed him to escape capture.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MULLINORE,A native of Taanab, Mullinore was a classmate of Natasi Daala at the Carida Academy and was one of the few to not look down on her. Daala repaid Mullinore by offering him a command under her at the Maw Installation, which he eagerly accepted. Mullinore was assigned as captain of the Star Destroyer Basilisk, sharing a distant but mutual friendship with Daala during their time at the Maw. His loyalty to Daala extended so far Mullinore offered to engage in a suicide run into the surface of Coruscant for her before the arrival of the Sun Crusher interrupted their plans.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_MUNIFICENT,Designed by the Intergalactic Banking Clan as a mobile communications, transport and debt-enforcement vessel. Its modular and skeletal design causes it to be fragile but easy to produce, traits which led to it becoming the backbone of the CIS fleet in the Clone Wars, and assorted usage afterwards. Has a significant amount of firepower but does not hold up when taking damage.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_MYRSK,A Nikto veteran of the Clone Wars who served with the Separatists, Myrsk is known for his disgruntled nature and dislike of nearly everybody. He would join the Espos and rise to the rank of Sergeant, working under Lieutenant Fasser. During Imperial agent Jahan Cross' investigation of Rossum Droidworks, Myrsk would lead the hunt for Cross after the latter was framed for murder.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NEOMEN,Captain Neomen was a competent officer stationed in the Sisar Run under the command of Governor Newen Streeg. He quietly despised the position and his superior and sought art on the black market to amuse himself.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_NEREUS,Wilek Nereus was angling for the governorship of the resort world of Gyosha when a clerical error stationed him on the edge of the galaxy. Though he wondered if some offense was truly the cause, Nereus made do in his remote post; purging the local government, forming a network of supporters, and writing a manifesto on the Rebel sentiment in the Outer Rim with which he hoped to regain the public eye. When news of the Emperor's death came on the heels of an invasion by the Ssi-Ruuvi, the ambitious governor carefully weighed his options to ensure his position within the new reality of the Empire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NEUTRON,Hapan culture was traditionally heavily isolationist and for much of its history the Hapan people had been left alone by the wider galaxy. However this changed with the rise of the Empire which made frequent incursions into the Cluster with significant force. This new threat led the Hapan Royal Navy to accept a new larger ship design which moved away from standard Hapan doctrine of massed firepower to a ship more capable of brawling with a peer opponent in equal battle. While not successful enough to shift Hapan doctrine wholesale the Neutron was still a valuable addition to the Navy, capable of drawing enemy fire away from the massed cruisers and small ships.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NEUTRONSTAR_TENDER,A variant of the Neutron Star bulk cruiser intended to serve as an armed cargo vessel rather than second line military duty. For that purpose it drops several of the weapons from the base design to free up more space for internal cargo. When in military use these variants would transport supplies to fleets in areas where there were risks of pirates or enemy raids.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NICLARA,An Alderaanian Imperial Navy member who served on the Death Star just before Yavin. She remained loyal when her home world was destroyed by the station but was transferred to the ISD Pulsar as Chief Gunnery Officer prior to the Battle of Yavin. Following the Pulsar’s surrender she would escape to the Outer Rim Territories where she would also reform scattered Imperials into a roving fleet targeting New Republic facilities.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NIL_SPAAR,Nil Spaar was the leader of the Yevethan rebellion that freed the Duskhan League from the Empire. After gaining command of the Black Fleet, Nil Spaar began growing the Yevethan forces for an assault on the galaxy.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NIMBANESE_SPY,Since becoming associates of the Hutts, Nimbanels were found throughout the galaxy as bureaucrats and business administrators for legitimate businesses, governments, and Hutt criminal interests.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_NIMBUS_COMMANDO,Nimbus Commandos are specialized elite infantry from Jabiim, originally drawn from nationalist leader Alto Stratus' Bronze Nimbus command and elements of the Jabiimi Army who joined him. They are practiced soldiers of Jabiim's high-precipitation environment, with their repulsorlift skates allowing any Nimbus to effortlessly move across swampy terrain in ways normal infantry cannot. Nimbus units are armed with blaster rifles capable of long to mid-range fire, and cram their suits with enough explosives that they can take on even large walkers. The power of the Nimbus would persist on Jabiim in the time of the Empire by collaborating with the new government, with some members going off to serve as freelance mercenaries.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NOGHRI_ASSASSIN,Among the foremost assassins in the galaxy, the Noghri Death Commandos are a terrifying sight to all they set their eyes on. Darth Vader would earn the fanatic loyalty of these warriors with the false promise to heal their homeworld of Honoghr, and Grand Admiral Thrawn would gain their loyalty as well before this deception was revealed. Operating as a special forces unit within the Imperial Army, Death Commandos were famously used in assassinations of the Empire's targets as well as the rescue of captured personnel. Equipped with deadly blasters to match their formidable stealth skills, the Death Commandos would more than earn their fearsome reputation.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NOGHRI_BODYGUARD,Members of the Noghri people who served as the guardians of Leia Organa Solo and her family following the reveal of her lineage as Darth Vader's daughter. The Noghri Bodyguards continued to maintain and hone their formidable skills in combat and stealth to keep a watchful eye on the "Mal'ary'ush", or 'daughter of the savior'. Beyond protecting the Organa Solo household, they would be tasked by Leia with missions of importance to the New Republic such as scouting or providing escort services to important officials.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NORYM_KIM,A Myke pirate involved with the Interim Ruling Council, Norym Kim used his wealth to help Carnor Jax sabotage the ambitions of Emperor Palpatine. After Burr Nolyd's death he was the next successor, however he knew that made him a natural target and so passed the title to Xandel Carivus, a placid figurehead. This plan backfired when Carivus expelled the non-human council members and he was later captured by the New Republic.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NOVACRUISER_REFIT,As the Hapans interacted more with the wider galaxy they gained access to new technology which allowed them to cover up weaknesses in their technology base. The Nova Cruiser Refit took advantage of new technologies to add in Mon Calamari style shielding, and potentially even Mon Calamari shields themselves, and larger more capable power generators. This increase in power allowed for the calibre of turbolasers to be increased but forced a reduction of hangar space which now allowed only a single squadron.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NSSIS,The Chiss were the first to design clawcraft-style starfighters, but the Nssis-class and its siblings represent a fusion of Imperial and Chiss ideas, much like the Empire of the Hand itself. Compared to the TIE Interceptor, the Nssis is slower but more agile and tougher.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_NSSIS_GRAY,After joining the Empire of the Hand, Soontir Fel continued to drill and train the pilots under his command to exacting standards. In 8 ABY, Fel and his Gray Squadron played a pivotal role in executing Thrawn's plan to defeat the warlord Nuso Esva on Quethold. Before the battle, Fel boasted that "Impossible is Gray Squadron's specialty."

TEXT_TOOLTIP_NYAMA,Nyama was Stromma Council Liaison to the Empire of the Hand's top echelons, speaking for his leaders and offering useful local knowledge in occasions such as Nuso Esva's final defense on Quesoth. Nyama possessed the characteristic belligerence of his species, though this was often mediated by more conciliatory aides.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_NYROX,Extremely skilled vehicle operator that drew the attention of General Veers and became his prodigy, and contributed to the officer handbook for AT-AT use. Enjoys the sense of power the large vehicle provides over other options and is well-skilled in keeping it operational in heavy combat.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_NYROX_ATAT_HEALTH,Health: 800 [2%/5s] (Heavy Vehicle)
TEXT_TOOLTIP_OBERHAUR,The C.O. of Storm Squadron, Jhon Oberhaur was a friendly but tough leader who was frank over the high expectations demanded of its pilots. While the squadron served aboard the ISD Accuser, Oberhaur and several members would station themselves on the Tatooine city of Bestine during the Galactic Civil War, keeping pressure on pirates while accepting promising recruits as applicants.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_OBEROS,A notorious bounty hunter, Dalan Oberos has been active since the Clone Wars, where he completed contracts from Count Dooku and fought against Jedi. Under the Empire, Oberos cemented his reputation by bringing in Jedi survivors and Force-sensitives to the new regime. Even when such commissions dried up, Oberos was eager to test his mettle, and took up a job on Cato Neimoidia hunting owners of a Jedi holocron, but supposedly perished there. Oberos later resurfaced as an enforcer for the Hutt Cartels, flying a Kimogila fighter instead of his old StarViper MK2. Rumors were abound that he had came back disfigured or had illegal cybernetics, while others claim this was not the man who fell on Cato Neimoidia at all.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ODOSK,Odosk was nearing retirement when he took a position at the Maw Installation, but he, nevertheless, served as commander of Daala's ground forces and one of her chief advisors when she returned to the galaxy.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ODUMIN,The Tynnan Odumin was a CSA agent who once saved the life of High Inquisitor Laddinare Torbin and was subsequently promoted to Territorial Administrator. He continued his undercover work, concentrating on dissolving slaver organizations such as the Xiochi Gang, once teaming up with Han Solo. After publishing his memoirs, he became a hero on his homeworld.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_OKINS,An elderly admiral well known for his loyalty, Okins was often placed in charge of ambitious and unscrupulous officers whose doings he would report in great detail to the Emperor. His most famous mission was leading the Executor and supporting ships against a Rebel shipyard in the Vergesso asteroids.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_OTRO,An executive of Galentro Heavy Works, Wyrn Otro was raised on a Galentro family enclave in the Core like most elite members of the corporation. Otro would serve as Galentro's representative at Grand Moff Kaine's Pentastar Talks, and despite his misgivings that it was a power play by Kaine, helped ratify the Pentastar Alignment Treaty. While a member of the Alignment's Chamber of Order along with other corporates, he prioritizes Galentro over anything else, viewing the corporation as the only family he needed.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_OTTO,A renegade Imperial General, Otto earned his rank from his political connections and friendship with Admiral Motti. Repeated defeats and rumors of spice addiction saw Otto reassigned to Lok where Motti hoped his friend would keep a low profile. Out of wanderlust Otto would abandon his post, taking an AT-ST walker to participate in Jabba the Hutt's demolition games in hopes to gain the money needed to retire to Cloud City. His obese and bumbling nature is matched only by his insane zealotry to the Emperor, granting him startling capability in combat.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_OVERRACER_BIKE,The Mobquet Swoops and Speeders Overracer was a minimalistic and cheap speeder bike capable of high speeds and of optionally carrying a small blaster cannon. This made it popular with both speeder racers and military scouts for organisations without a large budget. The Overracer would see service both for PDF forces operating on a budget and later with the Rebellion.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_OWEN,An extremely vain and short tempered Moff; Owen was the governor of the Rayter Sector in the Galactic Civil War. Owen would lose his position due to the scheming and treachery of his personal assistant. Relegated to the command of his ISD, Impending Doom, Owen would go on a rampage of revenge and executions to regain his honour and position in the Empire.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PAIGE_TARKIN,Shayla Paige-Tarkin was the Senator of the Seswenna Sector during Clone Wars and an ardent supporter and occasional confidant of Emperor Palpatine. TEXT_TOOLTIP_PALPATINE,Palpatine was a Senator from Naboo who was elected Supreme Chancellor of the Republic. The instability of the Clone Wars allowed him to accrue tremendous executive power, to the point he declared himself Emperor by its end. Behind his genial public persona Palpatine is the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, and he commands a terrifying mastery of the Dark Side.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_PALPATINE_MOVE,Speed: 1.1
TEXT_TOOLTIP_PALPATINE_REBORN,Emperor Palpatine had conquered death through the Dark Side long ago, yet was unprepared for his demise at Endor. His spirit was adrift before his consciousness returned to a clone on Byss, and the ordeal forced the Emperor to enter a period of convalescence. He was not idle, as this time was used to prepare his legions and superweapons, while growing even stronger in the Dark Side through his studies. Now fully recovered, Palpatine aims to subjugate the galaxy once more, and usher in an empire of darkness across the stars.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PANAKA,Panaka began his career with the Royal Naboo security forces, eventually becoming head of security for the Monarchs. He later joined the Imperial military, where he served with distinction in a mapping expedition that turned into a defense against the Rozzums where he met and recruited Bertroff Hissa. Panaka was Moff of his home Chommell Sector by the Battle of Endor and remained a staunch Imperial loyalist in the face of a growing New Republic.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PARALLAX_ARTILLERY,The Parallax Artillery System is an adaptation of the militarised version of the Water Dragon transport where the internal space and external weapon systems were stripped out to provide munitions storage and targeting computers for the massive torpedo launcher system installed on top of the vehicle. Because it is a conversion and not a purpose-built artillery design, the Parallax has to deploy stabilisers before firing in order to stay upright against the recoil forces.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PARNELL,Sebastian Parnell was an Imperial Army General and the Governor of the Sil’Lume System under the Empire. The Governor-General was in personal charge of protecting the asteroid mining which occurred in the system and the prisons on the third planet. Parnell was extremely brutal in his tactics and was happy to spill as much blood as needed to achieve his goals, and probably a bit more blood than that just to make sure. 
TEXT_TOOLTIP_PARNET,An Imperial General stationed on High Inquisitor Tremayne's flagship, the Interrogator. Baring a face that looked as handsome as it did cruel, Parnet lived in terror of Tremayne, a common sentiment amongst the Interrogator's crew. When Captain Vharing boasted of his ability to persist in his post, Parnet warned him there was only one penalty for failure to the High Inquisitor; the longest fall a man could take, death. His words would be proven true when Vharing was unceremoniously executed by Tremayne shortly thereafter.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PATROL_MIYTIL,Developed with the help of Separatist engineers fleeing the Clone Wars, the Miy'til was an extremely cheap and miniaturised fighter that was the predominant fighter of the Hapes Consortium during the Galactic Civil war and after and served in several variants. The Patrol configuration of the Miy'til is a cheaper and weaker version designed to patrol and protect low priority sectors thus freeing up more capable variants for fleet duty.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PESTAGE,One of the earliest supporters of Palpatine and Grand Vizier of the Empire, Sate Pestage's power in the New Order allowed him to give or deny access to the Emperor at will on top of managing his personal archives. Pestage also increasingly took part in the ruling of the Empire as Palpatine withdrew from the concerns of the state for arcane matters. With the Emperor's death, Pestage took the role of ruler of the Empire for a time. He would attempt to raise Imperial citizens morale through acts such as establishing the Church of the Dark Side as a state religion. Conflicts with both Isard and the Imperial Advisors would see Pestage deposed however, with mixed reports of him being assassinated on his homeworld of Ciutric, to simply retiring to the sanctuary world of Byss.

It first saw use in the neutralization of the Ssi-Ruuvi Imperium.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PIRATE_FIGHTER,The Rihkxyrk assault fighter is a strong, if sluggish fighter. Developed by TransGalMeg, it is commonly seen among pirate groups such as Black Sun, to the point people associate the term 'pirate fighter' with it exclusively. The heavy-duty shielding and dense armor makes the Rihkxyrk able to withstand attacks that leaves most fighters scrap, with accompanying firepower to give back as good as it gets. Most Rihkxyrk pilots develop a high level of confidence in these fighters after wading through heavy fire and coming out undamaged.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PITTA,Danetta Pitta was a Near-Human who served in the Imperial Navy and was one of the original twelve Grand Admirals. A rabid xenophobe, Pitta was one of the fiercest champions of Human High Culture in the Galactic Empire. He carried out multiple genocidal campaigns in the Outer Rim Territories, depopulating non-Human worlds. After Endor, Pitta took command of a Torpedo Sphere and became the unofficial protector of the Corellian sector after bribing the local diktat.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PROWLER_1000,Created by Arakyd Industries, the small, repulsor-driven seeker droid excelled in surveillance. Designed specifically for work in a military capacity, the Arakyd Prowler 1000 featured a frontal photoreceptor array equipped with various detection systems and scanners. A tasking antennae protruded from atop the drone's domed head, allowing clone commanders to send orders directly to their seeker groups.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_PRYL,Tanda Pryl came from an influential family, assuring her a spot within the naval academy. She proved capable and had a meteoric rise to captain of the ISD Thunderflare assigned to the Elrood Sector. Pryl would develop a fascination with Force sensitives from reading of Luke Skywalker, developing a goal to find those gifted in the Force to either turn them to the dark side or eradicate them. Before the Battle of Endor her ship was assigned to Darth Vader's Death Squadron, but in the wake of the fleets break-up she returned to Elrood. Pryl had a fierce rivalry with captain Akal Zed, who she considered a buffoon.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_PRYL_SPECIAL,Special: Obsidian Squadron
TEXT_TOOLTIP_PTER_THANAS,Pter Thanas was a Coruscanti Imperial Navy commander who defected to the Rebel Alliance after the Bakura Incident during the Galactic Civil War. Thanas was noted as a diplomatic officer of the Imperial Starfleet, known for his ability to rule successfully without using fear as a motivator.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_PULSAR_CRUISER,The isolation of the Hapan cluster has resulted in their divergent tech from galactic standard. The Pulsar is the culmination in the Hapan advances in the field of Ion weaponry as it sports a massive ion cannon which can cripple ships much larger than itself. However the Pulsar is overly specialised as it lacks normal weapon systems, shielding, and armour as its hull is dedicated nearly entirely to the main ion weapons leaving the Pulsar as a support ship which needs protection at all times.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_PX7,The PX-7 Troop Transport, simply known as the troop carrier by boots on the ground, is designed for quick and efficient deployment of Imperial troops. Its unassuming appearance belies the fact the troop carrier can move a massive number of army personnel on land, giving it an edge over transport by AT-AT or ferry shuttle. The PX-7 is minimally armed, decked with only a single anti-vehicle gun and decent armor plating. This makes the troop carrier not suited for sustained assaults on its own, but when loaded with infantry it can smash and grab critical locations with efficacy.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_QUA,Chancellor Qua of the Zenox Star Cluster was a man comfortable with the upper echelons of society, able to hobnob with the likes of Thalassa Tarkin or the Most High Proctor of Thebeon 8. Though there were those who considered him an Imperial lackey, his territory was deep in strongholds of the Empire and he personally benefited handsomely from the arrangement.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_QUAFFUG,Quaffug was a Hutt who lived an extravagant life funded by a Ceramo-Werx complex until he lost it to Lando Calrissian is a sabacc match. Quaffug rebuilt his enterprises by exploiting the Jokhalli of Blimph's third moon, but never forgave Calrissian and attempted to execute the gambler during a Rebel negotiation. Calrissian gained the upper hand and saved Quaffug's life from the Jokhalli in exchange for a position in the Hutt Guardsmens' Guild.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_QUICK_REF,Planet Quick Reference
TEXT_TOOLTIP_QUILLER,Compatriot of Daric LaRone, Joak Quiller was one of the five members of the Hand of Judgment vigilante group. During their service in the Empire, after Daric LaRone had killed their superior officer, Quiller knew he would be considered complicit in his superior's death. When LaRone had defected, Quiller joined, and would become a valued member of their new group.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_QUINCE,'Big Quince' was a Sludir slave lord raiding and selling in the Outer Rim, brother to the infamous shadowport operator Omze. He rose to fame in the gladiator pits of Loovira and carried his old force pike as a reminder of his origins and a weapon against troublesome slaves and buyers. Quintik prided himself in his business, arranging his deals in person and willing to offer every courtesy and bribe to officials and powerful customers.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_R22_SPEARHEAD,Inspired by the Aurek, the Tammuz-An designed the Spearhead as a hyperspace capable escort fighter. The Spearhead would prove to be a reliable and long serving hyperspace escort fighter for the Galactic Republic and then later widely used by the Rebellion whose pilots liked to heavily tinker with its design. This modability would see the Spearhead serve not only as a fighter craft but also as a Rebellion experimental test bed. The Spearhead would later receive a wide ranging upgrade program under the oversight of Dodonna and Blissex which would develop into the famous A-wing Interceptor.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_R41_STARCHASER,The R-41 was Hoersch-Kessel's belated answer to the ubiquitous Incom Z-95. The Starchaser was a reliable and quick fighter with lasers, ion cannons, and a missile launcher that provide excellent firepower. But it was also relatively fragile and clumsy, and arrived too late to interrupt the Z-95's sweeping popularity and not long before the Clone Wars flooded the market with new ideas and designs. Still, the Starchaser can be capable enough in the right hands, and it enjoys a certain popularity in fringe markets that appreciate the low cost and ease of maintenance.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_R42_STARCHASER,The R-42 was a modest improvement to the original Starchaser that added a gunner position to reduce the workload for the pilot lacking an astromech droid. Further refinements of the interior space allowed additional missiles for its already formidable arsenal, though in most respects it is identical to the original.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RADDUS,Raddus was from the polar regions of Mon Calamari, and proved himself as a gifted military leader as tensions rose between the Empire and his world. He was quick to join the Rebel Alliance and soon became one of its most important commanders.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAGAB,Mon Calamari officer who served in the New Republic Navy as part of the Second Fleet under Hiram Drayson.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAGEZ_DASTA,The Baron Ragez D'Asta was an ardent supporter of Emperor Palpatine and a member of the Ruling Council. His vast merchant fleets and security apparatus of the D'Astan Sector were vital to the Empire in the chaos after Endor, and he continued to be closely involved even as his health decayed. The Baron highly valued honor in men such as Gilad Pellaeon and had little respect when it was lacking.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAIDER,A fast, effective anti-fighter corvette with a heavy weapons load. It was designed by Lira Wessex to be a KDY-produced escort smaller than a Lancer and to assist hyperdrive-equipped smallcraft on missions. It is best used to protect larger ships from enemy bombers in a mobile role.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAIDER_HULL,Hull: 650 (Corvette)

Trades out most of the anti-fighter weaponry and physical munitions for a mix of turbolasers and ion cannon mounts. It is best used for support fire against larger vessels in numbers or as a wolfpack.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAMIER,An ardent supporter of the New Order, Lesan Ramier was made Moff of the Brak Sector as a reward for his service. Fearing a Rebel takeover of the sector, he agreed to a joint Imperial-Lant Mining Corporation takeover of the Aramand Cluster within it. A stern, hardened man who is dedicated to the Emperor, Ramier also realized he is not well-versed in military tactics and relied on the advice of his subordinates in the military for tactical planning.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RANA_APC,The Rana APC is the military version of a long-running police transporter ubiquitous across the entire Hapes consortium. The APC version gains additional weaponry over its police counterpart while maintaining the same large internal space for troop storage and the internal medical station for rapid trauma response should attached infantry get injured in the field.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_RANCOR,Rancors were large carnivorous reptomammals native to the planet of Dathomir. They were usually born brown, but in special circumstances, such as the mutant rancor, jungle rancor, and the bull rancor, their color may have differed. Although found on other worlds such as Lehon—where they were brought by crashed starships—Ottethan, Carida, Corulag, and Felucia, those from Dathomir were said to be stronger and more intelligent than others.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_RANULF_TROMMER,Son of an Imperial Admiral, Ranulf Trommer was an experienced TIE pilot. He would gain acclaim for the Battle of Aguarl 3 which saw him destroy a Rebel base with a single bomber. Ranulf would be sent by Grand Moff Hauser to spy on Governor Grigor of M'haeli, but while there he fell in love with Mora, a woman who supported the rebels. While Ranulf at first hoped the Empire's evils were only caused by corrupt men like Grigor, he would come to see the Empire itself as unjust during his time on M'haeli. Ranulf would escape with Mora when Hauser attempted to execute her, declaring his intention to join the Rebel Alliance to the eager acceptance of Leia Organa.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAPTOR_GUNSHIP,With the end of Clone Wars came an influx of CIS scientists and designers seeking refuge in the Hapes Consortium, Hapan designers took the chance to review galactic standard designs to seek inspiration for their own. The Corellian Gozanti Cruiser was one such vessel they would be inspired by. While the aesthetics of the Gozanti was not to Hapan tastes, its capabilities would prove useful in supplementing and later replacing the older Stinger gunship in border patrols and small scale anti piracy operations. Henceforth the sleek Raptor Gunship was designed to recreate the specifications of the Gozanti in the beautiful artistic styles of Hapes Consortium ship designs. This elegant style however sacrificed some of the durability of the Gozanti but made it noticeably faster.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAPTOR_GUNSHIP_GROUP,Group: 15x Raptor Gunship

TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAPTOR_TROOPER,While many Imperials attempted to field faux-stormtroopers in the fallout of Endor to flaunt their legitimacy, Warlord Zsinj sought a new symbol for his authority. Formed under his subordinate Melvar, the black-uniformed Raptors are special forces infantry devoted to Zsinj as stormtroopers were to the Emperor. Raptors were originally trained for infiltration, but many would act as secret police for their master on conquered worlds. Trained in the art of terror, Raptor Troopers are skilled with blasters and demolitions, making them fierce opponents to any that opposed Zsinj.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAVEEN,Raveen was a Commander in the Imperial Navy and the Second in Command to Grand Admiral Zaarin. He worked alongside Zaarin in the TIE Avenger and Defender projects. After Zaarin's death, he remained at large with the remaining forces of the coup attempt.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RAZOR,Veteran Clone Trooper and one of the original members of Lightning Squadron. Operates a heavier walker in his older age compared to his choice during the Clone Wars.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_REAPER,The third Executor-class produced by the Empire, Reaper was the flagship of Scourge Squadron, and was gifted to Ardus Kaine following Wilhuff Tarkin's death. Reaper under Kaine would hunt Rebels in the Outer Rim up to the Battle of Endor, which led to Kaine seceding from the Empire with Oversector Outer. Reaper served as the host for the Pentastar Talks that brought local corporate leaders to join Kaine's Pentastar Alignment of Powers. Years after Kaine's death during Operation Shadow Hand, Gilad Pellaeon would command Reaper during the Orinda Campaign.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_REDD_WESSEL,The son of the ex-Grand Moff Marcellin Wessel, Redd Wessel, had always aspired to become a fleet commander. When he attended the academy, however, the tests revealed he had an aptitude for ground tactics. After steadily climbing his way to the rank of General, he stagnated in his career. Carnor Jax broke that stagnation by offering Redd to join the Interim Ruling Council; for his part, Redd Wessel was promoted to Supreme Commander.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_REDER,Dor Reder was an Imperial Navy officer hailing from Pirralor. He was a commander aboard the ISD Pulsar at the battle of Endor but would evade capture along with Niclara Varnillian using a stolen Rebel transport. Reder would join the warlord Gaen Drommel and was promoted to Captain of the Star Destroyer Krieger. Reder used the information gained from the Rebel transport to learn the locations of many New Republic bases and safe worlds, attacking them in a deadly but quick campaign.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_REDLINE,A TIE pilot in Darth Vader's Black Eight Squadron, Redline flew alongside Juno Eclipse during the bombardment of Callos, which desecrated the planet. Eclipse considered him the cruelest being she had ever flown alongside and outright psychopathic, with Redline arguing in favor of staying to 'finish the job' even as she ordered a retreat from Callos. Redline would take her place as Black Eight's leader when Eclipse was reassigned. In the battlefield Redline engages in combat with the eagerness of an adrenaline junkie.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_RESUUN,Governor of the Tamarin Sector, Lobax Resuun was a dauntless leader. As his sector erupted into chaos following Endor, Resuun refused to give in to the plummeting morale of his forces and the rising paramilitaries of organized crime gangs. At the peak of the tension in the sector, he would relocate the sector capital to his flagship, and he managed to retain control of the sector in spite of a coup attempt.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_REXLER_BRATH,An ace pilot with a storied history of combat missions in the Galactic Empire, Rexler Brath was a terror from the cockpit of a TIE Interceptor. TEXT_TOOLTIP_RHYMER,Callsign Scimitar 1, Rhymer was the leader of Scimitar Squadron's TIE Bombers. Reaching the rank of major by the Battle of Endor, Rhymer's group was tasked with the defense of the Death Star II's shield generator from ground assault. When the superweapon was destroyed, Rhymer and his wingmate, Jonus, barely escaped the explosion. The collapse of Imperial authority left Rhymer and Jonus bouncing between garrisons around the Anoat sector while rebuilding the squadron's ranks, before reluctantly joining with Trioculus and the Central Committee of Grand Moffs forces at their secret base on Hoth.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RIBOGA,Riboga established himself as the crime lord in the Cularin system several decades before the outbreak of the Clone Wars, supervising a number of unsavory practices including a slaving ring and a spice mining operation on Kemix he built a huge warship to guard. He left due to the alleged unprofitability of the remote system, though in truth he was the victim of an embezzling majordomo who took over the operation and abolished the slave trade.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RIGA,Riga Lanchenzoor was the youngest person to become chief manager for the Bank of Aargau, and later came into the employ of the Corporate Sector Authority's Financial Division. Thanks to her shrewdness and ambition, Lanchenzoor was made the Executive Viceprex of that division, a position that she held for the next twenty years. She constantly pushed to bring CSA currency off the Imperial credit standard, but met resistance due to fears of Imperial retaliation.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RIIZOLO,Ambitious but cowardly member of Leona Tavira's Invid pirate gang with a penchant for going after civilian star liners.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RIIZOLO_SQUADRON,Fighters: 2 / 1
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RINGNECK_RECON_DROID,The Ringneck recon droid, also known as the Separatist probe droid, was a model of probe droid manufactured by Arakyd Industries. It was used by the Confederacy of Independent Systems during the Clone Wars. It came in varying sizes, one of the most feared variants which protected the Separatist stronghold on Lola Sayu.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_RNEEKII_PIRATES,The Rneekii pirates operated in the Outer Rim, including a number of operations to acquire exotic starfighters to sell to the highest bidder.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ROGAR_GARRET,The ranking officer of Vilonis Sector, General Garret was a methodical commander who spent much thought on why his enemies did what they did and how he could channel their actions to his advantage.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_ROGRISS,A skilled officer known for his loyalty and honor. Rogriss was leader of the Empire's taskforce against Zsinj and collaborated with Han Solo and Wedge Antilles. Years later he was given orders to attack the planet of Adumar in spite of Rogriss' promise to leave it unharmed, leading him to defect.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCIMITAR,Designed by Sienar under Tomax Bren's proposal for a TIE Bomber Mark 2, the Scimitar assault bomber has improved durability, shielding and faster speed compared to the TIE Bomber at the cost of a leaner payload. It was kept in production outside off the book test models before the return of Grand Admiral Thrawn, who demanded a new generation bomber from his reading of Bren's work. Sienar then began official production on the Scimitar, with the bomber becoming a valuable asset to Thrawn's military that saw use in the Empire for decades to come.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCIMITAR_WHITE_DEATH,Members of the Scimitar assault wing "White Death" under the lead of Tomax Bren. "White Death" pilots are highly disciplined even on dangerous missions, yet they are constantly striving to surpass their already outstanding results. Combined with their adherence to the New Order this has allowed the Scimitar wing to operate without severe scrutiny from their superiors.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCORPENEK,The Annihilator Droid is the apex of Colicoid engineering, a formidable platform built on the success of the Droideka to destroy enemy tanks and troop transports that might threaten their homeworld. Though they lack the rolling mobility of their smaller cousins, Annihilators pack powerful shield generators and versatile cannons that can engage heavy armor or fire in lower power modes to defeat infantry. Too expensive to see truly mass use and seldom deployed off of Colla, the rarely seen "Scorpeneks" have become the subject of fearsome legends.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCREED,A hero of the Clone Wars and commander of the first Victory Fleet in the Galactic Republic. After the death of Palpatine, Screed carved out his own Warlord kingdom and allied himself with Warlord Zsinj.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCURRG_H6,Heavy bomber created by the Nubian Design Collective who hoped to sell the design to Outer Rim Planetary Defense Forces. Very well armed and capable, but with a large price tag that reduced popularity among legal interests. Such interests resulted in the common addition of the topside turret compared to the original design.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCURRG_H6_PROTOTYPE,Heavy bomber created by the Nubian Design Collective who hoped to sell the design to Outer Rim Planetary Defense Forces. Very well armed and capable, but with a large price tag that reduced popularity among legal interests. This closer-to-stock model lacks the extra turret popularized by secondhand production of the craft.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCYK_FIGHTER,Named after a vicious Tatooine reptile, the M3-A Scyk is the first in a series of custom starfighters commissioned by the Hutt Cartels from MandalMotors. It is a lightly armored interceptor that functions well for base defense and capital escort duties. The loadout of the fighter is customizable but generally limited to a single laser cannon, making it best used in short-range strikes.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SCYK_HEAVY_FIGHTER,The M3-A Scyk was a basic fighter produced by MandalMotors for semi-legitimate organizations such as the Zann Consortium or the Hutt Cartels. The Scyk was just dangerous enough to be worthwhile without drawing the attention of MandalMotors Imperial supervisors, with its cheap price and variable weapon mounts making it popular on secondary markets even after production ceased. Heavier variants of the Scyk feature marginally improved durability and slugthrower cannons paired with a concussion missile launcher.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SD_10,Designed by Umak Leth, the SD-10 was a member of the SD series battle droids from Balmorran Arms. The 10 was a massive upgrade on the 9 line of droids with personnel shields, self healing and laser reflecting armour, and several powerful weapon systems. It also possessed the tactics used by the SD-9 in its memory banks. Despite their strength however they would prove no match for Shadow Droids deployed by the Empire during the Balmorran Rebellion.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SD_6,Large, infantry-scale battle droid produced in the final days of the Galactic Republic as an installation defense unit and fire support platform. A solid design that builds on several earlier generations and effective enough to see Republic service throughout the Clone Wars despite political outcry. The repeating blasters and Ion weaponry make it suited to engaging infantry, light vehicles and other battle droids.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SD_9,To all appearances a giant metal human, the SD-9 Battle Droid by Balmorran Arms was a powerful droid capable of wiping out infantry with its repeating blaster and plasma cannon while being protected with its shields and reflective armour. The SD-9 would be used by both the New Republic and Empire by the time of the resurrected Emperor's return.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SD_9_ASSAULT,Common mercenary conversion of the standard SD-9 War Droid into a heavier assault unit. Replacing the saturation repeating blaster with an integrated EWEB and higher quality armor plating provide an individual capabilities boost, especially at close range.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SECUTOR,A massive carrier design that shares features with its Venator and Maelstrom predecessors. It is fast for its size, well-armed, and carries several times the fighter complement of most other capitals. It was designed late in the Clone Wars and saw continued use after the conflict by the Empire and its splinter factions. It is best used as a flagship for fleet groups seeking additional fighter superiority and firepower.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SEDRISS,Born Qaga Lok and a direct descendant of the famed Ulic Qel-Droma, Sedriss QL worked as a mercenary until the Inquisitorius brought him before Palpatine. Convinced to swear loyalty to the Emperor and train as a Dark Side Adept, he took on the Sith name of Sedriss to honor his new master. When the Emperor died at Endor, Sedriss journeyed into the Deep Core to await the return of his master on Byss. As a reward for his loyalty, the reborn Emperor named Sedriss his Executor and leader of the Dark Side Elite.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SEDRISS_HEALTH,Health: 190 [2%/5s] (Hero)

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHADOW,Shadow is a member of the elite Empire of the Hand stormtrooper squad, Aurek-Seven. He participated in the liberation of Kariek and the Battle of the Redoubt.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHADOW_DROID_HEAVY,A variant of the standard Shadow droid which added more winglets to the frame of the fighter. This increased the amount of missiles and torpedoes able to be carried on the fighter as well as increasing the amount of guns the cyborg is able to bring to bear.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHADOW_DROID_LIGHT,Created by the Revived Palpatine and Umak Leth on the hidden throne world of Byss, Shadow Droids are more cyborg than droid with the extracted brains of injured Imperial pilots hardwired directly with the systems of the fighter and then imbued with the dark side of the force. Extremely fast and agile paired with potent weaponry; Shadow droids are lethal opponents for any pilot and can even threaten capital ships.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHADOW_EVO_TROOPER,Rare cross-combination of Shadow Stormtrooper and EVO Trooper concepts. Equipped with extremely expensive armour that incorporates a variety of support functions, they are impractical for mass deployment. Used for heavy suppression duties and decapitation strikes, they are extremely effective against lightly armoured targets.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHARGAEL,An ambitious Imperial Moff of a small, unimportant Outer Rim sector of the galaxy. When the Empire fractured after the battle of Endor, Bliss Shargael bided his time until after even the minor warlords of the Deep Core had collapsed, attempting to seize glory and power for himself by declaring himself a warlord. This was short lived as his sector was absorbed by the New Republic during the Death Seed Plague of 13 ABY.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHEA_HUBLIN,Shea Hublin was a distinguished fighter pilot who joined the Republic navy at sixteen. Participating in the Western Reaches Operation, Hublin famously brought down the shields of a Separatist holdout, and was named air marshal of the Greater Seswenna. When he was forced into line command he earned the sobriquet 'the Rebel Destroyer' for hunting rebels and pirates near Eriadu. Hublin later reluctantly sided with Sander Delvardus when the Empire fragmented.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHEA_HUBLIN_SPECIAL,Special: Sword Squadron

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHELOV,A tenth-generation native to Abregado-Rae and avid gambler, Shelov is a sadistic Herglic with a distinctive black circle tattoo around his blowhole. Leading the Tendui Tribunal - the local acting government - Shelov believes only he knows what is right for Abregado-Rae, and has promised to eliminate the planet's notorious criminal rings. In actuality, he has turned the planet into a police state. The Tendui Tribunal severely limits personal freedoms, and actively attempts to cut off food from political dissidents, ironically necessitating the use of smugglers by locals.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHIELD_GENERATOR_HEALTH,Health: 800 (Structure)
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHIELDED_ARMORED_INTERCEPTOR,Fighters combining the shielded and armored TIE Interceptor modifications into the same craft. Exist within the infinite variety of smallcraft employed by corporate or warlord forces, especially on craft renowned for modularity.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHIELDED_TIE_DROID,Rare variant of the TIE/D automated fighter equipped with shielding at the expense of some performance. Less favored over the standard model due to the extended materials needed to develop it along with droid casualties being seen as of little import. Production and usage would be limited to important command roles during Operation Shadow Hand as a result.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHIELDED_TIE_FIGHTER,A TIE Fighter variant modified with a shield generator. The additional weight creates a small but noticeable decrease to the craft’s speed and handling.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHIELDED_TIE_INTERCEPTOR,A TIE Interceptor variant equipped with a shield generator. Though popular among many pilots, the addition adds cost and is detrimental to the highly tuned performance of the engines, making these fighters somewhat scarce.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHIELDED_TIE_RAPTOR,An experimental variant of the TIE Raptor that gets rid of Warlord Zsinj's only qualm with the original design, the lack of shielding. While this Raptor variant loses some out in speed and maneuverability as a result, it still retains the deadly lasers and concussion missiles of the unshielded version.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SHIELDS,Shields

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SILANUS,The Silanus air speeder is the atmospheric conversion of the Hetrinar line of space superiority craft. Extremely fast and nimble groups of Silanus air speeders are capable of rapidly reacting to any armoured threats and saturating their chosen target with weapons fire. The Silanus relied on shielding to protect it from any return fire long enough to either destroy the threat or use its speed to escape from the AA weapons range.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SKANDREI,A notorious gang of pirates who terrorized the Kathol Outback, rumored to be based in the Marcol Void. The Skandrei fleet is usually dispersed, but will regroup to attack tougher targets such as the planets uniting against their menace.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SKETTO,Skettos were medium-sized, blood-sucking flying reptiles that dwelled in the caverns of Tatooine. Only emerging at daybreak and twilight, they possessed many rods in their eyes to assist their vision in low-light conditions as they ate airborne insects. Though reptilian, they were warm-blooded and gave birth to a single offspring at a time.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SKIPRAY,A heavy gunboat sometimes counted as a capital ship in small navies, the Imperial GAT-12h and its successors are a more combat-oriented improvement over the Republic GAT-12g model which lacked the concussion missiles and had a tractor beam for utility. It is best used to attack enemy capital ships but can be turned onto fighters in a pinch due to its turreted lasers.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SLOSIN,A Human from Entuur, he accepted his native Entuuran culture, giving a high value to pain and suffering. He was famous for programming his tendencies into the IT-O Interrogator droids, although he personally did not use droids for interrogation.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SNUNB,A former security chief who became one of the first officers of the Sullustan Home Guard. When Sullustan leadership voted to secede from the Empire, Snunb was selected as the representative to lead the Rebel Command Fleet to Sullust. The Rebels found Snunb to be a conscientious leader, and as a show of trust granted him command of the escort frigate Antares Six. From it Snunb would help rescue stranded New Republic crew on Coruscant during the Imperial Mutiny, and later was promoted to command of the Star Destroyer Resolve in the Fifth Fleet.

TEXT_TOOLTIP_SOLARIS,Alinda Solaris was the ranking Naval Intelligence officer of the Vilonis Sector. She kept a very close eye on the goings on in her jurisdiction and beyond and was always able to provide useful commentary to her superiors.
TEXT_TOOLTIP_SOMOS,An Imperial Stormtrooper commander infamous for his ruthless tactics and penchant for firing squads even by the standards of his peers. He also headed the Stormtrooper training facility on Morvogodine, which often sent Stormtroopers on "peacekeeping" missions as final tests, and much like its commander was notorious for being vicious. Owing to his status, Somos lives and dies for the Empire, and also held command over both regular Stormtroopers and Heavy Stormtroopers.
